verses.hebrew_full: בִּשְׁנַ֣ת שָׁל֔וֹשׁ לְמַלְכ֖וּת יְהוֹיָקִ֣ים מֶֽלֶךְ־יְהוּדָ֑ה בָּ֣א נְבוּכַדְנֶאצַּ֧ר מֶֽלֶךְ־בָּבֶ֛ל יְרוּשָׁלִַ֖ם וַיָּ֥צַר עָלֶֽיהָ
verses.translation: In the third year of the reign of King Jehoiakim of Judah, King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon came to Jerusalem and laid siege to it.
verses.rashi: In the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim Now is it possible to say so? Did he [Nebuchadnezzar] not reign in the fourth year of Jehoiakim, as is stated (Jer. 25:1) “in the fourth year of Jehoiakim son of Josiah, king of Judah that is the first year of Nebuchadnezzar?” So what is the meaning of “In the third year?” In the third year of his rebellion, as is stated (II Kings 24:1): “and Jehoiakim was his vassal for three years; then he turned and rebelled against him”; and in the third year, he overcame him, and that was the eighth year of Nebuchadnezzar, for the master said (Seder Olam ch. 24): In his first year he conquered Nineveh, and in his second year he advanced and vanquished Jehoiakim, who served him for three years and rebelled against him for three years. That was the eleventh year of the reign of Jehoiakim: five years before he vanquished him, three years that he served him, and three years that he rebelled against him. And then Jehoiakim died under his hand, and in his stead, Nebuchadnezzar enthroned Jehoiachin, his son.
verses.ibn_ezra: The discourse of Abraham son of Rabbi Meir Ibn Ezra haSefardi: This is the book of the "man greatly beloved" [Daniel 9:23, Daniel 10:11, Daniel 10:19]. Weighty matters are discussed in it, and prophecies past and future. Everything is presented concisely in riddles, and secrets [revealed] through angels on high, resting upon foundations. But its commentators have not grasped its secret (lit. "stood in its secret"), and each one explained according to his ability (lit. "as his hand reached"). The feet of all of them stumble regarding the end of "a time, times [and half a time]" [cf. Daniel 12:7]. Also, the [identity of the fourth] beast became confused for them, for they identified the fourth [beast/kingdom of Daniel 7] as the third. And now I will begin to explain according to the precision of the language. [Daniel 1:1] Bi-shnat (בִּשְׁנַת - In the... year): We find it written that Jehoiakim reigned for eleven years [II Kings 23:36; II Chronicles 36:5]. If so, what is the meaning of “in the third year of his reign”?! Now, concerning ["these are the sons of Josiah," I Chronicles 3:15] it is written that he left four sons: the firstborn Johanan, the second Jehoiakim, the third Zedekiah, the fourth Shallum. And we find that when Josiah died, his son reigned. Some of our Sages, of blessed memory, said that this Jehoahaz [who reigned first, II Kings 23:31] is Johanan. But behold, he reigned only three months [II Kings 23:31]. Then his brother Jehoiakim reigned after him, and he [Jehoiakim] was twenty-five years old [II Kings 23:36]. Now, he [Jehoahaz, who was 23, II Kings 23:31] was younger than Jehoiakim. So how could Scripture call [Johanan, identified by the Sages with Jehoahaz] the firstborn?! Therefore, they said [it means] "firstborn for kingship" [i.e., the first to be chosen to reign]. And [based on this line of reasoning] they were forced to say that Shallum is Zedekiah, because Jeremiah's prophecy concerning Shallum [Jeremiah 22:11-12] is adjacent to his prophecy concerning Zedekiah [Jeremiah 23:5-6]. Furthermore, [they argued this] because no one reigned over Israel after Zedekiah, and for Scripture to list a fourth [son, Shallum] merely as a name without substance [i.e., without significance] would be a strange way [of writing]. Now, understand that [contrary to those Sages] Jehoahaz is Shallum. And Johanan did not reign. And do not be astonished that Shallum is called by two names, for behold, Solomon is Qohelet [Ecclesiastes 1:1], and the sons of Samuel [were listed as] Vashni and Abijah [I Chronicles 6:13], yet elsewhere it is written that [the firstborn] was Joel [I Samuel 8:2]; and ʿAmminadav is Izhar [cf. I Chronicles 6:7, I Chronicles 6:23, based on certain lineage interpretations]; and Uriel is Zephaniah [cf. I Chronicles 6:9, I Chronicles 15:5, I Chronicles 15:11]; and many others like these. And in the Torah, Zerah is Zohar [Genesis 46:10 vs. Numbers 26:13 / Exodus 6:15 - different spellings for Simeon's son]. And the proof th...

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּתֵּן֩ אֲדֹנָ֨י בְּיָד֜וֹ אֶת־יְהוֹיָקִ֣ים מֶֽלֶךְ־יְהוּדָ֗ה וּמִקְצָת֙ כְּלֵ֣י בֵית־הָֽאֱלֹהִ֔ים וַיְבִיאֵ֥ם אֶֽרֶץ־שִׁנְעָ֖ר בֵּ֣ית אֱלֹהָ֑יו וְאֶת־הַכֵּלִ֣ים הֵבִ֔יא בֵּ֖ית אוֹצַ֥ר אֱלֹהָֽיו
verses.translation: The Sovereign delivered King Jehoiakim of Judah into his power, together with some of the vessels of the House of God, and he brought them to the land of Shinar to the house of his god; he deposited the vessels in the treasury of his god.
verses.rashi: and some of the vessels of the House of God he took, and some of them remained, as is stated (in Jeremiah 27:19): “so has the Lord of Hosts said concerning the pillars, concerning the sea, concerning the bases, and concerning the rest of the vessels, which Nebuchadnezzar did not take into exile (sic).” and he brought them to the land of Shinar, to the house of his god to praise his idols; he brought the entire captivity there, even the people, and the vessels he brought afterwards to the treasury.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:2] Va-yiten (וַיִּתֵּן - And [the Lord] delivered): It is written: “and some of the vessels of the House of God.” [It says "some"] because it was during the exile of Jehoiachin that the precious vessels (k'lei chemdat - כְּלֵי חֶמְדַּת, cf. II Chronicles 36:10) of the House of the Lord were brought to Babylon. For thus it is written [regarding Jehoiachin's exile]: “and the treasures of the House of the Lord, and the treasures of the king's house” [II Kings 24:13].

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Jehoiakim = "Jehovah raises up"
sign of the definite direct object·article

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ לְאַשְׁפְּנַ֖ז רַ֣ב סָרִיסָ֑יו לְהָבִ֞יא מִבְּנֵ֧י יִשְׂרָאֵ֛ל וּמִזֶּ֥רַע הַמְּלוּכָ֖ה וּמִן־הַֽפַּרְתְּמִֽים
verses.translation: Then the king ordered Ashpenaz, his chief officer, to bring some Israelites of royal descent and of the nobility—
verses.rashi: the nobles Heb. הַפַּרְתְּמִים, dukes.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:3] Va-yomer ha-melech (וַיֹּאמֶר הַמֶּלֶךְ - Then the king ordered): This Ashpenaz was a saris (סָרִיס) [official/eunuch – Ibn Ezra will discuss the term below], and he was the appointed officer over all the sarisim (סָרִיסִים) [officials/eunuchs] who served [the king] and his wives. And it is written [in Isaiah, quoting the prophecy given to Hezekiah concerning his descendants]: “and they shall be sarisim [officials/eunuchs] in the palace of the king of Babylon” [Isaiah 39:7]. [Daniel 1:3] Rav (רַב - Chief): [Means] great, as in “the city of a great king” [Psalms 48:3]. Therefore, Yefet [ben Ali] said that they [those brought under this order] were Daniel and his companions, and his proof is that it says “of royal descent.” But he did not speak correctly. For the verse [Daniel 1:6] states: “And among these were, of the children of Judah...” [implying Daniel's group was specifically from Judah], and others [chosen were] “of royal descent” [and nobility, as per v. 3]. [Yefet implies] that they [Daniel and companions] were sarisim [in the sense of eunuchs]. But you will not find [any indication] that Daniel was a saris [eunuch]. Rather, it is written about him and his companions: “youths in whom there was no blemish” [Daniel 1:4]. And there is no blemish more severe than [being made] a saris [eunuch]! Furthermore, the king did not command [Ashpenaz] to teach Daniel and his companions the “script” (which is the writing of the Chaldeans) and their language merely [for any service], but specifically “that they might stand before the king” [Daniel 1:5], and not [primarily] before his wives [a role often assigned to eunuchs]. And if an objector argues: If your words are true that they were not sarisim [eunuchs], why did the king appoint the Chief of the Sarisim over them? The answer: Because the king commanded to seek from Israel “youths... handsome in appearance” [Daniel 1:4]. And wicked men, even today, are known for homosexual practices. Furthermore, if a youth is castrated (yesoras - יְסֹרַס), his intellect diminishes, and he [Nebuchadnezzar] was seeking for those standing before him to be knowledgeable in all wisdom, as [the text later] explains [Daniel 1:4]. And Rabbi Yehoshua said: The word sarisim (סָרִיסִים) in the Holy Tongue [Hebrew] has two meanings: The first is castrated ones [eunuchs, cf. Esther 1:10]. The second is ministers/officials/servants [cf. II Kings 24:15]. Thus, the Chief of the Sarisim was the chief over the king's ministers/servants. Like [an example using the word is]: “...with the sarisim and the mighty men (gibborim - גִּבֹּרִים)” [II Kings 24:14 - listing groups exiled]. And there is no need [to think based on this listing that] the sarisim were necessarily officers over the mighty men, simply because they were servants of King David [or perhaps, servants of the king, like David's officials were]. He [Nebuchadnezzar] did not castrate them [Daniel and companions]; rather, he took them thus in captivity. [While ...

words.eng: Ashpenaz = "I will make prominent the sprinkled"

verses.hebrew_full: יְלָדִ֣ים אֲשֶׁ֣ר אֵֽין־בָּהֶ֣ם כׇּל־מאֿוּם֩ וְטוֹבֵ֨י מַרְאֶ֜ה וּמַשְׂכִּלִ֣ים בְּכׇל־חׇכְמָ֗ה וְיֹ֤דְעֵי דַ֙עַת֙ וּמְבִינֵ֣י מַדָּ֔ע וַאֲשֶׁר֙ כֹּ֣חַ בָּהֶ֔ם לַעֲמֹ֖ד בְּהֵיכַ֣ל הַמֶּ֑לֶךְ וּֽלְלַמְּדָ֥ם סֵ֖פֶר וּלְשׁ֥וֹן כַּשְׂדִּֽים
verses.translation: youths without blemish, handsome, proficient in all wisdom, knowledgeable and intelligent, and capable of serving in the royal palace—and teach them the writings and the language of the Chaldeans.
verses.rashi: and who have strength Our Sages explained (Sanh. 93b) that they should restrain themselves from laughing, speaking and sleeping, out of fear of the throne, and resist the urge when they feel the need to relieve themselves. and to teach them the script This refers back to “who have strength.”
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:4] Yeladim (יְלָדִים - Youths): [The verse specifies youths without blemish] unlike the blind and the lame, who are not fit to stand before a king. Therefore, it is written: “The blind and the lame shall not come into the house” [cf. II Samuel 5:8]. And the meaning [of "house" there] is: the royal house of David. [Daniel 1:4] Ve-tovei mar’eh (וְטוֹבֵי מַרְאֶה - And handsome of appearance): That they should not be sickly pale (lit. greenish) and appear like sick people. [Daniel 1:4] U-maskilim be-chol-chochmah (וּמַשְׂכִּלִים בְּכָל־חָכְמָה - And proficient in all wisdom): A general term for all [types of wisdom]. [Daniel 1:4] Ve-yodʿei daʿat (וְיֹדְעֵי דַעַת - And knowledgeable): That they should know from the beginning of a speaker's statement what he intended to say at the end. For there are many wise men who do not grasp this until the speaker finishes. [Daniel 1:4] U-mevinei maddaʿ (וּמְבִינֵי מַדָּע - And intelligent; lit. understanding science/knowledge): That they would be able to make others understand what is in their [own] heart and knowledge. As in [the verse]: “Do not curse the king, even in your thought (maddaʿ - מַדָּע)” [Ecclesiastes 10:20]. For there are wise men who struggle to express in language what is in their heart, except in a roundabout way and through crooked paths (be-orachot ʿaqalqalot - בְּאָרְחוֹת עֲקַלְקַלּוֹת). [Daniel 1:4] Va-’asher koach bahem (וַאֲשֶׁר כֹּחַ בָּהֶם - And who had ability/strength in them): That their physical constitution (toldatam - תּוֹלַדְתָּם) should be strong enough to suppress a sneeze (ha-ʿittush - הָעִטּוּשׁ), and that they should stand constantly before him and be able to endure [discomfort or holding back bodily needs] when necessary (lit. "at the time of their need" - bi-shʿat tzorcham). And behold, he [Nebuchadnezzar] commanded to seek those in whom these qualities were found, and [such qualities combined] are not found in a youth younger than 15 years old (ט"ו - Tet Vav = 15). And behold, there was no need to teach them [these already proficient youths] for the three years except for the Chaldean script and their language, so they could speak with the king in his language. And behold, they [Daniel and companions] left Jerusalem [already relatively] mature (lit. 'great ones'). And behold, Daniel left [Jerusalem] at age fifteen (tet-vav shanah - ט"ו שָׁנָה) when Jehoiakim went to Babylon [referring to the events around Daniel 1:1-2, i.e., Nebuchadnezzar's 1st year]. And [after] eight years [of Nebuchadnezzar's] reign (וּשְׁמוֹנֶה שָׁנִים מָלַךְ - u-shmoneh shanim malach), behold, [Daniel would be around] 23 (כ"ג). And some say [Daniel's life/prophecy extended through the time of] Zedekiah [i.e., until the Temple's destruction in Nebuchadnezzar's 19th year]. And behold, [adding approximately] 4, and 30, and 70 years (daled ve-lamed ve-ʿayin shanah) [to his age from the time of the exile], he [Daniel] would thus be very old in the days of Esther. Perhaps he had already died a...

verses.hebrew_full: וַיְמַן֩ לָהֶ֨ם הַמֶּ֜לֶךְ דְּבַר־י֣וֹם בְּיוֹמ֗וֹ מִפַּת־בַּ֤ג הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ וּמִיֵּ֣ין מִשְׁתָּ֔יו וּֽלְגַדְּלָ֖ם שָׁנִ֣ים שָׁל֑וֹשׁ וּמִ֨קְצָתָ֔ם יַֽעַמְד֖וּ לִפְנֵ֥י הַמֶּֽלֶךְ
verses.translation: The king allotted daily rations to them from the king’s food and from the wine he drank. They were to be educated for three years, at the end of which they were to enter the king’s service.
verses.rashi: allotted Heb. וַיְמַן, an expression of preparation. of... food Heb. מִפַּת־בַּג. That is the name of the king’s fare in the language of the Chaldeans. Some interpret פַּת־בַּג, [as] bread, but it does not appear correct, for it is written (below verse 16): “The steward would take away פַּת־בָּגָם... and give them pulse.” Now pulse is not a substitute for bread, but a substitute for cooked food.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:5] Vayman (וַיְמַן - And the king allotted): This is a transitive verb (po'al yotze - פֹּעַל יוֹצֵא). And the meaning is: to give them portions/allotments (manot - מָנוֹת). And the meaning of pat-bag (פַּת־בַּג) is provisions/food (ma'achal - מַאֲכָל). And it is written in Scripture [using a related root word]: “And I will give you le-vag (כתיב: לְבַג - written form) / le-vaz (קרי: לְבַז - read form, for plunder)” [Ezekiel 25:7 - referring to the K'tiv/K'ri]. And the meaning [of bag and baz] is close. Perhaps the specific meaning of bag [here in Daniel] is cooked meat. [Daniel 1:5] U-mi-yein mishtav (וּמִיֵּין מִשְׁתָּיו - And from the wine of his drinkings): And it is not written "and from the wine of his drinking" [using the singular form mishtehu (מִשְׁתֵּהוּ)]. Because there is a specific day [or designated type] for drinking white wine, or red, or wine from a specific known place [implying the plural mishtav refers to the variety of wines the king drank on different occasions or made available]. [Daniel 1:5] Ul’gadlam (וּלְגַדְּלָם - And to educate them): [The purpose was] to accustom them to stand [in service] and to know the protocols/procedures of royalty (mishpetei ha-meluchah - מִשְׁפְּטֵי הַמְּלוּכָה). And this is [related to the requirement that they have] “ability/strength in them” [Daniel 1:4]. [Daniel 1:5] U-mi-ketzatam (וּמִקְצָתָם - And at the end of which): [This word literally means "from their end".] Some say [it refers back to the earlier use of the root ק-צ-ה meaning "some", as in] "some of the youths" [were chosen, like] "some of the vessels of the House of God" [were taken, Daniel 1:2]. And [the construction using mi-ketzeh (מִקְצֵה) meaning "from the end/some of"] as in “from the edge/some of his brothers” [Genesis 47:2] is also relevant grammatically. And it [the word u-mi-ketzatam here] means: at the end of the aforementioned years [i.e., the three years]. And behold, similar to it is [the usage in Daniel 1:15]: “And at the end of (u-mi-ketzat - וּמִקְצָת) ten days...”

words.eng: Ben Bag Bag. B. Kam. 27ᵇ; a. fr. Kidd. 10ᵇ Joh. son of B. B.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיְהִ֥י בָהֶ֖ם מִבְּנֵ֣י יְהוּדָ֑ה דָּנִיֵּ֣אל חֲנַנְיָ֔ה מִֽישָׁאֵ֖ל וַעֲזַרְיָֽה
verses.translation: Among them were the Judahites Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:6] Vayehi bahem (וַיְהִי בָהֶם - And among them were): The reason [it specifies] “of the children of Judah” is because there were [also] descendants of Benjamin in Jerusalem [so this distinguishes Daniel and his companions tribally from other potential captives]. And these [four] were not [biological] brothers. For the Scripture says [in Aramaic, referring to the context of Daniel 2:13 and Daniel 2:18 where Daniel sought mercy for himself and his companions]: “...Daniel and his companions (chavrohi - חַבְרוֹהִי) [were sought] to be killed” [cf. Daniel 2:13, Daniel 2:18]. [The use of the word "companions" implies they were associates, not necessarily siblings].

verses.hebrew_full: וַיָּ֧שֶׂם לָהֶ֛ם שַׂ֥ר הַסָּרִיסִ֖ים שֵׁמ֑וֹת וַיָּ֨שֶׂם לְדָֽנִיֵּ֜אל בֵּ֣לְטְשַׁאצַּ֗ר וְלַֽחֲנַנְיָה֙ שַׁדְרַ֔ךְ וּלְמִֽישָׁאֵ֣ל מֵישַׁ֔ךְ וְלַעֲזַרְיָ֖ה עֲבֵ֥ד נְגֽוֹ
verses.translation: The chief officer gave them new names; he named Daniel Belteshazzar, Hananiah Shadrach, Mishael Meshach, and Azariah Abed-nego.
verses.rashi: Belteshazzar He named him after the god of Babylon, which was called Bel, as it is said (4: 5): “whose name is Belteshazzar, like the name of my god.” Teshazzar is the Aramaic expression denoting wisdom.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:7] Vayasem (וַיָּשֶׂם - And [the chief officer] set/gave): Just as Pharaoh gave Joseph the name Zaphenath-paneah [Genesis 41:45]. And Rav Saadia [Gaon] said: that Bel [in Belteshazzar] is a Chaldean name/word, and likewise -Tzar [as in Belteshazzar, Nebuchadnezzar]. And similarly [names like] Nebuchadnezzar, Belshazzar, Sheshbazzar [contain these elements]. And the name Bel is explicitly [linked by Nebuchadnezzar himself when he says about Daniel/Belteshazzar] “according to the name of my god” [Daniel 4:5]. And [Saadia also states that] Shadrach [and] Meshach are Chaldean language [names]. And the Gaon [Saadia] said that Nego [in Abed-nego] is like Nebo [the name of a Babylonian deity], but [this is asserted] without proof.

verses.hebrew_full: וַיָּ֤שֶׂם דָּנִיֵּאל֙ עַל־לִבּ֔וֹ אֲשֶׁ֧ר לֹֽא־יִתְגָּאַ֛ל בְּפַת־בַּ֥ג הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ וּבְיֵ֣ין מִשְׁתָּ֑יו וַיְבַקֵּשׁ֙ מִשַּׂ֣ר הַסָּרִיסִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֖ר לֹ֥א יִתְגָּאָֽל
verses.translation: Daniel resolved not to defile himself with the king’s food or the wine he drank, so he sought permission of the chief officer not to defile himself,
verses.rashi: not to be defiled Heb. לֹא יָתְגָּאַל, an expression of sullying.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:8] Vayasem Daniel al-libbo (וַיָּשֶׂם דָּנִיֵּאל עַל־לִבּוֹ - And Daniel resolved; lit. And Daniel placed upon his heart): The king's pat-bag (פַּת־בַּג) [food/provisions] is forbidden [according to Jewish law], and the wine is also forbidden. And it is stated [regarding the prohibition, particularly of the wine - likely Ibn Ezra's Aramaic paraphrase summarizing the concept]: “[One might say/It implies] to pour it [the wine] as a libation according to their rite for their gods” [alluding to practices like those mentioned in Daniel 5:4].

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּתֵּ֤ן הָֽאֱלֹהִים֙ אֶת־דָּ֣נִיֵּ֔אל לְחֶ֖סֶד וּֽלְרַחֲמִ֑ים לִפְנֵ֖י שַׂ֥ר הַסָּרִיסִֽים
verses.translation: and God disposed the chief officer to be kind and compassionate toward Daniel.
verses.rashi: the chief officer who was the chief over the king’s servants.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:9] Vayiten (וַיִּתֵּן - And God gave/disposed): The Gaon [Saadia Gaon] said: The meaning is that He [God] granted him [Daniel] favor (chen - חֵן), kindness (chesed - חֶסֶד), and compassion (rachamim - רַחֲמִים) in the eyes of the chief officer. [However,] he [the chief officer] did not [immediately] grant his [Daniel's] request. But he [the chief officer] responded [in such a way, as seen in the next verse] that he did not reveal his [Daniel's] secret [plan/request] to the king, because [if the king found out about the special diet and the youths looked worse for it] he [the king] would not let him [the chief officer] live. [Therefore, according to Saadia, the "favor" here was the officer's willingness to listen and his discreet handling of the sensitive request, rather than outright agreement at this point].

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·Daniel = "God is my judge"

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֜אמֶר שַׂ֤ר הַסָּרִיסִים֙ לְדָ֣נִיֵּ֔אל יָרֵ֤א אֲנִי֙ אֶת־אֲדֹנִ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ אֲשֶׁ֣ר מִנָּ֔ה אֶת־מַאֲכַלְכֶ֖ם וְאֶת־מִשְׁתֵּיכֶ֑ם אֲשֶׁ֡ר לָ֩מָּה֩ יִרְאֶ֨ה אֶת־פְּנֵיכֶ֜ם זֹֽעֲפִ֗ים מִן־הַיְלָדִים֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר כְּגִֽילְכֶ֔ם וְחִיַּבְתֶּ֥ם אֶת־רֹאשִׁ֖י לַמֶּֽלֶךְ
verses.translation: The chief officer said to Daniel, “I fear that my lord the king, who allotted food and drink to you, will notice that you look out of sorts, unlike the other youths of your age—and you will put my life in jeopardy with the king.”
verses.rashi: who allotted who prepared and provided. like you כְּגִילְכֶם, of your likeness. It is the language of the Mishnah; one of his likeness. and you will forfeit my head to the king You shall bring about that I will be condemned to be beheaded.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:10] Vayomer (וַיֹּאמֶר - And the chief officer said): It is written “your food” (ma’achalkhem - מַאֲכַלְכֶם), and this refers to the pat (פַּת - food) and the bag (בַּג - provisions/meat). And “your drinkings” (mishteikhem - מִשְׁתֵּיכֶם) is a plural form, [meaning] from whatever the king drinks in the summer and winter seasons, they too would drink. [Daniel 1:10] P’neikhem zo’afim (פְּנֵיכֶם זֹעֲפִים - Your faces looking troubled/out of sorts): like the faces of troubled/angry people. [Daniel 1:10] Ke-gilchem (כְּגִילְכֶם - Like your age group): This word [gil (גִּיל) meaning age group/peer] has no parallel (re’a - רֵעַ) in Scripture [used this way], only in the words of our predecessors (kadmoneinu - קַדְמוֹנֵינוּ, i.e., the Sages of the Rabbinic period), [as in the common Rabbinic phrase] ben gilo (בֶּן גִּילוֹ - "one of his age group"). [Daniel 1:10] Ve-chiyavtem (וְחִיַּבְתֶּם - And you will endanger/obligate): This is derived from the root [ח-י/ו-ב] related to “his pledge” (chavolato - חֲבֹלָתוֹ) [cf. Ezekiel 18:7, Ezekiel 18:16] and “debt/liability” (chov - חוֹב). And they [words derived from this root] exist in two grammatical structures (binyanim - בִּנְיָנִים), but their root is [the same]. Similar to [the variation between] kam (קָם - he arose) and kiyyeim (קִיַּם - he confirmed/established) [in the phrase] “confirming (kiyyam - קִיַּם) these days of Purim” [Esther 9:32, where kiyyam is the Piel form from the root ק-ו-ם, usually conjugated as kam in the Qal].

words.eng: I (first pers. sing. -usually used for emphasis)

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר דָּנִיֵּ֖אל אֶל־הַמֶּלְצַ֑ר אֲשֶׁ֤ר מִנָּה֙ שַׂ֣ר הַסָּֽרִיסִ֔ים עַל־דָּנִיֵּ֣אל חֲנַנְיָ֔ה מִֽישָׁאֵ֖ל וַעֲזַרְיָֽה
verses.translation: Daniel replied to the guard whom the chief officer had put in charge of Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah,
verses.rashi: the steward Heb. הַמֶּלְצַר, the one who arranges the portions and the plates, called maestre sala, seneschal, steward.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:11] Vayomer... HaMeltsar (וַיֹּאמֶר... הַמֶּלְצַר - And Daniel said... The guard/steward): This [HaMeltsar] is a Chaldean word [meaning] the one who gives the portions/allotments (manot - מָנוֹת). And behold, the meaning of “whom [the chief officer] had appointed” (asher minnah - אֲשֶׁר מִנָּה) is like “appointed/overseer” (ha-pkid - הַפְּקִיד). And in the language of the Sages, [it is like] memuneh (מְמֻנֶּה - appointed one/overseer). And for it [the word Meltsar itself] to be derived from [the concept of] "one who appoints/apportions (minnah) the portions (manot)" is very difficult [linguistically], according to Ibn Ezra. And especially since it is written concerning Daniel [and his companions, implying a specific context that perhaps makes a simple etymological link less likely in Ibn Ezra's view].

verses.hebrew_full: נַס־נָ֥א אֶת־עֲבָדֶ֖יךָ יָמִ֣ים עֲשָׂרָ֑ה וְיִתְּנוּ־לָ֜נוּ מִן־הַזֵּרֹעִ֛ים וְנֹאכְלָ֖ה וּמַ֥יִם וְנִשְׁתֶּֽה
verses.translation: “Please test your servants for ten days, giving us legumes to eat and water to drink.
verses.rashi: test Heb. נַס an expression of (נִסָיוֹן) testing. some pulse a type of beans instead of the food.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:12] Nas-na (נַס־נָא - Test, please): This [Nas (נַס)] is the imperative form (leshon tzivui - לְשׁוֹן צִוּוּי), similar to [the use of na with an imperative in]: “Please entreat (chal-na - חַל־נָא) the favor of the Lord” [I Kings 13:6]. [Daniel 1:12] Yamin asarah (יָמִים עֲשָׂרָה - Ten days): And the reason for “ten days” is because a living being (chai - חַי) cannot endure hardship [or illness, without it becoming noticeable] for more than nine days. Thus the masters of natural science/biology (ba'alei ha-toladot - בַּעֲלֵי הַתּוֹלָדוֹת) have mentioned. And instead of the king's pat-bag (פַּת־בַּג) [food], give us legumes (zero'im - זֵרֹעִים). These are seeds/grains (gargirim - גַּרְגְּרִים) when they mature/dry. And instead of wine, give us water.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·slave
to give·inflected pers. pron. meaning ‘to us’.

verses.hebrew_full: וְיֵרָא֤וּ לְפָנֶ֙יךָ֙ מַרְאֵ֔ינוּ וּמַרְאֵה֙ הַיְלָדִ֔ים הָאֹ֣כְלִ֔ים אֵ֖ת פַּת־בַּ֣ג הַמֶּ֑לֶךְ וְכַאֲשֶׁ֣ר תִּרְאֵ֔ה עֲשֵׂ֖ה עִם־עֲבָדֶֽיךָ
verses.translation: Then compare our appearance with that of the youths who eat of the king’s food, and do with your servants as you see fit.”
verses.rashi: and as you will see According to what you see, do.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:13] Ve-yera’u (וְיֵרָא֤וּ - And let [our appearance] be seen): [Meaning:] At the end of the ten [days, let] our appearance [be seen and compared]. And he [Daniel] mentioned “the king’s pat-bag [food]” and adopted a concise manner of speaking (derech ketzarah - דֶּרֶךְ קְצָרָה) in that he did not mention the wine [even though they were also abstaining from it].

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: וַיִּשְׁמַ֥ע לָהֶ֖ם לַדָּבָ֣ר הַזֶּ֑ה וַיְנַסֵּ֖ם יָמִ֥ים עֲשָׂרָֽה
verses.translation: He agreed to this plan of theirs, and tested them for ten days.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:14] Vayishma (וַיִּשְׁמַע - And he listened/agreed): Yefet ben Ali said: that every day he [the guard/Meltsar] would observe their appearance (lit. "see their faces") throughout the ten-day period (lit. "until ten days").

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִקְצָת֙ יָמִ֣ים עֲשָׂרָ֔ה נִרְאָ֤ה מַרְאֵיהֶם֙ ט֔וֹב וּבְרִיאֵ֖י בָּשָׂ֑ר מִ֨ן־כׇּל־הַיְלָדִ֔ים הָאֹ֣כְלִ֔ים אֵ֖ת פַּת־בַּ֥ג הַמֶּֽלֶךְ
verses.translation: When the ten days were over, they looked better and healthier than all the youths who were eating of the king’s food.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:15] U-mi-ketzat... nir’ah (וּמִקְצָת... נִרְאָה - And at the end of... appeared): The meaning is: their entire appearance [looked good]. And the Gaon [Saadia Gaon] said: This was a great miracle. But [Ibn Ezra argues] there is no need [to assume a miracle], because they could have been eating rice (orez - אוֹרֶז), which is called in the Arabic language aruz (ארוז). And it [rice] is very esteemed/excellent, and more pleasing/palatable than wheat, and produces pure blood [according to medieval medicine]. It is the food of the people of India, who do not have wheat, and cooked dishes are made from it. And this rice flourishes/increases greatly [in volume] when it is cooked with milk. In general, a small amount of it [rice] satiates, and it is heavy in weight. Its grains are very slender and not round, and its nature [in humoral terms, i.e., its inherent quality according to medieval science] is hot and moist. And it is astonishing [coming] from a great Sage that he said it [the zero’im] was millet (dochan - דֹּחַן), whose grains are round. It lasts for many years and does not rot, but it is a poor food, and its nature is cold and dry [the opposite qualities needed for looking healthy, according to humoral theory]. Also, among the types of legumes (kitniyot - קִטְנִיּוֹת) are peas (afunim - אֲפוּנִים) – but not those [types] found in France, because those are cold [in nature], bad [food], and cause flatulence. Rather, the peas [Ibn Ezra refers to] are those found in Spain, Egypt, Rome [area], and Provence. They are of three types: some white, some red, and some black – and this [black] type is the most esteemed of all. All three types are hot and moist [in nature]. And their cooking water is a remedy for the liver. They brighten the face more than wine. (And a person can recognize that those [peas] in France are without a 'mouth' [perhaps referring to a distinct seam or characteristic shape], whereas the three [good] types have a 'mouth'). And behold, rice and [these good] peas are [types of] legumes/seeds (zero’im), and they make the flesh healthy (mavri’im et ha-basar - מַבְרִיאִים אֶת הַבָּשָׂר) [echoing the verse's phrase beri’ei basar (בְּרִיאֵי בָשָׂר), healthy of flesh]. Furthermore, any food that is desired by the soul/appetite is more beneficial than food that the soul/appetite does not like, even if [the disliked food] is hot and moist according to the [ideal] nature of living things [i.e., even if it matches the body's ideal humoral balance]. And behold, these [youths] were eating something that their soul/appetite desired [since they chose it over the king's food], and [therefore] it was beneficial for them.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: וַיְהִ֣י הַמֶּלְצַ֗ר נֹשֵׂא֙ אֶת־פַּת־בָּגָ֔ם וְיֵ֖ין מִשְׁתֵּיהֶ֑ם וְנֹתֵ֥ן לָהֶ֖ם זֵרְעֹנִֽים
verses.translation: So the guard kept on removing their food, and the wine they were supposed to drink, and gave them legumes.
verses.rashi: carry away their food He would take it for himself.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:16] Vayehi... nosei (וַיְהִי... נֹשֵׂא - And it was... removing): [Meaning the guard/Meltsar] took it upon himself (be-nafsho - בְּנַפְשׁוֹ) [to consistently remove their royal rations]. And that which he [Daniel] was brief in mentioning [i.e., omitted mentioning] the wine [in Daniel 1:13], he [the guard, acting, or the text, describing] mentioned it now. And the Nun [element] of legumes (zeronim - זֵרְעֹנִים) is additional [forming the -on suffix before the plural ending, rather than the simpler plural zera'im (זְרָעִים)]. Like [the K'tiv (written form) in Psalms 123:4] le-ga’yonim (לגאיונים - referring to 'the haughty'), and many [words] are like them [formed with such additional elements].

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·fragment·[junction

verses.hebrew_full: וְהַיְלָדִ֤ים הָאֵ֙לֶּה֙ אַרְבַּעְתָּ֔ם נָתַ֨ן לָהֶ֧ם הָֽאֱלֹהִ֛ים מַדָּ֥ע וְהַשְׂכֵּ֖ל בְּכׇל־סֵ֣פֶר וְחׇכְמָ֑ה וְדָנִיֵּ֣אל הֵבִ֔ין בְּכׇל־חָז֖וֹן וַחֲלֹמֽוֹת
verses.translation: God made all four of these young men intelligent and proficient in all writings and wisdom, and Daniel had understanding of visions and dreams of all kinds.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:17] Veha-yeladim (וְהַיְלָדִים - And [to] these youths): The reason [it specifies] “the four of them” (arba’atam - אַרְבַּעְתָּם) is [to emphasize] that they were equal in all [general] wisdom. However, Daniel had an additional [ability] beyond them, in that he understood every vision and the interpretation of dreams, for he alone was a prophet, and not the [other] three.

verses.hebrew_full: וּלְמִקְצָת֙ הַיָּמִ֔ים אֲשֶׁר־אָמַ֥ר הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ לַהֲבִיאָ֑ם וַיְבִיאֵם֙ שַׂ֣ר הַסָּרִיסִ֔ים לִפְנֵ֖י נְבֻכַדְנֶצַּֽר
verses.translation: When the time the king had set for their presentation had come, the chief officer presented them to Nebuchadnezzar.
verses.rashi: that the king ordered to bring them that they come before him.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:18] U-le-miketzat (וּלְמִקְצָת - And at the end of): [Meaning] at the end of the three years [mentioned in Daniel 1:5]. [Daniel 1:18] La-havi’am (לַהֲבִיאָם - To bring them): [Meaning:] before the king.

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: וַיְדַבֵּ֣ר אִתָּם֮ הַמֶּ֒לֶךְ֒ וְלֹ֤א נִמְצָא֙ מִכֻּלָּ֔ם כְּדָנִיֵּ֣אל חֲנַנְיָ֔ה מִֽישָׁאֵ֖ל וַעֲזַרְיָ֑ה וַיַּֽעַמְד֖וּ לִפְנֵ֥י הַמֶּֽלֶךְ
verses.translation: The king spoke with them, and of them all none was equal to Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah; so these entered the king’s service.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:19] Vayedaber (וַיְדַבֵּר - And the king spoke): In his [own] language [i.e., Chaldean/Aramaic, which the youths had learned]. [Daniel 1:19] Ve-lo nimtza (וְלֹא נִמְצָא - And none was found): [Meaning] from all the Jewish youths who were exiled, [none were found] like these four. [Daniel 1:19] Vaya’amdu lifnei ha-melech (וַיַּעַמְדוּ לִפְנֵי הַמֶּלֶךְ - And they stood before the king): They alone [were selected for this service from the group].

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: וְכֹ֗ל דְּבַר֙ חׇכְמַ֣ת בִּינָ֔ה אֲשֶׁר־בִּקֵּ֥שׁ מֵהֶ֖ם הַמֶּ֑לֶךְ וַֽיִּמְצָאֵ֞ם עֶ֣שֶׂר יָד֗וֹת עַ֤ל כׇּל־הַֽחַרְטֻמִּים֙ הָֽאַשָּׁפִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֖ר בְּכׇל־מַלְכוּתֽוֹ
verses.translation: Whenever the king put a question to them requiring wisdom and understanding, he found them to be ten times better than all the magicians and exorcists throughout his realm.
verses.rashi: the necromancers These are the ones who inquire of טִימֵי מֵתִים, the bones of the dead, as you say, “May his bones be crushed,” שְּׁחִיק טַמַיָא. the astrologers These are the ones who “press” the constellation. So it is explained in Tanhuma (Miketz 2, Buber, Miketz 4).
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:20] Ve-chol (וְכֹל - And every): This indicates that Nebuchadnezzar [himself] knew wisdom, and he found each one of these four knew ten measures/times (eser yadot - עֶשֶׂר יָדוֹת) [more] than every magician (chartom - חַרְטֹם) and exorcist/astrologer (ashshaf - אַשָּׁף). And it is possible that ten (eser) is [treated linguistically as] a plural term, like [in the phrase] “than ten rulers” (me-asarah shallitim - מֵעֲשָׂרָה שַׁלִּיטִים) [Ecclesiastes 7:19], because ten is a sum total/complete count (sach cheshbon - סָךְ חֶשְׁבּוֹן). And the author of Sefer Yetzirah said: “Ten Sefirot of Nothingness” (eser sefirot blimah - עֶשֶׂר סְפִירוֹת בְּלִי מָה) [Sefer Yetzirah 1:4]. And the magicians (chartummim - חַרְטֻמִּים) are not the exorcists/astrologers (ashshafim - אַשָּׁפִים), because behold, it is written [elsewhere]: “to call the magicians (chartummim) and the exorcists/astrologers (ashshafim)” [Daniel 2:2, listing them separately]. And there [in the commentary on that verse] I will explain them.

verses.hebrew_full: וַֽיְהִי֙ דָּֽנִיֵּ֔אל עַד־שְׁנַ֥ת אַחַ֖ת לְכ֥וֹרֶשׁ הַמֶּֽלֶךְ
verses.translation: Daniel was there until the first year of King Cyrus.
verses.rashi: And Daniel was there in greatness in Babylon until the first year of King Cyrus. According to the one who says that Hathach (Esther; 4:5, 6, 9, 10) is Daniel; and why was he called Hathach? Because he was cut off (חֲתָכוּהוּ) from his greatness; [according to his explanation] we must say that this is Cyrus I, who preceded Ahasuerus. But according to the one who says that all royal affairs were decided (נֶחְתָּכִים) by his orders, we must say that this Cyrus was Darius II, [who came] after Ahasuerus, and in the days of Darius the Mede, when they cast [Daniel] into the lions’ den, he was not demoted from his greatness, as it is said (6:29): “And this Daniel prospered during the reign of Darius and during the reign of Cyrus the Persian.” We learn that during the days of Cyrus I he was [still] in his greatness.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 1:21] Vayehi (וַיְהִי - And [Daniel] was): The meaning is that he was in Babylon until the first year (shnat achat - שְׁנַת אַחַת) of King Cyrus. And if it had been written "until one year" (shanah achat - שָׁנָה אַחַת), it would seem that a complete year had passed. But by its saying [using the construct form] shnat achat (lit. "year of one"), the meaning is [that he was there sometime] within that year [not necessarily until its end].

verses.hebrew_full: וּבִשְׁנַ֣ת שְׁתַּ֗יִם לְמַלְכוּת֙ נְבֻֽכַדְנֶצַּ֔ר חָלַ֥ם נְבֻֽכַדְנֶצַּ֖ר חֲלֹמ֑וֹת וַתִּתְפָּ֣עֶם רוּח֔וֹ וּשְׁנָת֖וֹ נִהְיְתָ֥ה עָלָֽיו
verses.translation: In the second year of the reign of Nebuchadnezzar, Nebuchadnezzar had a dream; his spirit was agitated, yet he was overcome by sleep.
verses.rashi: Now in the second year of Nebuchadnezzar’s reign It is impossible to say this, except that [it happened] in the second year after the destruction of the Temple. So it is taught in Seder Olam (ch. 28), and Scripture called it “of Nebuchadnezzar’s reign,” because he demonstrated his insolence by entering the Inner Sanctum of the Sovereign of the Universe. and his spirit was troubled Heb. וַתִּתְפָּעֶם רוּחוֹ. Concerning Pharaoh it says (Gen. 41:8): וַתִּפָּעֶם רוּחוֹ, for only the interpretation of the dream was hidden from him, but here, since the dream too was hidden from him, the expression is doubled: וַתִּתְפָּעֶם, an expression of pulsating and beating. was interrupted Heb. נִהְיְתָה, an expression of breaking like (Ezek. 7:25): “Breach upon breach (הוָה עַל הוָה).”
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:1] Uvishnat (וּבִשְׁנַת - And in the... year): One interpretation (lit. "says the book," possibly referring to an interpretation of the book) is that this was the second year of Nebuchadnezzar's reign over Jerusalem, after Zedekiah rebelled against him and he [Nebuchadnezzar] destroyed it. And Rabbi Moshe [perhaps Ibn Gikatilla] said: that [only] two years remained of his reign [at this point]. But we have not found anything like this [latter interpretation] in all of Scripture. And the correct [interpretation] in my eyes is: [It refers to the second year] of his reign over all the nations that Jeremiah mentioned he would conquer [cf. Jeremiah 25:9, Jeremiah 27:6]. Then [after achieving this dominion] he became tranquil and thought thoughts upon his bed about what would be in the end of days, as Daniel mentioned [later, Daniel 2:29]. And it mentions dreams [plural] because in the statue there is gold, silver, bronze, iron, and earthenware – whereas the [usual] way of a dreamer is to see one thing [a singular object]. [Daniel 2:1] Vatitpaʿem (וַתִּתְפָּעֶם - His spirit was agitated): [This root פ-ע-ם relates to being struck or agitated,] like [David says]: “I was agitated (nifʿamti - נִפְעַמְתִּי) and could not speak” [Psalms 77:5)]. And their derivation is [related to the striking action in] “striking (holem - הוֹלֵם) the anvil (paʿam - פַּעַם)” [Isaiah 41:7] – like a person who is struck/crushed and struck/crushed again. The Gaon [Saadia Gaon] said: that Pharaoh knew the dream but did not know its interpretation, therefore it is written concerning Pharaoh “and his spirit was agitated” (vattippaʿem rucho - וַתִּפָּעֶם רוּחוֹ, Genesis 41:8), which is from the Nifal conjugation. But [regarding] Nebuchadnezzar, who did not know the dream or the interpretation, it is written concerning him “and his spirit became agitated” (vatitpaʿem rucho - וַתִּתְפָּעֶם רוּחוֹ, Daniel 2:1), [using the Hitpael conjugation]. If so [Ibn Ezra challenges Saadia], what will he do [with the fact] that he [Nebuchadnezzar himself later] says “my spirit was agitated” (vatipaʿem ruchi - וַתִּפָּעֶם רוּחִי) [Daniel 2:3], [using the Nifal form even though he didn't know the dream]? And it is possible that the dagesh [doubling dot] in the Pey [פ] of vatipaʿem (וַתִּפָּעֶם - Nifal form) is instead of the Nun [which typically characterizes] the Nifal conjugation [indicating assimilation]. Because a verb which is transitive [in Qal/Piel] can be [expressed passively/reflexively in the] Nifal – like “and he fought (vayilachem - וַיִּלָּחֶם, Nifal) against Israel” [Numbers 21:1]. Or [alternatively, explaining the Hitpael form וַתִּתְפָּעֶם in this verse, Daniel 2:1], the dagesh in the Pey is due to the assimilation of the Tav [ת] of the Hitpael conjugation [a common feature of this conjugation with certain root letters]. And thus, “his spirit became agitated” (vatitpaʿem rucho) would be like [saying] “his strength became agitated/stirred” (vatipaʿem kocho - וַתִּפָּעֶם כֹ...

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"
Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר הַ֠מֶּ֠לֶךְ לִקְרֹ֨א לַֽחַרְטֻמִּ֜ים וְלָֽאַשָּׁפִ֗ים וְלַֽמְכַשְּׁפִים֙ וְלַכַּשְׂדִּ֔ים לְהַגִּ֥יד לַמֶּ֖לֶךְ חֲלֹמֹתָ֑יו וַיָּבֹ֕אוּ וַיַּֽעַמְד֖וּ לִפְנֵ֥י הַמֶּֽלֶךְ
verses.translation: The king ordered the magicians, exorcists, sorcerers, and Chaldeans to be summoned in order to tell the king what he had dreamed. They came and stood before the king,
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:2] Vayomer ha-melech... la-chartummim (וַיֹּאמֶר הַמֶּלֶךְ... לַֽחַרְטֻמִּים - And the king ordered... magicians): These are the sages of natural science (chakhmei ha-toladet - חַכְמֵי הַתּוֹלֶדֶת) who know the fundamental principles (ikkarim - עִקָּרִים) and the properties/outcomes (toladot - תּוֹלָדוֹת) of everything, and all actions that are performed secretly (b'lat - בְּלָט). And thus it is written regarding the magicians (chartummim - חַרְטֻמִּים) of Egypt [cf. Exodus 7:11, Exodus 7:22, etc.]. [Daniel 2:2] v'la-ashshafim (וְלָֽאַשָּׁפִים - And Exorcists/Astrologers): These are the physicians (rof'im - רוֹפְאִים). v'la-mekhashshefim (וְלַֽמְכַשְּׁפִים - And Sorcerers): These are the ones who alter the natural order (davar ha-toladah - דְּבַר הַתּוֹלָדָה) to visual appearance [i.e., create illusions]. [Daniel 2:2] v'la-Kasdim (וְלַכַּשְׂדִּים - And the Chaldeans): These are the sages of the constellations/astrology (chakhmei ha-mazzalot - חַכְמֵי הַמַּזָּלוֹת). [And elsewhere mentioned are] the Diviners/Deciders (v'ha-gazrin - וְהַגָּזְרִין [cf. Daniel 2:27]): they are the masters of divination (kesamim - קְסָמִים) who practice augury (nichushim - נִחוּשִׁים). And these [types of practitioners] are the ones the king needs [for this matter], not the sages of measurements (middot - מִדּוֹת), calculation (cheshbon - חֶשְׁבּוֹן), valuations (arakhim - עֲרָכִים), the science of building (chokhmat ha-binyanim - חָכְמַת הַבִּנְיָנִים), or the higher wisdom [metaphysics] (ha-chokhmah ha-elyonah - הַחָכְמָה הָעֶלְיוֹנָה). And behold, he called the Magicians (chartummim) because they know the fundamental principles of dreams and how the soul sees visions of the night. He also called the Exorcists/Astrologers (ashshafim) [whom Ibn Ezra identified as physicians] because they know, from the pulse of the arm or from the urine, the changes in the body – and most dreams follow the constitution (matkonet - מַתְכֹּנֶת) of the body [so they might diagnose the cause of the troubling dream]. He also called the Sorcerers (mekhashshefim) because they know the propitious times/hours for performing their actions, [thinking] perhaps they could observe [astrological conditions?] and know the dream according to the arrangement of the stars at this [particular] hour. He also called the Chaldeans (Kasdim) [astrologers] because they know future events according to their craft, through their knowledge of people's nativities (moladim - מוֹלָדִים) – calculating the [planetary positions by astrological] degrees (maʿalot - מַעֲלוֹת) concerning the [astrological] terms (g'vulin - גְּבוּלִין), and concerning the aspects (mabbatim - מַבָּטִים) of the planets ("serving stars") and the two luminaries [Sun and Moon], and the cycles (tekufot - תְּקוּפוֹת) of the year, month, week, and day. And they knew Nebuchadnezzar's nativity chart. Therefore, the Chaldeans answered first [cf. Daniel 2:4], because the matter [of interpreting such a significant dream] depended on them a...

verses.hebrew_full: וַיֹּ֧אמֶר לָהֶ֛ם הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ חֲל֣וֹם חָלָ֑מְתִּי וַתִּפָּ֣עֶם רוּחִ֔י לָדַ֖עַת אֶֽת־הַחֲלֽוֹם
verses.translation: and the king said to them, “I have had a dream and I am full of anxiety to know what I have dreamed.”
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:3] Vayomer (וַיֹּאמֶר - And the king said): One of the Geonim said: that Nebuchadnezzar did know the dream and came [with this demand] to test his wise men. [The Gaon argued that] if this were not so, he would not have believed Daniel's words [later]. But behold, Scripture [the verse itself] attests: “my spirit was agitated (vatipaʿem ruchi - וַתִּפָּעֶם רוּחִי) to know the dream.” And when he [Daniel later] said to him [the king] “your thoughts came [to your mind] upon your bed” [Daniel 2:29], he [the king] immediately remembered that it was so. And when he [Daniel] mentioned the statue, then he [the king] likewise remembered it.

words.eng: sign of the definite direct object·dream

verses.hebrew_full: וַֽיְדַבְּר֧וּ הַכַּשְׂדִּ֛ים לַמֶּ֖לֶךְ אֲרָמִ֑ית מַלְכָּא֙ לְעָלְמִ֣ין חֱיִ֔י אֱמַ֥ר חֶלְמָ֛א (לעבדיך) לְעַבְדָ֖ךְ וּפִשְׁרָ֥א נְחַוֵּֽא
verses.translation: The Chaldeans spoke to the king in Aramaic, “O king, live forever! Relate the dream to your servants, and we will tell its meaning.”
verses.rashi: May the king live forever Aram. מַלְכָּא לְעָלְמִין חֱיִי. Tell your servants the dream [as translated,] Tell your servants the dream.
verses.ibn_ezra: Daniel 2:4] Vayedabberu (וַֽיְדַבְּר֧וּ - And the Chaldeans spoke): Now [the book] begins to speak the Chaldean language [Aramaic], which is the language of the king, as they [the Chaldeans] spoke [to the king] and he replied to them [in that language].

verses.hebrew_full: עָנֵ֤ה מַלְכָּא֙ וְאָמַ֣ר (לכשדיא) לְכַשְׂדָּאֵ֔י מִלְּתָ֖ה מִנִּ֣י אַזְדָּ֑א הֵ֣ן לָ֤א תְהֽוֹדְעוּנַּ֙נִי֙ חֶלְמָ֣א וּפִשְׁרֵ֔הּ הַדָּמִין֙ תִּתְעַבְד֔וּן וּבָתֵּיכ֖וֹן נְוָלִ֥י יִתְּשָׂמֽוּן
verses.translation: The king said in reply to the Chaldeans, “I hereby decree: If you will not make the dream and its meaning known to me, you shall be torn limb from limb and your houses confiscated.
verses.rashi: The matter has escaped me The thing has left me. If you do not let me know the dream and its meaning [as translated,] if you do not let me know the dream and its meaning. you shall be torn limb from limb Aram. הַדָמִין תִּתְעַבְּדוּן. You shall be cut to pieces, and there are many such in the Gemara (Gittin 67b): “Arrange the cuts” (הַדוּמֵי הַדְמּוּהָ). a dungheap Aram. נְוָלִי, a dungheap. shall be made Aram. יִתְּשָּׂמוּן an expression of וְיָשֵּׂם, and he will make. They shall be made into dungheaps.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:5] ʿAneh malka (עָנֵה מַלְכָּא - Answered the king): There are many words in the Chaldean language [Aramaic] whose interpretation depends on the context (lit. 'according to the sense of the place'). And according to my interpretation, azda (אַזְדָּא) [from the phrase mill'ta minni azda (מִלְּתָה מִנִּי אַזְדָּא)] is like kayyama (קַיָּמָא) [Aramaic: established, certain] and is true. The meaning is: this word/decree is established/certain [from me]. However, [the consequence is]: “If you will not make known to me the dream and its meaning,” you are liable to death [This is Ibn Ezra's paraphrase of the threat haddamin titʿavdun (הַדָּמִין תִּתְעַבְדוּן)]. And Yefet [ben Ali] said: [It means] “you shall be made pieces, pieces” (k'tiʿot k'tiʿot - קְטִיעוֹת קְטִיעוֹת). And he [Yefet] said that similar [usage of the related root ד-מ-ה, meaning 'to resemble' or 'to destroy/silence', connecting it to haddamin] is [in] “the king of Samaria shall be utterly cut off (nidmoh - נִדְמֹה)” [Hosea 10:15] and likewise “and I will destroy (v’damiti - וְדָמִיתִי) your mother” [Hosea 4:5]. As if it said: Pieces, pieces you shall be made by the sword. And the word n'vali (נְוָלִי) [from the phrase u-vatteikhon n'vali yit'samun (וּבָתֵּיכוֹן נְוָלִי יִתְּשָׂמוּן) - and your houses shall be made into n'vali] [means] ruins, destruction, and desolation. And Rabbi Yeshua said: [It means] like domen (דֹּמֶן - dung/refuse).

verses.hebrew_full: וְהֵ֨ן חֶלְמָ֤א וּפִשְׁרֵהּ֙ תְּֽהַחֲוֺ֔ן מַתְּנָ֤ן וּנְבִזְבָּה֙ וִיקָ֣ר שַׂגִּ֔יא תְּקַבְּל֖וּן מִן־קֳדָמָ֑י לָהֵ֕ן חֶלְמָ֥א וּפִשְׁרֵ֖הּ הַחֲוֺֽנִי
verses.translation: But if you tell the dream and its meaning, you shall receive from me gifts, presents, and great honor; therefore, tell me the dream and its meaning.”
verses.rashi: But if [as translated,] But if. and lavish presents [as translated,] presents. but [as translated,] but. tell me [as translated,] tell me.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:6] V'hen... u'nevizbah (וְהֵן... וּנְבִזְבָּה - But if... and presents/rewards): U'nevizbah [Means] a gift (minchah - מִנְחָה) and a present (doron - דּוֹרוֹן).

verses.hebrew_full: עֲנ֥וֹ תִנְיָנ֖וּת וְאָמְרִ֑ין מַלְכָּ֕א חֶלְמָ֛א יֵאמַ֥ר לְעַבְד֖וֹהִי וּפִשְׁרָ֥ה נְהַחֲוֵֽה
verses.translation: Once again they answered, “Let the king relate the dream to his servants, and we will tell its meaning.”
verses.rashi: a second time [as translated,] a second time. we shall tell [as translated,] we shall tell.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:7] ʿAnu tinyanut (עֲנוּ תִנְיָנוּת - They answered a second time): The Gaon [Saadia Gaon] said: that they did not add anything now beyond their first answer, except that in the first instance [Daniel 2:4] they were speaking directly to the king (l'nochach ha-melekh - לְנֹכַח הַמֶּלֶךְ), in the manner of [Jacob saying to Isaac] “Arise, now, sit” (kum na sh'vah - קוּם נָא שְׁבָה) [Genesis 27:19]. But now [they use] the third-person singular jussive form ["Let the king tell..."] which is not [so directly] addressed [to the person present], like [Esau saying of Isaac] “Let my father arise” (yakum avi - יָקֻם אָבִי) [Genesis 27:31]. And the reason [according to the Gaon] is that they were afraid to speak directly to him [now], and [so] they said [in effect]: "We have no [other] response to give back except what we [already] responded to the king." [The shift to a more indirect grammatical form reflects their fear and inability to offer a different answer.]

verses.hebrew_full: עָנֵ֤ה מַלְכָּא֙ וְאָמַ֔ר מִן־יַצִּיב֙ יָדַ֣ע אֲנָ֔ה דִּ֥י עִדָּנָ֖א אַנְתּ֣וּן זָבְנִ֑ין כׇּל־קֳבֵל֙ דִּ֣י חֲזֵית֔וֹן דִּ֥י אַזְדָּ֖א מִנִּ֥י מִלְּתָֽא
verses.translation: The king said in reply, “It is clear to me that you are playing for time, since you see that I have decreed
verses.rashi: in truth, I know [as translated,] in truth, I know. truth Aram. יַצִיב, a true and permanent thing. that this time you are doomed Aram. דִּי עִדָּנָא אַנְתוּן זַבְנִין, that this time you are delivered to death. because you see because you see that the matter escaped me, but you repeatedly ask me to tell you.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:8] ʿAneh malka (עָנֵה מַלְכָּא - Answered the king): [The king says:] Truly (min yatziv - מִן־יַצִּיב, lit. "from certainty") I know that you are buying time (ʿiddana antun zavnin - עִדָּנָא אַנְתּוּן זָבְנִין). The meaning is: you are delaying the hour/stalling. This is because (kol kovel di - כָּל־קֳבֵל דִּי) you saw that the word/decree (mill'ta - מִלְּתָא) is established/certain (azda - אַזְדָּא) from me. And it [the decree] is: "If you do not make the dream known to me, your sentence (dat'khon - דָּתְכוֹן) is one" [cf. Daniel 2:9] – the sentence (ha-dat - הַדָּת) that I gave, that you will be killed. And the Dalet (ד) of [the word] hizdaminton (הִזְדַּמִּנְתּוּן - "you have prepared/agreed upon" [found in Daniel 2:9]) [stands] instead of the Tav (ת) of the Hitpael conjugation [due to assimilation]. And Rabbi Marinus said: that similarly the word hizzakku (הִזַּכּוּ - "cleanse yourselves," Isaiah 1:16) is like hizdakku (הִזְדַּכּוּ - hypothetical form). But it is not plausible for it (hizzakku) to be from the Nifal conjugation because of the dagesh [strong dot] in the Khaf (כּ). And Rabbi Moshe [ibn Gikatilla] HaKohen Sefardi said: that it (hizzakku) is the Hitpael conjugation, and the dagesh in the Zayin (זּ) is due to the assimilation of the Tav (ת) [meaning the original form was hithzakku (הִתְזַכּוּ)]. And this is correct in my eyes.

verses.hebrew_full: דִּ֣י הֵן־חֶלְמָא֩ לָ֨א תְהֽוֹדְעֻנַּ֜נִי חֲדָה־הִ֣יא דָֽתְכ֗וֹן וּמִלָּ֨ה כִדְבָ֤ה וּשְׁחִיתָה֙ (הזמנתון) הִזְדְּמִנְתּוּן֙ לְמֵאמַ֣ר קׇֽדָמַ֔י עַ֛ד דִּ֥י עִדָּנָ֖א יִשְׁתַּנֵּ֑א לָהֵ֗ן חֶלְמָא֙ אֱמַ֣רוּ לִ֔י וְֽאִנְדַּ֕ע דִּ֥י פִשְׁרֵ֖הּ תְּהַחֲוֻנַּֽנִי
verses.translation: that if you do not make the dream known to me, there is but one verdict for you. You have conspired to tell me something false and fraudulent until circumstances change; so relate the dream to me, and I will then know that you can tell its meaning.”
verses.rashi: or if you have prepared yourselves...a false and corrupt word Or if you have prepared yourselves to say before me a false and corrupt word. until the time changes until the time changes, i.e., before the shadows of evening slant, when the time of morning changes. “Until the time changes” is an expression of rebuke and an exaggeration: so will he be punished, and the punishment was already delineated above. A similar instance is (Gen. 4:15): “Therefore, whoever kills Cain.” but the dream; tell me what I dreamed, and I shall know that you will surely tell me the interpretation.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:9] ʿad di ʿiddana yishtanne (עַד דִּי עִדָּנָא יִשְׁתַּנֵּא - ...until the time/circumstances change): [The king means, you hope to stall until] "And I [the king] become occupied with another matter" [and forget about this].

verses.hebrew_full: עֲנ֨וֹ (כשדיא) כַשְׂדָּאֵ֤י קֳדָם־מַלְכָּא֙ וְאָ֣מְרִ֔ין לָֽא־אִיתַ֤י אֱנָשׁ֙ עַל־יַבֶּשְׁתָּ֔א דִּ֚י מִלַּ֣ת מַלְכָּ֔א יוּכַ֖ל לְהַחֲוָיָ֑ה כׇּל־קֳבֵ֗ל דִּ֚י כׇּל־מֶ֙לֶךְ֙ רַ֣ב וְשַׁלִּ֔יט מִלָּ֤ה כִדְנָה֙ לָ֣א שְׁאֵ֔ל לְכׇל־חַרְטֹ֖ם וְאָשַׁ֥ף וְכַשְׂדָּֽי
verses.translation: The Chaldeans said in reply to the king, “There is no one on earth who can satisfy the king’s demand, for great king or ruler—none has ever asked such a thing of any magician, exorcist, or Chaldean.
verses.rashi: who...the king’s word who will be able to tell the king’s word. because Aram. כָּל קַבֵל, lit. all opposite; i.e., our reply is true, in view of the fact that there was never a king who asked anything like this. such a thing Aram. מִלָה כִדְּנָה, a thing like this. astrologer who presses the constellation.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:10] ʿAnu Khasda'e (עֲנוּ כַשְׂדָּאֵי - Answered the Chaldeans): [They said:] There is no man (enash - אֱנָשׁ) on the dry land (ʿal yabeshta - עַל־יַבֶּשְׁתָּא) who can tell the king's matter (millat malka - מִלַּת מַלְכָּא). And behold, every king who was before you never asked such a question as this.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִלְּתָ֨א דִֽי־מַלְכָּ֤ה שָׁאֵל֙ יַקִּירָ֔ה וְאׇחֳרָן֙ לָ֣א אִיתַ֔י דִּ֥י יְחַוִּנַּ֖הּ קֳדָ֣ם מַלְכָּ֑א לָהֵ֣ן אֱלָהִ֔ין דִּ֚י מְדָ֣רְה֔וֹן עִם־בִּשְׂרָ֖א לָ֥א אִיתֽוֹהִי
verses.translation: The thing asked by the king is difficult; there is no one who can tell it to the king except the gods whose abode is not among mortals.”
verses.rashi: difficult Aram. יַקִירָה, heavy, and its burden is heavy. and there is no other who can tell it and there is no other who will tell it. but the angels but the angels whose dwelling is not with flesh and blood. whose dwelling Aram. מְדָרְהוֹן, their dwelling. The Midrash of Rabbi Tanhuma (ad loc.) [says]: וְאַחֲרָן לָא אִיתַי, and Aaron is not here. A priest who wears the Urim and Tummim is not here to tell you. He said to them, “That Temple was so powerful, and you advised me to destroy it?” Immediately, he became angry and commanded to slay them.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:11] U-mill'ta... yakkirah (וּמִלְּתָא... יַקִּירָה - And the matter... is difficult): [The word yakkirah means difficult/weighty,] like [the usage in] “And costly/precious (v'yakar - וְיָקַר) is the redemption of their soul” [Psalms 49:9], for which no [sufficient] value/reason can be found (shelo matz'ah taʿam - שֶׁלֹּא מָצְאָה טַעַם). [The parallel illustrates the weightiness or difficulty implied by the root י-ק-ר]. [Daniel 2:11] Elahin (אֱלָהִין - Gods): [The Chaldeans refer to beings who] are the upper angels, who are the form of truth (tzurat emet - צוּרַת אֱמֶת) and are not like bodies [i.e., they are incorporeal]. And the [human] souls are derived (nigzarot - נִגְזָרוֹת, lit. 'cut') from them, like light that goes out from the sun. But the souls are [like] bodies [in that they are embodied or less pure/more limited than the angels]. Therefore, they [human souls] do not know the hidden things except a little, and [primarily] in visions of the night when the souls are not occupied with the needs of the body. And one who knows the secret of the soul will testify to my words.

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־קֳבֵ֣ל דְּנָ֔ה מַלְכָּ֕א בְּנַ֖ס וּקְצַ֣ף שַׂגִּ֑יא וַאֲמַר֙ לְה֣וֹבָדָ֔ה לְכֹ֖ל חַכִּימֵ֥י בָבֶֽל
verses.translation: Whereupon the king flew into a violent rage, and gave an order to do away with all the sages of Babylon.
verses.rashi: In view of this in view of this reply. in great wrath and anger Aram. מַלְכָּא בְּנַס וּקְצַף שַּׂגִיא. The king was in great wrath and anger. (בְּנַס) means in wrath. (Gen. 40:6): “And behold they were angry (זעַפִים), and Onkelos renders: נסִיסִין. and ordered to destroy Aram. וַאֲמַר לְהוֹבָדָה. About this “vav” of וַאֲמַר, Menahem and Dunash disputed. Menahem (p. 46) forced himself to make the “beth” of בְּנַס, a radical and did not interpret בְּנַס וּקְצַף as nouns, but as an expression of a verb: was greatly wroth and angry and ordered to destroy. The “vav” in וַאֲמַר forced him to interpret it this way. Dunash (p. 9) says that it is impossible for the “beth” to be a radical, but it is a noun, and he interpreted it in this manner: with great wrath and anger he ordered to destroy, and the “vav” there is superfluous, like the “vav” of (Ps. 76:7): “chariot (וְרֶכֶב) and horse.” But I say that the “vav” is not forced to be superfluous, but so is its interpretation: מַלְכָּא בְּנַס וּקְצַף שַּׂגִּיא, the king was in great wrath and anger, and he ordered in his wrath to destroy them. Accordingly, this is an elliptical verse.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:12] Kol kovel denah (כָּל־קֳבֵל דְּנָה - Because of this): [Regarding the word] became angry/provoked (benas - בְּנַס) - [this means] "with rage" (b'zaʿaf - בְּזַעַף) [which is its] Aramaic equivalent/translation (Targum), and [it is related to the Hebrew] “and they look troubled/angry” (v'hinam zoʿafim - וְהִנָּם זוֹעֲפִים) [Daniel 1:10]. [Daniel 2:12] chakkimei Vavel (חַכִּימֵי בָבֶל - wise men of Babylon): They are the ones mentioned at the beginning [Daniel 2:2].

verses.hebrew_full: וְדָתָ֣א נֶפְקַ֔ת וְחַכִּֽימַיָּ֖א מִֽתְקַטְּלִ֑ין וּבְע֛וֹ דָּנִיֵּ֥אל וְחַבְר֖וֹהִי לְהִתְקְטָלָֽה
verses.translation: The decree condemning the sages to death was issued. Daniel and his companions were about to be put to death
verses.rashi: And a decree was issued The law of the king and his decree were issued in the province. and Daniel and his colleagues were sought to be slain [as translated,] and Daniel and his colleagues were sought to be slain.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:13] V'data nefqat (וְדָתָא נֶפְקַת - And the decree went out): The decree went out from before the king.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֣יִן דָּנִיֵּ֗אל הֲתִיב֙ עֵטָ֣א וּטְעֵ֔ם לְאַרְי֕וֹךְ רַב־טַבָּחַיָּ֖א דִּ֣י מַלְכָּ֑א דִּ֚י נְפַ֣ק לְקַטָּלָ֔ה לְחַכִּימֵ֖י בָּבֶֽל
verses.translation: when Daniel remonstrated with Arioch, the captain of the royal guard who had set out to put the sages of Babylon to death.
verses.rashi: Then Aram. בֵּאדַיִן, the Targum for אָז. answered with counsel and good sense He answered [with] counsel and good sense to Arioch, the name of the man. the...chief executioner the officer over those who executed the ones condemned to death.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:14] Be'dayin (בֵּאדַיִן - Then): Then (az - אָז) Daniel answered (hetiv - הֵתִיב) with counsel (ʿeta - עֵטָא) and discretion/reason (teʿem - טְעֵם). And behold, [the concept of giving good counsel/reason is] like [in Proverbs 26:16 concerning] “seven men who can render a reason” (shivʿah meshivei taʿam - שִׁבְעָה מְשִׁיבֵי טָעַם). [Regarding] Arioch, the captain of the guards (rav tabbachayya - רַב־טַבָּחַיָּא): [Consider] Potiphar, Pharaoh's captain of the guard (sar ha-tabbachim - שַׂר הַטַּבָּחִים) [Genesis 39:1]. According to my opinion, [the meaning of the title is complex because] the word tabbach (טַבָּח) in the Holy Tongue [Hebrew] is interpreted in two senses/meanings (טעמים): The first: killing/slaughter (harigah - הֲרִיגָה), like [the title] chief of the executioners/slaughterers (rav tabbachim - רַב טַבָּחִים). And the proof [that Arioch's title relates to killing] is [the phrase in this verse] “who had set out to put [them] to death” (di nefaq l'qattala - דִּי נְפַק לְקַטָּלָא). And similar to it [the use of the root ט-ב-ח for killing] is [the phrase] “like a lamb (k'cheves) or a ram (alluf) led (yuval) to the slaughter (litvoach) like sheep (k'tzon)” [cf. Jeremiah 11:19]. And many [other examples] are like this. And the second meaning is like [the word] in the language of Ishmael [Arabic] for cook/butcher (mevashel - מְבַשֵּׁל). Like [in]: “and slaughter a slaughtering (v'tavoach tevach) and prepare (v'hachen)” [Genesis 43:16]. [This second meaning is supported by verses like I Samuel 8:13 referring to women as] “perfumers (l'raqqachot) and cooks/butchers (ul'tabbachot)” – because women do not [ritually] slaughter. And also [the verse] “And the cook (ha-tabbach) took up (vayarem) [the portion]” [I Samuel 9:24]. And because the chief baker baked the bread and the chief cupbearer was his counterpart, therefore I said [in Genesis commentary] that he [Pharaoh] put them [the baker and cupbearer] in the house of their colleague [Potiphar], who was the chief cook (שר המבשלים), and did not put them in the prison where all prisoners were confined, because of their high rank as officials. [This digression clarifies the dual meaning of tabbach relevant to understanding Potiphar and perhaps Arioch].

verses.hebrew_full: עָנֵ֣ה וְאָמַ֗ר לְאַרְיוֹךְ֙ שַׁלִּיטָ֣א דִֽי־מַלְכָּ֔א עַל־מָ֥ה דָתָ֛א מְהַחְצְפָ֖ה מִן־קֳדָ֣ם מַלְכָּ֑א אֱדַ֣יִן מִלְּתָ֔א הוֹדַ֥ע אַרְי֖וֹךְ לְדָנִיֵּֽאל
verses.translation: He spoke up and said to Arioch, the royal officer, “Why is the decree of the king so urgent?” Thereupon Arioch informed Daniel of the matter.
verses.rashi: Why was the decree issued so hastily Why is the decree of the king [issued] in such a haste to kill? Then Arioch made the matter known to Daniel that the king was angry because no one told him his dream.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:15] ʿAneh v'amar (עָנֵה וְאָמַר - Answered and said): The meaning of urgent/harsh (mehachtz'fah - מְהַחְצְפָה) is hastiness/urgency (mehirut - מְהִירוּת). Then (Edayin - אֱדַיִן) Arioch informed Daniel [of the matter], so that he [Daniel] could come and request time for himself. And so he [Daniel] did. And Daniel came and requested from the king, [perhaps framing his need for time with words similar to those] in the Holy Tongue [Hebrew, from Isaiah 21:12]: “If you would inquire, inquire” (im tivʿayun beʿayu - אִם־תִּבְעָיוּן בְּעָיוּ).

verses.hebrew_full: וְדָ֣נִיֵּ֔אל עַ֖ל וּבְעָ֣ה מִן־מַלְכָּ֑א דִּ֚י זְמָ֣ן יִנְתִּן־לֵ֔הּ וּפִשְׁרָ֖א לְהַֽחֲוָיָ֥ה לְמַלְכָּֽא
verses.translation: So Daniel went to ask the king for time, that he might tell the meaning to the king.
verses.rashi: entered and requested [as translated,] entered and requested. be given him [as translated,] be given him.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱדַ֥יִן דָּנִיֵּ֖אל לְבַיְתֵ֣הּ אֲזַ֑ל וְ֠לַחֲנַנְיָ֠ה מִֽישָׁאֵ֧ל וַעֲזַרְיָ֛ה חַבְר֖וֹהִי מִלְּתָ֥א הוֹדַֽע
verses.translation: Then Daniel went to his house and informed his companions, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah, of the matter,
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:17] Edayin Daniel l'vaiteh azal (אֱדַיִן דָּנִיֵּאל לְבַיְתֵהּ אֲזַל - Then Daniel went to his house): [He went directly home] in order not to be occupied with any other matter, [but rather] to be secluded (l'hitboded - לְהִתְבּוֹדֵד) with his companions [to focus solely on this issue].

verses.hebrew_full: וְרַחֲמִ֗ין לְמִבְעֵא֙ מִן־קֳדָם֙ אֱלָ֣הּ שְׁמַיָּ֔א עַל־רָזָ֖א דְּנָ֑ה דִּ֣י לָ֤א יְהֽוֹבְדוּן֙ דָּנִיֵּ֣אל וְחַבְר֔וֹהִי עִם־שְׁאָ֖ר חַכִּימֵ֥י בָבֶֽל
verses.translation: that they might implore the God of Heaven for help regarding this mystery, so that Daniel and his colleagues would not be put to death together with the other sages of Babylon.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:18] V'rachamin... min kodam Elah shemayya (וְרַחֲמִין... מִן־קֳדָם אֱלָהּ שְׁמַיָּא - And [to request] mercy from before the God of Heaven): [They turned to God] because there is no sage on earth who could help (l'hoʿil - לְהוֹעִיל, be beneficial) in this matter.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱדַ֗יִן לְדָנִיֵּ֛אל בְּחֶזְוָ֥א דִֽי־לֵילְיָ֖א רָזָ֣א גְלִ֑י אֱדַ֙יִן֙ דָּֽנִיֵּ֔אל בָּרִ֖ךְ לֶאֱלָ֥הּ שְׁמַיָּֽא
verses.translation: The mystery was revealed to Daniel in a night vision; then Daniel blessed the God of Heaven.
verses.rashi: the secret was revealed [as translated,] the secret was revealed.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:19] Raza (רָזָא - Mystery): This [word] means secret (sod - סוֹד). And [the phrase from Isaiah 24:16] “My secret/woe to me, my secret/woe to me” (razi li, razi li - רָזִי לִי, רָזִי לִי) is not like it [the word raza in Daniel]. Rather, it [razi in Isaiah] is derived from [the root related to] leanness/wasting (razon - רָזוֹן).

verses.hebrew_full: עָנֵ֤ה דָֽנִיֵּאל֙ וְאָמַ֔ר לֶהֱוֵ֨א שְׁמֵ֤הּ דִּֽי־אֱלָהָא֙ מְבָרַ֔ךְ מִן־עָלְמָ֖א וְעַ֣ד עָלְמָ֑א דִּ֧י חׇכְמְתָ֛א וּגְבוּרְתָ֖א דִּ֥י לֵֽהּ־הִֽיא
verses.translation: Daniel spoke up and said:“Let the name of God be blessed forever and ever,For wisdom and power are God’s—
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:20] ʿAneh Daniel v'amar (עָנֵה דָנִיֵּאל וְאָמַר - Answered Daniel and said): And he mentioned wisdom and power (chokhm'ta u'gvur'ta - חָכְמְתָא וּגְבוּרְתָא), because to God alone truly belong (di leh hi - דִּי לֵהּ־הִיא) wisdom and power. And all created beings, above [in the heavens, i.e., angels] and below [on earth], [possess these attributes only] like happenstance/contingently (k'mo mikreh - כְּמוֹ מִקְרֶה), because they [their attributes] are from Him.

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֠ה֠וּא מְהַשְׁנֵ֤א עִדָּנַיָּא֙ וְזִמְנַיָּ֔א מְהַעְדֵּ֥ה מַלְכִ֖ין וּמְהָקֵ֣ים מַלְכִ֑ין יָהֵ֤ב חׇכְמְתָא֙ לְחַכִּימִ֔ין וּמַנְדְּעָ֖א לְיָדְעֵ֥י בִינָֽה
verses.translation: Who changes times and seasons,Removes kings and installs kings;Who gives the wise their wisdomAnd knowledge to those who know;
verses.rashi: changes [as translated,] changes. removes He removes kings, for he understood from the dream that Nebuchadnezzar’s kingdom would terminate, and another kingdom would rise.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:21] V'hu (וְהוּא - And He): According to His wisdom, He changes times (mehasshne ʿiddanayya - מְהַשְׁנֵא עִדָּנַיָּא), removes kings (mehaʿdeh malkhin - מְהַעְדֵּה מַלְכִין), and establishes (umehakem - וּמְהָקֵים) others in their place – and [He does] that which He sees [fit] in His wisdom. And the wisdom that man knows, He has given it.

verses.hebrew_full: ה֛וּא גָּלֵ֥א עַמִּיקָתָ֖א וּמְסַתְּרָתָ֑א יָדַע֙ מָ֣ה בַחֲשׁוֹכָ֔א (ונהירא) וּנְהוֹרָ֖א עִמֵּ֥הּ שְׁרֵֽא
verses.translation: Who reveals deep and hidden things,Knows what is in the darkness—With whom light dwells.
verses.rashi: He reveals the profound [as translated,] He reveals the profound. and secret things for He revealed this profound thing to me.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:22] Hu (הוּא - He): [He] reveals deep (ʿammiqata - עַמִּיקָתָא) and hidden (umesatt'rata - וּמְסַתְּרָתָא) things that man cannot know, when His servants implore Him – like me, [for] He revealed to me the secret of the dream. [Daniel 2:22] Yadaʿ mah vachashokha (יָדַע מָה בַחֲשׁוֹכָא - He knows what is in the darkness): The opposite of man [who does not fully know what is hidden in darkness/potentiality]. And according to my opinion, the meaning is: What has the potential/power (b'choach - בְּכֹחַ) to come into being. Similar to [Job 28:11]: “and hidden things (v'taʿalumah - וְתַעֲלֻמָה) He brings forth to light (yotzi or - יוֹצִיא אוֹר)”.... And the meaning of “And light (un'hora - וּנְהוֹרָא) dwells (sh're - שְׁרֵא) with Him”: Everything is before Him as if [already] done/actualized (k'maʿaseh - כְּמַעֲשֶׂה).... And the word sh're (שְׁרֵא - dwells) is like [the Hebrew] choneh (חוֹנֶה - dwells/encamps).... And [the word] n'hora (נְהוֹרָא - light) is like [the Hebrew word in Job 3:4]: “and let no light (n'harah - נְהָרָה) shine upon it”. And similarly [related conceptually or etymologically is the word] ha-minharot (הַמִּנְהָרוֹת - the tunnels/burrows) [Judges 6:2].

verses.hebrew_full: לָ֣ךְ אֱלָ֣הּ אֲבָהָתִ֗י מְהוֹדֵ֤א וּמְשַׁבַּח֙ אֲנָ֔ה דִּ֧י חׇכְמְתָ֛א וּגְבוּרְתָ֖א יְהַ֣בְתְּ לִ֑י וּכְעַ֤ן הֽוֹדַעְתַּ֙נִי֙ דִּֽי־בְעֵ֣ינָא מִנָּ֔ךְ דִּֽי־מִלַּ֥ת מַלְכָּ֖א הוֹדַעְתֶּֽנָא
verses.translation: I acknowledge and praise You,O God of my ancestors,You who have given me wisdom and power,For now You have let me know what we asked of You;You have let us know what concerns the king.”
verses.rashi: You have let me know [as translated,] You have let me know.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:23] Lakh Elah avahati (לָךְ אֱלָהּ אֲבָהָתִי - To You, O God of my fathers): [Daniel addresses God specifically as God of his fathers] because my fathers were prophets, unlike the fathers of other nations. [And as this God,] You who have given me wisdom and power superior to all [other] wise men. And he said: “You have let me know” (hodaʿtani - הוֹדַעְתַּנִי): [Using the singular] because the secret became known to him alone.... And he said: “what we asked” (di veʿeina minnakh - דִּי בְעֵינָא מִנָּךְ): [Using the plural, referring to] himself and his companions. As if saying: "Not by my merit alone did You make this secret known to me" [acknowledging their joint prayer].... And he said: “You have let us know” (hodaʿtana - הוֹדַעְתֶּנָא): [Using the plural again] because he [Daniel] informed them [his companions of the revealed secret].

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־קֳבֵ֣ל דְּנָ֗ה דָּֽנִיֵּאל֙ עַ֣ל עַל־אַרְי֔וֹךְ דִּ֚י מַנִּ֣י מַלְכָּ֔א לְהוֹבָדָ֖ה לְחַכִּימֵ֣י בָבֶ֑ל אֲזַ֣ל וְכֵ֣ן אֲמַר־לֵ֗הּ לְחַכִּימֵ֤י בָבֶל֙ אַל־תְּהוֹבֵ֔ד הַעֵ֙לְנִי֙ קֳדָ֣ם מַלְכָּ֔א וּפִשְׁרָ֖א לְמַלְכָּ֥א אֲחַוֵּֽא
verses.translation: Thereupon Daniel went to Arioch, whom the king had appointed to do away with the sages of Babylon; he came and said to him as follows, “Do not do away with the sages of Babylon; bring me to the king and I will tell the king the meaning!”
verses.rashi: In view of this in view of this, that the secret was revealed to him. Daniel came in to Arioch He entered Arioch’s premises. had appointed [as translated,] had appointed. Do not destroy [as translated,] Do not destroy. bring me in [as translated,] bring me in.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:24] Kol kovel denah (כָּל־קֳבֵל דְּנָה - Because of this): And he [Daniel] said: “and the interpretation (u'fishra - וּפִשְׁרָא) [I will tell the king]” – [This is stated] in a concise manner (derekh ketzarah - דֶּרֶךְ קְצָרָה). Because it is not possible to interpret except after one makes known the dream. [Therefore, saying "I will tell the interpretation" implicitly includes telling the dream itself first].

verses.hebrew_full: אֱדַ֤יִן אַרְיוֹךְ֙ בְּהִתְבְּהָלָ֔ה הַנְעֵ֥ל לְדָנִיֵּ֖אל קֳדָ֣ם מַלְכָּ֑א וְכֵ֣ן אֲמַר־לֵ֗הּ דִּֽי־הַשְׁכַּ֤חַת גְּבַר֙ מִן־בְּנֵ֤י גָֽלוּתָא֙ דִּ֣י יְה֔וּד דִּ֥י פִשְׁרָ֖א לְמַלְכָּ֥א יְהוֹדַֽע
verses.translation: So Arioch rushed Daniel into the king’s presence and said to him, “I have found among the exiles of Judah a man who can make the meaning known to the king!”
verses.rashi: Then Aram. אֶדַיִן, [the equivalent of] אָז. in haste Aram. בְּהִתְבְּהָלָה, in haste, i.e., quickly. brought... in brought Daniel in. that “I have found a man [as translated,] that “I have found a man. the exiles of Judea [as translated,] the exiles of Judea.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:25] Edayin Aryokh (אֱדַיִן אַרְיוֹךְ - Then Arioch): [Regarding the word Arioch says] Hashkachat (הַשְׁכַּחַת - I have found): [Means] I have found (matzati - מָצָאתִי). And there is no equivalent [form like hashkachat] in the Holy Tongue [Hebrew]. And the one who says that “and they were forgotten (v'yishtak'chu - וְיִשְׁתַּכְּחוּ) in the city” [Ecclesiastes 8:10] is like it (hashkachat), is not correct.

words.eng: Judah-the territory of the tribe of Judah

verses.hebrew_full: עָנֵ֤ה מַלְכָּא֙ וְאָמַ֣ר לְדָנִיֵּ֔אל דִּ֥י שְׁמֵ֖הּ בֵּלְטְשַׁאצַּ֑ר (האיתיך) הַֽאִיתָ֣ךְ כָּהֵ֗ל לְהוֹדָעֻתַ֛נִי חֶלְמָ֥א דִֽי־חֲזֵ֖ית וּפִשְׁרֵֽהּ
verses.translation: The king said in reply to Daniel (who was called Belteshazzar), “Can you really make known to me the dream that I saw and its meaning?”
verses.rashi: Do you have the ability Are you able? that I have seen [as translated,] that I have seen.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:26] ʿAneh malka v'amar (עָנֵה מַלְכָּא וְאָמַר - The king answered and said): [Regarding the phrase] Are you (ha'itakh - הַֽאִיתָךְ) able (kahel - כָּהֵל)? [This means:] Are you able? (hayeshkha yachol - הֲיֶשְׁךָ יָכוֹל).

verses.hebrew_full: עָנֵ֧ה דָנִיֵּ֛אל קֳדָ֥ם מַלְכָּ֖א וְאָמַ֑ר רָזָא֙ דִּֽי־מַלְכָּ֣א שָׁאֵ֔ל לָ֧א חַכִּימִ֣ין אָֽשְׁפִ֗ין חַרְטֻמִּין֙ גָּזְרִ֔ין יָכְלִ֖ין לְהַֽחֲוָיָ֥ה לְמַלְכָּֽא
verses.translation: Daniel answered the king and said, “The mystery about which the king has inquired—sages, exorcists, magicians, and diviners cannot tell to the king.
verses.rashi: demonologists Aram. גָזְרִין, the name of a type of ruler over demons.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:27] ʿAneh Daniel (עָנֵה דָנִיֵּאל - Answered Daniel): [Regarding the word] wise men (chakkimin - חַכִּימִין): [This is] an encompassing term (millah kolelet - מִלָּה כּוֹלֶלֶת) [which includes] the sorcerers (ha-mekhashshefim - הַמְּכַשְּׁפִים) and the Chaldeans (v'ha-Kasdim - וְהַכַּשְׂדִּים) [mentioned previously, e.g., in Daniel 2:2].

verses.hebrew_full: בְּרַ֡ם אִיתַ֞י אֱלָ֤הּ בִּשְׁמַיָּא֙ גָּלֵ֣א רָזִ֔ין וְהוֹדַ֗ע לְמַלְכָּא֙ נְבֽוּכַדְנֶצַּ֔ר מָ֛ה דִּ֥י לֶהֱוֵ֖א בְּאַחֲרִ֣ית יוֹמַיָּ֑א חֶלְמָ֨ךְ וְחֶזְוֵ֥י רֵאשָׁ֛ךְ עַֽל־מִשְׁכְּבָ֖ךְ דְּנָ֥ה הֽוּא
verses.translation: But there is a God in heaven who reveals mysteries, who has made known to King Nebuchadnezzar what is to be at the end of days. This is your dream and the vision that entered your mind in bed:
verses.rashi: But there is...and He lets Nebuchadnezzar know He revealed this secret to me, and through me let the king know that his dream refers to future events. your dream and the visions of your head on your bed which are on your bed.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:28] Baram itai Elah bishmayya (בְּרַם אִיתַי אֱלָהּ בִּשְׁמַיָּא - But there is a God in heaven): The [Chaldean] wise men said to him [the king, in Daniel 2:11]: “gods” (Elahin - אֱלָהִין - plural). But Daniel specified the one God (Elah chad - אֱלָה חַד - 'God, one'). [Ibn Ezra highlights the contrast between the polytheistic reference of the Chaldeans and Daniel's monotheistic declaration].

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: (אנתה) אַ֣נְתְּ מַלְכָּ֗א רַעְיוֹנָךְ֙ עַל־מִשְׁכְּבָ֣ךְ סְלִ֔קוּ מָ֛ה דִּ֥י לֶהֱוֵ֖א אַחֲרֵ֣י דְנָ֑ה וְגָלֵ֧א רָזַיָּ֛א הוֹדְעָ֖ךְ מָה־דִ֥י לֶהֱוֵֽא
verses.translation: O king, the thoughts that came to your mind in your bed are about future events; the Revealer of Mysteries has let you know what is to happen.
verses.rashi: your thoughts Aram. רַעְיוֹנָיךְ. came while on your bed What you were thinking of during the day, what would be after you, came [to you] on your bed. and the Revealer of secrets and the Holy One, blessed be He, Who reveals secrets, let you know what will come about.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:29] Ant malka (אַנְתְּ מַלְכָּא - You, O king): He [Daniel] mentioned what came up upon his [the king's] mind (mah sheʿalah ʿal libbo - מַה שֶׁעָלָה עַל לִבּוֹ) while lying down (bishokhvo - בְּשָׁכְבוֹ), that he [the king] thought about what would be after this (mah sheyihyeh acharav - מַה שֶׁיִּהְיֶה אַחֲרָיו). [Ibn Ezra clarifies that Daniel is referring back to the king's own musings about the future, mentioned in the verse].

verses.hebrew_full: וַאֲנָ֗ה לָ֤א בְחׇכְמָה֙ דִּֽי־אִיתַ֥י בִּי֙ מִן־כׇּל־חַיַּיָּ֔א רָזָ֥א דְנָ֖ה גֱּלִ֣י לִ֑י לָהֵ֗ן עַל־דִּבְרַת֙ דִּ֤י פִשְׁרָא֙ לְמַלְכָּ֣א יְהוֹדְע֔וּן וְרַעְיוֹנֵ֥י לִבְבָ֖ךְ תִּנְדַּֽע
verses.translation: Not because my wisdom is greater than that of other creatures has this mystery been revealed to me, but in order that the meaning should be made known to the king, and that you may know the thoughts of your mind.
verses.rashi: And I, not with wisdom And I, not with superior wisdom that I have, more than other creatures, was this secret revealed to me. but in order that they should let the king know the interpretation They should let you know from heaven. and you should know the thoughts of your heart and the thoughts of your heart about which you are constantly thinking, [which are] to know who will rise after you. you should know [as translated,] you should know.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:30] Va'anah (וַאֲנָה - And I): [Daniel speaks] in the way of humility (derekh ʿanavah - דֶּרֶךְ עֲנָוָה). [Saying effectively:] For there is no wisdom in me that I should be able to know this secret [through my own abilities]. Rather, the secret was revealed to me for the sake of your [the king's] honor (baʿavur k'vodkha - בַּעֲבוּר כְּבוֹדְךָ), [so] that you should know the interpretation (pitron - פִּתְרוֹן) of your dream, and that you should know the thoughts of your heart (raʿyonei levavkha - רַעְיוֹנֵי לְבָבָךְ)—which you thought at the beginning of the night. And the word “they should make known” (yehodoʿun - יְהוֹדְעוּן): [This plural verb] refers back to the servants of the Glorious Name (meshartei ha-Shem ha-nikhbad - מְשָׁרְתֵי הַשֵּׁם הַנִּכְבָּד) [i.e., the angels or heavenly agents, indicating the interpretation comes through divine means, not from Daniel alone].

verses.hebrew_full: (אנתה) אַ֣נְתְּ מַלְכָּ֗א חָזֵ֤ה הֲוַ֙יְתָ֙ וַאֲל֨וּ צְלֵ֥ם חַד֙ שַׂגִּ֔יא צַלְמָ֨א דִכֵּ֥ן רַ֛ב וְזִיוֵ֥הּ יַתִּ֖יר קָאֵ֣ם לְקׇבְלָ֑ךְ וְרֵוֵ֖הּ דְּחִֽיל
verses.translation: “O king, as you looked on, there appeared a great statue. This statue, which was huge and its brightness surpassing, stood before you, and its appearance was awesome.
verses.rashi: you were watching [as translated,] you were watching. and behold Aram. וַאֲלוּ, an expression of “behold.” an image which had a large base [as translated,] an image which had a large base. with unusual splendor, was standing opposite you [as translated,] standing opposite you. and its form was frightening [as translated,] and its form was frightening.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:31] Ant malka chazeh havaita va'alu (אַנְתְּ מַלְכָּא חָזֵה הֲוַיְתָ וַאֲלוּ - You, O king, were looking, and behold): And behold (va'alu - וַאֲלוּ): [Means:] And behold (v'hinneh - וְהִנֵּה), like elu (כְּאִלּוּ - like these/behold). [Daniel 2:31] tzalma dikken (צַלְמָא דִכֵּן - that statue): [Means:] This statue (tzelem zeh - צֶלֶם זֶה).... [Daniel 2:31] rav (רַב - huge): [Means:] Great (gadol - גָּדוֹל).... [Daniel 2:31] v'ziveih yattir (וְזִיוֵהּ יַתִּיר - and its brightness surpassing): Perhaps similar to it [the word Ziv] is [the name of the month] “in the month of Ziv” (b'chodesh Ziv - בְּחֹדֶשׁ זִו) [I Kings 6:1, I Kings 6:37].... [Daniel 2:31] v'reiveih d'chil (וְרֵוֵהּ דְּחִיל - and its appearance awesome): [Means:] And its appearance was fearsome/awesome (u'mar'ehu nora - וּמַרְאֵהוּ נוֹרָא).

verses.hebrew_full: ה֣וּא צַלְמָ֗א רֵאשֵׁהּ֙ דִּֽי־דְהַ֣ב טָ֔ב חֲד֥וֹהִי וּדְרָע֖וֹהִי דִּ֣י כְסַ֑ף מְע֥וֹהִי וְיַרְכָתֵ֖הּ דִּ֥י נְחָֽשׁ
verses.translation: The head of that statue was of fine gold; its breast and arms were of silver; its belly and thighs, of bronze;
