verses.rashi: of fine gold [as translated,] of fine gold. its breast [as translated,] its breast. and its arms [as translated,] and its arms. its belly and thighs [as translated,] its belly and thighs.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:32] Hu tzalma (הוּא צַלְמָא - That statue): [Identifying the body parts mentioned:] Its head of fine gold; Its breast (chadohi - חֲדוֹהִי) and arms (ud'raʿohi - וּדְרָעוֹהִי) of silver [are] the breast (ha-chazeh - הֶחָזֶה) and its arms (uz'roʿotav - וּזְרֹעוֹתָיו); its belly (meʿohi - מְעוֹהִי) and thighs (v'yarkhateh - וְיַרְכָתֵהּ) of bronze [are] its belly (bitno - בִּטְנוֹ) and its thighs (viyrichav - וִירֵכָיו).

verses.hebrew_full: שָׁק֖וֹהִי דִּ֣י פַרְזֶ֑ל רַגְל֕וֹהִי (מנהון) מִנְּהֵין֙ דִּ֣י פַרְזֶ֔ל (ומנהון) וּמִנְּהֵ֖ין דִּ֥י חֲסַֽף
verses.translation: its legs were of iron, and its feet part iron and part clay.
verses.rashi: Its legs were of iron [as translated,] Its legs were of iron. were partly of iron and partly of clay [as translated,] were partly of iron and partly of clay [i.e., part of each foot was of iron and part of clay].
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:33] Shaqohi (שָׁקֹהִי - Its legs): [Means] its shins (shoqav - שׁוֹקָיו) [were of] iron (barzel - בַּרְזֶל). Its feet (Raglohi - רַגְלוֹהִי) - part of them (yesh mehem - יֵשׁ מֵהֶם) [was] iron (barzel), and part of them (v'yesh mehem - וְיֵשׁ מֵהֶם) [was] clay (cheres - חֶרֶס).

verses.hebrew_full: חָזֵ֣ה הֲוַ֗יְתָ עַ֠ד דִּ֣י הִתְגְּזֶ֤רֶת אֶ֙בֶן֙ דִּי־לָ֣א בִידַ֔יִן וּמְחָ֤ת לְצַלְמָא֙ עַל־רַגְל֔וֹהִי דִּ֥י פַרְזְלָ֖א וְחַסְפָּ֑א וְהַדֵּ֖קֶת הִמּֽוֹן
verses.translation: As you looked on, a stone was hewn out, not by hands, and struck the statue on its feet of iron and clay and crushed them.
verses.rashi: until one stone was hewn that one stone was cut and separated. without hands not with hands, but by itself. and it struck the image [as translated,] and it struck the image. and crumbled them [as translated,] and crumbled them.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:34] Chazeh havaita... hitg'zeret even di la viyadayin (חָזֵה הֲוַיְתָ עַד דִּי הִתְגְּזֶרֶת אֶבֶן דִּי־לָא בִידַיִן - As you looked, a stone was hewn out, but not by hands): Was hewn out (hitg'zeret - הִתְגְּזֶרֶת): [From the root meaning 'to cut'], like “To Him who divided (l'gozer - לְגֹזֵר) the Sea of Reeds into parts (ligzarim - לִגְזָרִים)” [Psalms 136:13]. [And like] “I said... ‘I am cut off (nigzarti - נִגְזַרְתִּי)’” [Lamentations 3:54]. [Daniel 2:34] Umchat l'tzalma (וּמְחָת לְצַלְמָא - And struck the statue): [Means] And it struck (v'hikk'tah) the statue (ba-tzelem).

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֣יִן דָּ֣קוּ כַחֲדָ֡ה פַּרְזְלָא֩ חַסְפָּ֨א נְחָשָׁ֜א כַּסְפָּ֣א וְדַהֲבָ֗א וַהֲווֹ֙ כְּע֣וּר מִן־אִדְּרֵי־קַ֔יִט וּנְשָׂ֤א הִמּוֹן֙ רוּחָ֔א וְכׇל־אֲתַ֖ר לָא־הִשְׁתְּכַ֣ח לְה֑וֹן וְאַבְנָ֣א דִּֽי־מְחָ֣ת לְצַלְמָ֗א הֲוָ֛ת לְט֥וּר רַ֖ב וּמְלָ֥את כׇּל־אַרְעָֽא
verses.translation: All at once, the iron, clay, bronze, silver, and gold were crushed, and became like chaff of the threshing floors of summer; a wind carried them off until no trace of them was left. But the stone that struck the statue became a great mountain and filled the whole earth.
verses.rashi: Then...crumbled together Then all the types of metals and the clay that were in it crumbled together. and they were like chaff from the threshing floors of the summer [as translated,] and they were like chaff from the threshing floors of the summer. and the wind carried them off [as translated,] and the wind carried them off. and no place was found for them i.e., their original place was unknown, and it was unrecognizable that they were ever there. became a huge mountain [as translated,] became a huge mountain.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:35] Be'dayin daku kachdah (בֵּאדַיִן דָּקוּ כַחֲדָה - Then they were crushed together): Together (Kachdah - כַחֲדָה): [Means] together (yachdav - יַחְדָּיו). [Daniel 2:35] k'ʿur min idrei qayit (כְּעוּר מִן־אִדְּרֵי־קַיִט - like chaff from the threshing floors of summer): [Means] like chaff (k'motz) that is driven (y'soʿer) from the threshing floor (min goren) in the days of summer (bimei ha-qayitz).... [Daniel 2:35] v'chol atar la hishtakach lehon (וְכָל־אֲתַר לָא־הִשְׁתֲּכַח לְהוֹן - and any place/trace was not found for them): Place (atar - אֲתַר): [Means] place (maqom - מָקוֹם).... [Daniel 2:35] havat l'tur rav (הֲוָת לְטוּר רַב - became a great mountain): To a great mountain (l'tur rav - לְטוּר רַב): [Means] to a great mountain (l'har gadol - לְהַר גָּדוֹל). And filled the whole earth.

verses.hebrew_full: דְּנָ֣ה חֶלְמָ֔א וּפִשְׁרֵ֖הּ נֵאמַ֥ר קֳדָם־מַלְכָּֽא
verses.translation: “Such was the dream, and we will now tell the king its meaning.
verses.rashi: This is the dream Behold this is the dream that you dreamed. and its interpretation we shall recite, etc. and its interpretation we shall tell you.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:36] Denah chelma (דְּנָה חֶלְמָא - Such is the dream): This is the dream. [Daniel 2:36] u'fishreh neimar (וּפִשְׁרֵהּ נֵאמַר - and its interpretation we will tell): And its interpretation – I am able [okhal (אֹכַל) / ukhal (אוּכַל)] and [divine] Wisdom [is able] to interpret it. Therefore, the plural form [“we will tell”] is used. And this is the way of propriety/humility (derekh musar - דֶּרֶךְ מוּסָר) [i.e., Daniel attributes the ability not only to himself but primarily to the divine wisdom granted to him].

verses.hebrew_full: (אנתה) אַ֣נְתְּ מַלְכָּ֔א מֶ֖לֶךְ מַלְכַיָּ֑א דִּ֚י אֱלָ֣הּ שְׁמַיָּ֔א מַלְכוּתָ֥א חִסְנָ֛א וְתׇקְפָּ֥א וִֽיקָרָ֖א יְהַב־לָֽךְ
verses.translation: You, O king—king of kings, to whom the God of Heaven has given kingdom, power, might, and glory;
verses.rashi: The King of kings Our Sages, of blessed memory, explained (Shev. 35b): Every mention of “king” in Daniel refers to an earthly king, except this one, which he said in reference to the Holy One, blessed be He, and this is what it means: The King of kings, Who is the God of heaven. a strong... kingdom gave you a strong and prominent kingdom.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:37] Ant malka... chisna (אַנְתְּ מַלְכָּא... חִסְנָא - You, O king... power): Power (Chisna - חִסְנָא): [This Aramaic word for power/strength] is like [its Hebrew cognate chason in the verse] “And the strong (ha-chason) shall become tow” [Isaiah 1:31]. And the Gaon [Rav Saadia Gaon] said: [It means] kingdom as an inheritance/possession (malchut nachalah - מַלְכוּת נַחֲלָה).

verses.hebrew_full: וּבְכׇל־דִּ֣י (דארין) דָֽיְרִ֣ין בְּֽנֵי־אֲ֠נָשָׁ֠א חֵיוַ֨ת בָּרָ֤א וְעוֹף־שְׁמַיָּא֙ יְהַ֣ב בִּידָ֔ךְ וְהַשְׁלְטָ֖ךְ בְּכׇלְּה֑וֹן (אנתה) אַ֨נְתְּ־ה֔וּא רֵאשָׁ֖ה דִּ֥י דַהֲבָֽא
verses.translation: into whose hands have been given humans, wild animals, and the fowl of heaven, wherever they may dwell; and to whom has been given dominion over them all—you are the head of gold.
verses.rashi: and has given you dominion over them all And He gave you dominion over them all, so that even if you decree upon a horse, it will not whinny, or upon a bird, it will not fly, as it is said (Jer. 27: 6): “and even the beasts of the field I have given to him to serve him.” You are the head of gold You are the golden head of the image that you saw, for your kingdom is strong, and now it is in existence and is very prominent.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:38] uv'chol di dayerin (וּבְכָל דִּי דָיְרִין - And wherever dwell): [The word dayerin, "dwell"] is like [the Hebrew word mador from the same root, meaning "to dwell," as in the verse]: “than to dwell (mid-dur) in the tents of wickedness” [Psalms 84:11].

verses.hebrew_full: וּבָתְרָ֗ךְ תְּק֛וּם מַלְכ֥וּ אׇחֳרִ֖י (ארעא) אֲרַ֣ע מִנָּ֑ךְ וּמַלְכ֨וּ (תליתיא) תְלִיתָאָ֤ה אׇחֳרִי֙ דִּ֣י נְחָשָׁ֔א דִּ֥י תִשְׁלַ֖ט בְּכׇל־אַרְעָֽא
verses.translation: But another kingdom will arise after you, inferior to yours; then yet a third kingdom, of bronze, which will rule over the whole earth.
verses.rashi: And after you will arise another kingdom lower than you And after you, after the reign of your son, Belshazzar, will arise a kingdom that will take the ruling power from your seed, lower and humbler than your kingdom. lower Aram. אֶרַע, lower, as silver is lower and humbler than gold, and you saw that the breast, which is after the head, was of silver; so will the kingdom of Media and Persia, which will follow the kingdom of Babylon, be humbler than the kingdom of Nebuchadnezzar. and another, a third kingdom [as translated,] another. of copper as strong as copper, and that is the kingdom of Alexander of Macedon.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:39] Uvatrekh (וּבָתְרָךְ - And after you): And after you an inferior kingdom will arise. [The word for inferior is] araʿ (אֲרַע), like the Targum’s rendering [of the concept as] ‘lower’ (tachtayim - תַּחְתַּיִים). And because of it [this sense of lowness inherent in the root], the earth is called arʿa (אַרְעָא) [in Aramaic], for it is beneath everything. The Gaon Rav Saadia—and all the commentators relied upon him—said: That the gold is the kingdom of Babylon: Nebuchadnezzar, his son Evil-merodach, and Belshazzar his grandson. With him [Belshazzar], the kingdom of the Chaldeans ceased. And so it is written concerning him: “That very night Belshazzar the Chaldean king was slain (b'he b'leilya q'til Belshe'tztzar malka). And Darius the Mede received the kingdom (v'Daryavesh Mada'ah qabbel malkhuta)” [Daniel 5:30-6:1]. And this view is correct, for Scripture says: “And all the nations shall serve him, and his son, and his son’s son” [Jeremiah 27:7]. And it is also written: “and they were servants to him and to his sons” [II Chronicles 36:20] – [the referenced ‘sons’ being] Evil-merodach and Belshazzar his son [i.e., grandson]. For a grandson is [sometimes] called a son, like “to Reuel their father” [Exodus 2:18], for he [Reuel] is Hobab, Moses' father-in-law [cf. Numbers 10:29; Judges 4:11 identify Hobab as father-in-law, traditionally equated with Reuel/Jethro]. And it is written: “And it came to pass in the thirty-seventh year of the exile of Jehoiachin... Evil-merodach king of Babylon, in the year that he began to reign, lifted up the head of Jehoiachin...” [II Kings 25:27 / Jeremiah 52:31]. And behold, he [Evil-merodach] is between the reign of Nebuchadnezzar and the reign of Belshazzar. [Daniel 2:39] [The Silver]: [represents] the kingdom of Media and Persia. I will explain further [later], for there is great confusion in their count/chronology. [Daniel 2:39] [The Bronze]: is the kingdom of Greece (Yavan - יָוָן), and their beginning was Alexander of Macedon, as Daniel will explain in the words of his [later] prophecies [cf. Daniel 8]. And the Gaon [Saadia] said that the fourth [kingdom, the iron] is the kingdom of Aram [=Rome/Byzantium]. And [he further interpreted that the element of] clay (cheres - חֶרֶס) [mixed with the iron, Daniel 2:41-43] is the kingdom of Ishmael [Islam]. And furthermore, he [Saadia] said in his book: ‘Do not be astonished that he compared the kingdom of Ishmael to clay. Behold, it is not [inherently] inferior to the aforementioned kingdoms; it is merely the way of one arranging his vessels: first he shows the gold, then the silver, then the bronze, then the iron, and lastly, earthenware.’ But these [Saadia's words justifying clay = Ishmael] are words of emptiness and void (tohu va-vohu - תֹּהוּ וָבֹהוּ)! For there is no correct [descending] order according to his arrangement [if clay doesn't mean weaker]. The proof [that the order implies value/strength] is that Daniel said of the kingdom of Persia “inferi...

words.eng: to be or become king or queen

verses.hebrew_full: וּמַלְכוּ֙ (רביעיה) רְבִ֣יעָאָ֔ה תֶּהֱוֵ֥א תַקִּיפָ֖ה כְּפַרְזְלָ֑א כׇּל־קֳבֵ֗ל דִּ֤י פַרְזְלָא֙ מְהַדֵּ֤ק וְחָשֵׁל֙ כֹּ֔לָּא וּֽכְפַרְזְלָ֛א דִּֽי־מְרָעַ֥ע כׇּל־אִלֵּ֖ן תַּדִּ֥ק וְתֵרֹֽעַ
verses.translation: But the fourth kingdom will be as strong as iron; just as iron crushes and shatters everything—and like iron that smashes—so will it crush and smash all these.
verses.rashi: And a fourth kingdom will be as hard as iron, as you saw the thighs, which are the fourth from the head, the breast, and the belly, and they are of iron. crumbles and flattens everything flattens and crumbles all kinds of metal, which are flattened with a hammer. and flattens Aram. וְחָשֵּׁל, and flattens. There are many such [words] in the Talmud. Keth. 77a, “who thins copper for kettles (חַשְּׁלָא דוּדֵי)”; Hul. 93a: “crushed testicles (בֵּיעֵי חֳשִּׁלָתָא).” all these all these kinds of metals that you saw in the image. it will crumble and shatter It will crumble and shatter all the nations.

verses.hebrew_full: וְדִֽי־חֲזַ֜יְתָה רַגְלַיָּ֣א וְאֶצְבְּעָתָ֗א (מנהון) מִנְּהֵ֞ן חֲסַ֤ף דִּֽי־פֶחָר֙ (ומנהון) וּמִנְּהֵ֣ין פַּרְזֶ֔ל מַלְכ֤וּ פְלִיגָה֙ תֶּהֱוֵ֔ה וּמִן־נִצְבְּתָ֥א דִֽי־פַרְזְלָ֖א לֶֽהֱוֵא־בַ֑הּ כׇּל־קֳבֵל֙ דִּ֣י חֲזַ֔יְתָה פַּ֨רְזְלָ֔א מְעָרַ֖ב בַּחֲסַ֥ף טִינָֽא
verses.translation: You saw the feet and the toes, part potter’s clay and part iron; that means it will be a divided kingdom; it will have only some of the stability of iron, inasmuch as you saw iron mixed with common clay.
verses.rashi: potter’s clay Aram. חֲסַף דִּי פֶחָר, a potter’s clay, and there are many instances of this in the Talmud (Taanith 7a): “vessels of a potter (מָאנֵי דְפַחְרָא).” it will be a divided kingdom It will be a divided kingdom; two kings will rise from it at once, strong and weak, as explained below (verse 42): “part of the kingdom will be strong.” and in it will be some of the strength of iron Even the weak one will be stronger than the other nations through the strength of his fellow, for they will fear him. And this is the meaning of וּמִן נִצְבְּתָא דִּי פַרְזְלָא לֶהֶוֵא בָהּ ; some of the strength of the stability of iron will be in the weak king. in view of what you saw in view of what you saw—iron mixed with clay.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:41] v'di chazaita... chasaf di pachar (וְדִי חֲזַיְתָה... חֲסַף דִּי פֶחָר - And whereas you saw... potter's clay): [Means] an earthenware vessel of the potter (k'li cheres ha-yotzer - כְּלִי חֶרֶס הַיּוֹצֵר). And behold, because of this [mixture with weak clay], it explains: “it will be a divided kingdom” (malchu feliga - מַלְכוּ פְלִיגָא). [Daniel 2:41] Nitzbeta (נִצְבְּתָא - stability): [Means] like strength/power (koach - כֹּחַ).... [Daniel 2:41] meʿarab (מְעָרַב - mixed): [This Aramaic word for "mixed"] is like [the Hebrew cognate in]: “And they mingled (vayitʿarvu - וַיִּתְעָרְבוּ) among the nations” [Psalms 106:35].

words.eng: to be or become king or queen

verses.hebrew_full: וְאֶצְבְּעָת֙ רַגְלַיָּ֔א (מנהון) מִנְּהֵ֥ין פַּרְזֶ֖ל (ומנהון) וּמִנְּהֵ֣ין חֲסַ֑ף מִן־קְצָ֤ת מַלְכוּתָא֙ תֶּהֱוֵ֣ה תַקִּיפָ֔ה וּמִנַּ֖הּ תֶּהֱוֵ֥א תְבִירָֽה
verses.translation: And the toes were part iron and part clay; that [means] the kingdom will be in part strong and in part brittle.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:42] V'etzbeʿat (וְאֶצְבְּעָת - And the toes): These are the princes (sarei - שָׂרֵי) of Ishmael and the kings in the East and in the West.

verses.hebrew_full: (די) וְדִ֣י חֲזַ֗יְתָ פַּרְזְלָא֙ מְעָרַב֙ בַּחֲסַ֣ף טִינָ֔א מִתְעָרְבִ֤ין לֶהֱוֺן֙ בִּזְרַ֣ע אֲנָשָׁ֔א וְלָֽא־לֶהֱוֺ֥ן דָּבְקִ֖ין דְּנָ֣ה עִם־דְּנָ֑ה הֵֽא־כְדִ֣י פַרְזְלָ֔א לָ֥א מִתְעָרַ֖ב עִם־חַסְפָּֽא
verses.translation: You saw iron mixed with common clay; that means: they shall intermingle through marriage, but shall not hold together, just as iron does not mix with clay.
verses.rashi: that they will mingle with the seed of men They will intermarry with the other nations but they will not be at peace and truly cleave to them wholeheartedly, and their laws will differ from the laws of the other nations. as iron does not mix with clay Is it not just as iron does not stick well to clay?
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:43] di chazaita (דִּי חֲזַיְתָ - Whereas you saw): The meaning of “they shall intermingle” (mitʿarvin - מִתְעָרְבִין) is that all of them [the various peoples within the kingdom] take daughters of [other] nations [i.e., intermarry]. For example, the Persians take Babylonian women, and the Sabaeans take Egyptian women. Yet they [these diverse groups] are not similar one to another in their physical form/makeup (matkunetam - מַתְכֻּנְתָּם), in their clothing, their food, or their customs. Furthermore, there is division/disagreement in their religion/law (toratam - תּוֹרָתָם). This is even more so concerning the sons of Ham [peoples associated with Africa] who are toward the southwest, across the sea [the Mediterranean Sea], and are adjacent to the Great Sea which is called Oceanus [the Atlantic Ocean].

verses.hebrew_full: וּֽבְיוֹמֵיה֞וֹן דִּ֧י מַלְכַיָּ֣א אִנּ֗וּן יְקִים֩ אֱלָ֨הּ שְׁמַיָּ֤א מַלְכוּ֙ דִּ֤י לְעָלְמִין֙ לָ֣א תִתְחַבַּ֔ל וּמַ֨לְכוּתָ֔ה לְעַ֥ם אׇחֳרָ֖ן לָ֣א תִשְׁתְּבִ֑ק תַּדִּ֤ק וְתָסֵיף֙ כׇּל־אִלֵּ֣ין מַלְכְוָתָ֔א וְהִ֖יא תְּק֥וּם לְעָלְמַיָּֽא
verses.translation: And in the time of those kings, the God of Heaven will establish a kingdom that shall never be destroyed, a kingdom that shall not be transferred to another people. It will crush and wipe out all these kingdoms, but shall itself last forever—
verses.rashi: And in the days of these kings in the days of these kings, when the kingdom of Rome is still in existence. the God Of heaven will set up a kingdom The kingdom of the Holy One, blessed be He, which will never be destroyed, is the kingdom of the Messiah. it will crumble and destroy It will crumble and destroy all these kingdoms.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:44] Uv'yomeihon di malkhayya illein (וּֽבְיוֹמֵיה֞וֹן דִּי מַלְכַיָּא אִלֵּין - And in the days of those kings): [This refers to the time of the establishment of] the kingdom of the Messiah (malchut ha-mashiach - מַלְכוּת הַמָּשִׁיחַ). [Daniel 2:44] la tishtevik (לָ֣א תִשְׁתְּבִ֔ק - shall not be transferred [lit. left]): It shall not be forsaken (lo teʿazev - לֹא תֵעָזֵב) nor shall it be left behind/remain [for another] (lo tisha'er - לֹא תִשָּׁאֵר).... [Daniel 2:45] Kol kovel di chazaita (כָּל־קֳבֵל דִּי חֲזַיְתָ - Just as you saw): And the meaning of “that not by hands” (di la v'yadayin - דִּי־לָא בִידַיִן) is: “Not by might nor by power” [Zechariah 4:6], but rather by itself (meʿatzmah - מֵעַצְמָהּ - spontaneously, supernaturally) at the time of the End (bishʿat ha-ketz - בִּשְׁעַת הַקֵּץ).

words.eng: to be or become king or queen

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־קֳבֵ֣ל דִּֽי־חֲזַ֡יְתָ דִּ֣י מִטּוּרָא֩ אִתְגְּזֶ֨רֶת אֶ֜בֶן דִּי־לָ֣א בִידַ֗יִן וְ֠הַדֵּ֠קֶת פַּרְזְלָ֨א נְחָשָׁ֤א חַסְפָּא֙ כַּסְפָּ֣א וְדַהֲבָ֔א אֱלָ֥הּ רַב֙ הוֹדַ֣ע לְמַלְכָּ֔א מָ֛ה דִּ֥י לֶהֱוֵ֖א אַחֲרֵ֣י דְנָ֑ה וְיַצִּ֥יב חֶלְמָ֖א וּמְהֵימַ֥ן פִּשְׁרֵֽהּ
verses.translation: just as you saw how a stone was hewn from the mountain, not by hands, and crushed the iron, bronze, clay, silver, and gold. The great God has made known to the king what will happen in the future. The dream is sure and its interpretation reliable.”
verses.rashi: Just as you saw just as you saw that a stone was broken off the mountain, which crumbled the entire image. This is the interpretation that the fifth kingdom will destroy and shatter them all. what will be after this what will be after this, after this kingdom of yours. and its interpretation is reliable for the Eternal of Israel neither lies nor repents.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:45] v'yatziv chelma umehaiman pishreh (וְיַצִּיב חֶלְמָא וּמְהֵימַן פִּשְׁרֵהּ - the dream is sure and its interpretation reliable): And the dream is correct/true (v'nachon ha-chalom) and its interpretation is reliable/trustworthy (v'ne'eman pitrono).

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּ֠אדַ֠יִן מַלְכָּ֤א נְבֽוּכַדְנֶצַּר֙ נְפַ֣ל עַל־אַנְפּ֔וֹהִי וּלְדָנִיֵּ֖אל סְגִ֑ד וּמִנְחָה֙ וְנִ֣יחֹחִ֔ין אֲמַ֖ר לְנַסָּ֥כָה לֵֽהּ
verses.translation: Then King Nebuchadnezzar prostrated himself and paid homage to Daniel and ordered that a grain offering and pleasing offerings be made to him.
verses.rashi: ordered to offer up... libations He wished to deify him.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:46] Be'dayin malka Nevuchadnetztzar... segid (בֵּאדַיִן מַלְכָּא נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּר... סְגִד - Then King Nebuchadnezzar... paid homage): Paid homage (segid - סְגִד): He prostrated himself (hishtachavah - הִשְׁתַּחֲוָה). [The word segid means] and he paid homage/bowed to him (v'yisgod lo - וְיִסְגֹּד לוֹ - using the Hebrew cognate root), and he prostrated himself (v'yishtachu - וַיִּשְׁתַּחוּ). [Daniel 2:46] Nichochin (נִיחֹחִין - pleasing offerings): And behold, [by ordering this type of offering, typically reserved for deity] he regarded him [Daniel] as a god (k'mo eloah - כְּמוֹ אֱלוֹהַּ).

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: עָנֵה֩ מַלְכָּ֨א לְדָנִיֵּ֜אל וְאָמַ֗ר מִן־קְשֹׁט֙ דִּ֣י אֱלָהֲכ֗וֹן ה֣וּא אֱלָ֧הּ אֱלָהִ֛ין וּמָרֵ֥א מַלְכִ֖ין וְגָלֵ֣ה רָזִ֑ין דִּ֣י יְכֵ֔לְתָּ לְמִגְלֵ֖א רָזָ֥א דְנָֽה
verses.translation: The king said in reply to Daniel, “Truly your God must be the God of gods and Sovereign of kings and the revealer of mysteries to have enabled you to reveal this mystery.”
verses.rashi: truly [as translated,] in truth. and the Master of the kings The Lord of lords. this secret [as translated,] this secret.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:47] ʿAneh malka... min keshot (עָנֵה מַלְכָּא... מִן־קְשֹׁט - The king answered... Truly): [The word] K'shot [means] words of truth (imrei emet - אִמְרֵי אֱמֶת).

verses.hebrew_full: אֱדַ֨יִן מַלְכָּ֜א לְדָנִיֵּ֣אל רַבִּ֗י וּמַתְּנָ֨ן רַבְרְבָ֤ן שַׂגִּיאָן֙ יְהַב־לֵ֔הּ וְהַ֨שְׁלְטֵ֔הּ עַ֖ל כׇּל־מְדִינַ֣ת בָּבֶ֑ל וְרַ֨ב־סִגְנִ֔ין עַ֖ל כׇּל־חַכִּימֵ֥י בָבֶֽל
verses.translation: The king then elevated Daniel and gave him very many gifts, and made him governor of the whole province of Babylon and chief prefect of all the sages of Babylon.
verses.rashi: elevated Daniel He elevated Daniel over all the princes.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:48] Edayin malka... rabbi (אֱדַיִן מַלְכָּא... רַבִּי - Then the king... elevated): Elevated (rabbi - רַבִּי): [Means] He made great/elevated (gadal - גָּדַל). There are those who say that province (medinat - מְדִינַת) [as in "province of Babylon"] means a district/province (machoz - מָחוֹז). If so, how could Ahasuerus have one hundred and twenty-seven provinces (medinah)? [Esther 1:1]. And [even] a king who has [only] ten [such districts] is [considered] a great king! And it is written: “...robbery, and perversion of justice and righteousness you see in the province (ba-medinah - בַּמְּדִינָה)” [Ecclesiastes 5:7] – [this context suggests] that the meaning is the capital city (ʿir ha-meluchah - עִיר הַמְּלוּכָה). And it is written [Aramaic]: “in the province (medinata) of Media” [Ezra 6:2]. Furthermore: “And I was in Shushan the capital (ha-birah - הַבִּירָה), which is in Elam the province (ha-medinah - הַמְּדִינָה)” [Daniel 8:2]. And if Elam is a [standard] province/district (machoz), why did it not say “in the province of Elam” (b'medinat ʿElam - בִּמְדִינַת עֵילָם) just as they interpreted “province of Babylon” (medinat Bavel - מְדִינַת בָּבֶל)?! [Daniel 2:48] v'rav signin (וְרַב־סִגְנִין - and chief prefect): [The word signin, prefects/governors] is like [the Hebrew cognate s'ganim in passages such as]: “And he shall come upon rulers (s'ganim - סְגָנִים)” [Isaiah 41:25].

verses.hebrew_full: וְדָנִיֵּאל֙ בְּעָ֣א מִן־מַלְכָּ֔א וּמַנִּ֗י עַ֤ל עֲבִֽידְתָּא֙ דִּ֚י מְדִינַ֣ת בָּבֶ֔ל לְשַׁדְרַ֥ךְ מֵישַׁ֖ךְ וַעֲבֵ֣ד נְג֑וֹ וְדָנִיֵּ֖אל בִּתְרַ֥ע מַלְכָּֽא
verses.translation: At Daniel’s request, the king appointed Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego to administer the province of Babylon; while Daniel himself was at the king’s court.
verses.rashi: and he appointed, over the affairs of the capital cities of Babylon He appointed, over all the necessities of the kingdom, Hananiah Mishael, and Azariah.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 2:49] v'Daniel bitraʿ malka (וְדָנִיֵּאל בִּתְרַע מַלְכָּא - And Daniel was at the king’s court [lit. gate]): For this is a great status (maʿalah gedolah - מַעֲלָה גְדוֹלָה), because there sat the judges (ha-dayyanin - הַדַּיָּנִין) and the magistrates (v'ha-shoftim - וְהַשֹּׁפְטִים). And likewise: “and Mordecai was sitting at the king’s gate” [Esther 2:19, Esther 2:21; Esther 5:9, Esther 5:13; Esther 6:10, Esther 6:12], as I explained in [my commentary on] the Scroll of Esther.

verses.hebrew_full: נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֣ר מַלְכָּ֗א עֲבַד֙ צְלֵ֣ם דִּֽי־דְהַ֔ב רוּמֵהּ֙ אַמִּ֣ין שִׁתִּ֔ין פְּתָיֵ֖הּ אַמִּ֣ין שִׁ֑ת אֲקִימֵהּ֙ בְּבִקְעַ֣ת דּוּרָ֔א בִּמְדִינַ֖ת בָּבֶֽל
verses.translation: King Nebuchadnezzar made a statue of gold sixty cubits high and six cubits broad. He set it up in the plain of Dura in the province of Babylon.
verses.rashi: made an image He had an urge to make an image of gold. its width six cubits; he set it up etc. If its thickness was only six [cubits], how could it stand at a height of sixty cubits? Said Rav Bibi: They would set it up and it would fall, until they brought all the gold of Jerusalem and poured a base around its feet, to fulfill what was said (Ezek. 7:19): “and their gold will be for a repugnant thing.” in the plain of Dura the name of a place.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:1] Nebuchadnezzar... Its height (רוּמֵהּ - rumeh): This is its height (govho), this is its stature (komato). Its breadth (פְּתָיֵהּ - pətayeh): Its width (rochbo) [was] six cubits. In the plain of Dura (בְּבִקְעַת דּוּרָא - bəviqʿat Dura): [Perhaps alluding to the plain where the Tower of Babel was built, where it says:] “and they settled there” [Genesis 11:2]; this is the plain of Dura.

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: וּנְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֣ר מַלְכָּ֡א שְׁלַ֡ח לְמִכְנַ֣שׁ לַֽאֲחַשְׁדַּרְפְּנַיָּ֡א סִגְנַיָּ֣א וּֽפַחֲוָתָ֡א אֲדַרְגָּזְרַיָּא֩ גְדָ֨בְרַיָּ֤א דְּתָבְרַיָּא֙ תִּפְתָּיֵ֔א וְכֹ֖ל שִׁלְטֹנֵ֣י מְדִֽינָתָ֑א לְמֵתֵא֙ לַחֲנֻכַּ֣ת צַלְמָ֔א דִּ֥י הֲקֵ֖ים נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֥ר מַלְכָּֽא
verses.translation: King Nebuchadnezzar then sent word to gather the satraps, prefects, governors, counselors, treasurers, judges, officers, and all the provincial officials to attend the dedication of the statue that King Nebuchadnezzar had set up.
verses.rashi: the satraps Aram. לַאֲחַשְּׁדַרְפְּנַיָּא dukes. governors rulers. the judges, the treasurers, etc. They are all names of nations. the dedication of the image the beginning of its worship.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:2] And Nebuchadnezzar... to gather (לְמִכְנַשׁ - l'michnash): To assemble (l'kabetz) and to bring together (ul'chaber). The satraps (אֲחַשְׁדַּרְפְּנַיָּא - achashdarpenaya) and those mentioned after them [the list of officials]: These are [like] seven levels [of administration] in the language of Ishmael [i.e., in Arabic administrative systems], but we do not know the [precise] meaning of counselors (אֲדַרְגָּזְרַיָּא - adargazraya) and those that come after them [i.e., treasurers, judges, officers]. And there was a sage in Spain who interpreted counselors (אֲדַרְגָּזְרַיָּא - adargazraya) as "mighty deciders" (adirei g'zerin - combining Hebrew/Aramaic roots a-d-r 'mighty' and g-z-r 'decree/decide'). But there is no need [for such speculation]. Therefore, we will leave them [uninterpreted], as it will not be beneficial [to guess]. To attend [lit. to come] (לְמֵתֵא - l'mete): To come (lavo).

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֡יִן מִֽתְכַּנְּשִׁ֡ין אֲחַשְׁדַּרְפְּנַיָּ֡א סִגְנַיָּ֣א וּֽפַחֲוָתָ֡א אֲדַרְגָּזְרַיָּ֣א גְדָבְרַיָּא֩ דְּתָ֨בְרַיָּ֜א תִּפְתָּיֵ֗א וְכֹל֙ שִׁלְטֹנֵ֣י מְדִֽינָתָ֔א לַחֲנֻכַּ֣ת צַלְמָ֔א דִּ֥י הֲקֵ֖ים נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֣ר מַלְכָּ֑א (וקאמין) וְקָֽיְמִין֙ לׇקֳבֵ֣ל צַלְמָ֔א דִּ֥י הֲקֵ֖ים נְבֻכַדְנֶצַּֽר
verses.translation: So the satraps, prefects, governors, counselors, treasurers, judges, officers, and all the provincial officials assembled for the dedication of the statue that King Nebuchadnezzar had set up, and stood before the statue that Nebuchadnezzar had set up.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:3] Then (בֵּאדַיִן - Be'adayin): Then (Az) the aforementioned ones [i.e., the officials previously listed] assembled (hit'asfu).

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"
Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: וְכָרוֹזָ֖א קָרֵ֣א בְחָ֑יִל לְכ֤וֹן אָֽמְרִין֙ עַֽמְמַיָּ֔א אֻמַּיָּ֖א וְלִשָּׁנַיָּֽא
verses.translation: The herald proclaimed in a loud voice, “You are commanded, O peoples and nations of every language,
verses.rashi: And the announcement was issued aloud [as translated,] and the announcement was issued aloud. To you we are saying, O peoples [as translated,] To you, all peoples and nations, we are announcing the command of the king as a message from the king.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:4] And the herald (וְכָרוֹזָא - v'kharoza): The one who transmits a proclamation forcefully/loudly (הַמַּעֲבִיר קוֹל בְּחַיִל - ha-maʿavir kol b'chayil). Yefet [ben Ali] said: That ‘peoples’ (עַמְמַיָּא - ʿammemaya) are those who have [their own distinct] gods, while ‘nations’ (אֻמַּיָּא - ʾummayya) are [those groups defined perhaps more politically or ethnically, possibly] without a [specific foundational] law/religion (Torah). And [regarding] ‘languages’ (לִשָּׁנַיָּא - lishanayya): among them [groups sharing a language], there might be one law/religion (Torah) for them but not one language [unifying them with others of the same religion], or [they might exist] without a [distinct unifying] law/religion (Torah).

verses.hebrew_full: בְּעִדָּנָ֡א דִּֽי־תִשְׁמְע֡וּן קָ֣ל קַרְנָ֣א מַ֠שְׁרוֹקִיתָ֠א (קיתרס) קַתְר֨וֹס שַׂבְּכָ֤א פְסַנְתֵּרִין֙ סוּמְפֹּ֣נְיָ֔א וְכֹ֖ל זְנֵ֣י זְמָרָ֑א תִּפְּל֤וּן וְתִסְגְּדוּן֙ לְצֶ֣לֶם דַּהֲבָ֔א דִּ֥י הֲקֵ֖ים נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֥ר מַלְכָּֽא
verses.translation: when you hear the sound of the horn, pipe, zither, lyre, psaltery, bagpipe, and all other types of instruments, to fall down and worship the statue of gold that King Nebuchadnezzar has set up.
verses.rashi: At the time you hear the sound of the whistling horn Aram. קָל קַרְנָא מַשְּׁרוֹקֵיתָא, the sound of the whistling horn, like (Zech. 10:8) “I will whistle (אֶשְּׁרְקָא) to them, and I will gather them.” the clavichord, the harp, etc. They are all musical instruments. kinds of music Aram. זְנֵי זְמָרָא kinds of music. (Gen. 1:11) “After its kind,” Onkelos renders: לִזְנוֹהִי.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:5] At the time (בְּעִדָּנָא - Beʿiddana): [Means] At the time (Beʿet). The sound of the horn (קָל קַרְנָא - kal karna): The sound of the shofar (kol ha-shofar). Pipe (מַשְׁרוֹקִיתָא - mashrokita): An instrument to whistle (lishrok) with. However, the zither (קַתְרֹס - katros) and its companions [i.e., the lyre (sabbekha), psaltery (psanterin), bagpipe (sumponyah listed in the verse)], all of them are musical instruments (k'lei niggunim). Also in the Holy Tongue [Hebrew, there are instruments like] the kinnor [lyre/harp] and ugav [pipe/flute] [mentioned together first in Genesis 4:21: “he was the ancestor of all who play the kinnor and ugav”], and machol [dance/pipe?] [cf. Psalms 149:3], and minim [strings?] [cf. Psalms 150:4], and their [exact] identities (shmotam - lit. 'names', but referring to the precise nature/identification of the instrument) we do not know. Although ugav is [perhaps] derived from [the root related to pleasure/love, as in the verb] “rejoice” (vətaʿagvah) [Job 21:12], and minim [perhaps means] two instruments, and machol [perhaps means] a flute (chalil), and the kinnor [is shaped] like a menorah [candelabrum]. And these are speculations (sevarot) without proof (re'ayah).

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: וּמַן־דִּי־לָ֥א יִפֵּ֖ל וְיִסְגֻּ֑ד בַּהּ־שַׁעֲתָ֣א יִתְרְמֵ֔א לְגֽוֹא־אַתּ֥וּן נוּרָ֖א יָקִֽדְתָּֽא
verses.translation: Whoever will not fall down and worship shall at once be thrown into a burning fiery furnace.”
verses.rashi: a fiery furnace a pit that is made to burn rocks into lime, and that is כִּבְשָּׁן in all the Scriptures.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:6] And whoever (וּמַן - u-man)... shall be thrown (יִתְרְמֵא - yitremē): [Means] Shall be cast (yushlakh). And [it is] like [the Hebrew cognate verb in the verse]: “He has thrown (ramah) into the sea” [Exodus 15:1].

words.eng: meaning ‘in her’·to look at or to

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־קֳבֵ֣ל דְּנָ֡ה בֵּהּ־זִמְנָ֡א כְּדִ֣י שָֽׁמְעִ֣ין כׇּֽל־עַמְמַיָּ֡א קָ֣ל קַרְנָא֩ מַשְׁר֨וֹקִיתָ֜א (קיתרס) קַתְר֤וֹס שַׂבְּכָא֙ פְּסַנְטֵרִ֔ין וְכֹ֖ל זְנֵ֣י זְמָרָ֑א נָֽפְלִ֨ין כׇּֽל־עַֽמְמַיָּ֜א אֻמַּיָּ֣א וְלִשָּׁנַיָּ֗א סָֽגְדִין֙ לְצֶ֣לֶם דַּהֲבָ֔א דִּ֥י הֲקֵ֖ים נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֥ר מַלְכָּֽא
verses.translation: And so, as soon as all the peoples heard the sound of the horn, pipe, zither, lyre, psaltery, and all other types of instruments, all peoples and nations of every language fell down and worshiped the statue of gold that King Nebuchadnezzar had set up.
verses.rashi: In view of this in view of this, that they took heed to observe the king’s command concerning this bagpipes would be played. at that time At the time that they hear the sound of the music, which is a sign that those who prostrate themselves should come to prostrate, all peoples would fall etc.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:7] Therefore [lit. Corresponding to all this] (כָּל־קֳבֵל דְּנָה - Kol kovel denah): [Meaning] Upon the completion of all this (B'hashlamat kol zeh).

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־קֳבֵ֤ל דְּנָה֙ בֵּהּ־זִמְנָ֔א קְרִ֖בוּ גֻּבְרִ֣ין כַּשְׂדָּאִ֑ין וַאֲכַ֥לוּ קַרְצֵיה֖וֹן דִּ֥י יְהוּדָיֵֽא
verses.translation: Seizing the occasion, certain Chaldeans came forward to slander the Jews.
verses.rashi: In view of this in view of this, that their enemies saw that there is a time to denounce them. some Chaldean men approached and denounced Aram. וַאֲכַלוּ קַרְצֵיהוֹן informed about them. (Lev. 19: 16) “Do not go as a talebearer among your people,” is translated by Targum as קוּרצִין לָא תֵיכוּ I say that it was the custom of talebearers to eat a meal in the place where they informed, and that was a sign of the verification of their words; for at that time it was incumbent upon them to clarify their words and to verify the slander that they spoke, and that meal was called eating קוּרצִין, an expression of (Prov. 6:13) “he winks (קוֹרֵץ) with his eyes,” which is a sign of slander.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:8] To slander [lit. And they ate their pieces] (וַאֲכַלוּ קַרְצֵיהוֹן - va'akhalu kartzeihon): Targum [Onkelos translates the prohibition] “You shall not go about as a talebearer (rakhil)” [Leviticus 19:16] [using this very idiom]: “Do not eat fragments (kurtzin) among your people” (לָא תֵיכוּל קוּרְצִין בְּעַמָּךְ) [Onkelos Leviticus 19:16]. [Ibn Ezra elaborates on the meaning of this idiom:] This refers to the one who speaks evil (raʿah) about another in secret, as if he were eating his flesh (k'ilu okhel besaro).

verses.hebrew_full: עֲנוֹ֙ וְאָ֣מְרִ֔ין לִנְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֖ר מַלְכָּ֑א מַלְכָּ֖א לְעָלְמִ֥ין חֱיִֽי
verses.translation: They spoke up and said to King Nebuchadnezzar, “O king, live forever!
verses.rashi: They spoke up and said Aram. עֲנוֹ, they shouted. Every [expression of] עֲנִיָה in Scriptures is an expression of raising the voice.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:9] They spoke up (עֲנוֹ - ʿano): This is the customary practice (mishpat) for those speaking to the king: to offer a prayer that his days may be long (l'hitpallel she-ya'arichu yamav).

verses.hebrew_full: (אנתה) אַ֣נְתְּ מַלְכָּא֮ שָׂ֣מְתָּ טְּעֵם֒ דִּ֣י כׇל־אֱנָ֡שׁ דִּֽי־יִשְׁמַ֡ע קָ֣ל קַרְנָ֣א מַ֠שְׁרֹקִיתָ֠א (קיתרס) קַתְר֨וֹס שַׂבְּכָ֤א פְסַנְתֵּרִין֙ (וסיפניה) וְסוּפֹּ֣נְיָ֔ה וְכֹ֖ל זְנֵ֣י זְמָרָ֑א יִפֵּ֥ל וְיִסְגֻּ֖ד לְצֶ֥לֶם דַּהֲבָֽא
verses.translation: You, O king, gave an order that everyone who hears the horn, pipe, zither, lyre, psaltery, bagpipe, and all types of instruments must fall down and worship the golden statue,
verses.rashi: gave an order issued a decree.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:10] You (אַנְתְּ - Ant): [Meaning, regarding the phrase "You... gave an order" (שָׂמְתָּ טְּעֵם - samta te'em)]: You made (samta) the announcement of your decree (hoda'at ta'amcha).

verses.hebrew_full: וּמַן־דִּי־לָ֥א יִפֵּ֖ל וְיִסְגֻּ֑ד יִתְרְמֵ֕א לְגֽוֹא־אַתּ֥וּן נוּרָ֖א יָקִֽדְתָּֽא
verses.translation: and whoever does not fall down and worship shall be thrown into a burning fiery furnace.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:11] Into the furnace (לְגוֹא אַתּוּן - l'go attun): [Means] the fiery furnace (kivshan ha-esh).

verses.hebrew_full: אִיתַ֞י גֻּבְרִ֣ין יְהוּדָאיִ֗ן דִּֽי־מַנִּ֤יתָ יָתְהוֹן֙ עַל־עֲבִידַת֙ מְדִינַ֣ת בָּבֶ֔ל שַׁדְרַ֥ךְ מֵישַׁ֖ךְ וַעֲבֵ֣ד נְג֑וֹ גֻּבְרַיָּ֣א אִלֵּ֗ךְ לָא־שָׂ֨מֽוּ (עליך) עֲלָ֤ךְ מַלְכָּא֙ טְעֵ֔ם (לאלהיך) לֵֽאלָהָךְ֙ לָ֣א פָלְחִ֔ין וּלְצֶ֧לֶם דַּהֲבָ֛א דִּ֥י הֲקֵ֖ימְתָּ לָ֥א סָגְדִֽין
verses.translation: There are certain Jews whom you appointed to administer the province of Babylon, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego; those men pay no heed to you, O king; they do not serve your god or worship the statue of gold that you have set up.”
verses.rashi: There are Aram. אִיתַי Judahite men of the tribe of Judah. these Aram. אִלֵּךְ did not take counsel to follow your decree Aram. לָא שָּׂמוּ עֲלָיִךְ מַלְכָּא טְעֵם. They did not care to give themselves counsel to care for your decrees.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:12] There are (אִיתַי - Itai): [Means] There are (Yesh). Whom you appointed (דִּי־מַנִּיתָ יָתְהוֹן - di mannita yathhon): Like [the concept of appointment described previously, e.g.,] “whom the chief officer appointed” (אשר מנה שר הסריסים - asher minnah sar ha-sarisim) [Daniel 1:11]. And behold, there is a question: There is no doubt that Daniel was more honored [than his companions], and we know that he did not bow down to the statue. Why did the Chaldeans not mention him to the king? According to my opinion: They were afraid to mention him, after they knew that the king had offered pleasing offerings (נִיחֹחִין - nichochin) before him, just as one would do for his god [cf. Daniel 2:46]. But Yefet ben Ali said: That they [the Chaldeans] were his [Daniel's] admirers/friends (ohavav), therefore they did not mention him. [Ibn Ezra concludes:] But there is no need [for this explanation by Yefet; the explanation based on fear is sufficient].

words.eng: (mark of the direct object or the accusative and not translated)

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֤יִן נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּר֙ בִּרְגַ֣ז וַחֲמָ֔א אֲמַר֙ לְהַיְתָיָ֔ה לְשַׁדְרַ֥ךְ מֵישַׁ֖ךְ וַעֲבֵ֣ד נְג֑וֹ בֵּאדַ֙יִן֙ גֻּבְרַיָּ֣א אִלֵּ֔ךְ הֵיתָ֖יוּ קֳדָ֥ם מַלְכָּֽא
verses.translation: Then Nebuchadnezzar, in raging fury, ordered Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego to be brought; so those men were brought before the king.
verses.rashi: ordered to bring [as translated,] ordered to bring. were brought [as translated,] were brought.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:13] Then (בֵּאדַיִן - Be'adayin)... to be brought (לְהַיְתָיָה - l'haytaya): [This means] like [the Hebrew imperative meaning] “Bring!” [e.g., הֵתָיוּ - hetayu]. However, it [the Aramaic verb form] is from the causative verbal stem (binyan kavéd ha-nusaf - lit. 'the heavy conjugation with addition'), because the verb is transitive (ha-poʿel yotze).

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: עָנֵ֤ה נְבֽוּכַדְנֶצַּר֙ וְאָמַ֣ר לְה֔וֹן הַצְדָּ֕א שַׁדְרַ֥ךְ מֵישַׁ֖ךְ וַעֲבֵ֣ד נְג֑וֹ לֵֽאלָהַ֗י לָ֤א אִֽיתֵיכוֹן֙ פָּֽלְחִ֔ין וּלְצֶ֧לֶם דַּהֲבָ֛א דִּ֥י הֲקֵ֖ימֶת לָ֥א סָֽגְדִֽין
verses.translation: Nebuchadnezzar spoke to them and said, “Is it true, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego, that you do not serve my god or worship the statue of gold that I have set up?
verses.rashi: Is it meaningless Aram. הַצְדָא. Are my decrees void and desolate and an empty thing in your eyes, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, that you do not worship my god? The “hey” of הַצְדָא is used as an interrogative expression. that I have set up [as translated,] that I have set up.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:14] Is it true (הַצְדָּא - Hatzda): He asked them if it was indeed true (ha-emet hu khakha) that it was so.

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: כְּעַ֞ן הֵ֧ן אִֽיתֵיכ֣וֹן עֲתִידִ֗ין דִּ֣י בְעִדָּנָ֡א דִּֽי־תִשְׁמְע֡וּן קָ֣ל קַרְנָ֣א מַשְׁרוֹקִיתָ֣א (קיתרס) קַתְר֣וֹס שַׂבְּכָ֡א פְּסַנְתֵּרִין֩ וְסוּמְפֹּ֨נְיָ֜ה וְכֹ֣ל זְנֵ֣י זְמָרָ֗א תִּפְּל֣וּן וְתִסְגְּדוּן֮ לְצַלְמָ֣א דִֽי־עַבְדֵת֒ וְהֵן֙ לָ֣א תִסְגְּד֔וּן בַּהּ־שַׁעֲתָ֣א תִתְרְמ֔וֹן לְגֽוֹא־אַתּ֥וּן נוּרָ֖א יָקִֽדְתָּ֑א וּמַן־ה֣וּא אֱלָ֔הּ דִּ֥י יְשֵֽׁיזְבִנְכ֖וֹן מִן־יְדָֽי
verses.translation: Now if you are ready to fall down and worship the statue that I have made when you hear the sound of the horn, pipe, zither, lyre, psaltery, and bagpipe, and all other types of instruments, [well and good]; but if you will not worship, you shall at once be thrown into a burning fiery furnace, and what god is there that can save you from my power?”
verses.rashi: Now, behold you are destined Now, behold you are destined against your will to accept this upon yourselves. and if you do not prostrate yourselves and if you do not prostrate yourselves, you shall be cast. into into the midst of. and who is a god and who is a god who will save you from my hands?
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:15] Now (כְּעַן - Ke'an): [Means] Now (ʿAtah). If you are ready (הֵן אִיתֵיכוֹן עֲתִידִין - hen iteikhon ʿatidin): [Ibn Ezra interprets the king giving them this second chance as follows:] Behold, you are ready (hineh yeshkhem ʿatidim). He accorded them honor (chalak lahem kavod), perhaps [thinking] he would not kill them on account of Daniel's honor (ulai lo yahargem baʿavur k'vod Daniel).

words.eng: meaning ‘in her’·to look at or to

verses.hebrew_full: עֲנ֗וֹ שַׁדְרַ֤ךְ מֵישַׁךְ֙ וַעֲבֵ֣ד נְג֔וֹ וְאָמְרִ֖ין לְמַלְכָּ֑א נְבֽוּכַדְנֶצַּ֔ר לָֽא־חַשְׁחִ֨ין אֲנַ֧חְנָא עַל־דְּנָ֛ה פִּתְגָ֖ם לַהֲתָבוּתָֽךְ
verses.translation: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego said in reply to the king, “O Nebuchadnezzar, we have no need to answer you in this matter,
verses.rashi: to the king, ‘Nebuchadnezzar Why is his name mentioned? But this is what they said to him: “If [it concerns] taking upon ourselves to pay taxes, either the tax from the crops or the head taxes, you are king over us, [but] if [it concerns] denying the Holy One, blessed be He, [you are merely] Nebuchadnezzar. In our eyes, you are a lowly person and the most despised of the people. You and a dog are equal.” we do not care We do not care to take counsel on what to answer you, for the answer is ready and fluent in our mouths.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:16] Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego answered (עֲנוֹ - ʿAno): We have no need (לָֽא־חַשְׁחִין - la chashchin): [This means] We do not need (lo tzrichin anu) to answer you [in this matter]. Because our God whom we serve (עובדים - ovdim), acting powerfully (lit. 'like one who cleaves [or plows] and breaks through' - כמו פולח ובוקע) upon the earth, is able to save us from the fiery furnace. Also [He is able to save us] from your hand, if He wishes. And behold, if He does not save [us]... it shall be known to you [i.e., their resolve remains unchanged, as elaborated in v. 17-18].

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: הֵ֣ן אִיתַ֗י אֱלָהַ֙נָא֙ דִּֽי־אֲנַ֣חְנָא פָֽלְחִ֔ין יָכִ֖ל לְשֵׁיזָבוּתַ֑נָא מִן־אַתּ֨וּן נוּרָ֧א יָקִֽדְתָּ֛א וּמִן־יְדָ֥ךְ מַלְכָּ֖א יְשֵׁיזִֽב
verses.translation: for if so it must be, our God whom we serve is able to save us from the burning fiery furnace—and will save us from your power, O king.
verses.rashi: He can save us He can save us from all our troubles. From the burning fiery furnace and from your hands, O king, He will save us from the furnace and from your hands He will save.

verses.hebrew_full: וְהֵ֣ן לָ֔א יְדִ֥יעַ לֶהֱוֵא־לָ֖ךְ מַלְכָּ֑א דִּ֤י (לאלהיך) לֵֽאלָהָךְ֙ לָא־אִיתַ֣נָא (איתינא) פָֽלְחִ֔ין וּלְצֶ֧לֶם דַּהֲבָ֛א דִּ֥י הֲקֵ֖ימְתָּ לָ֥א נִסְגֻּֽד
verses.translation: But even if [our God] does not, be it known to you, O king, that we will not serve your god or worship the statue of gold that you have set up.”
verses.rashi: And if not And if He will not wish to save our bodies, let it be known to you, etc.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֨יִן נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֜ר הִתְמְלִ֣י חֱמָ֗א וּצְלֵ֤ם אַנְפּ֙וֹהִי֙ (אשתנו) אֶשְׁתַּנִּ֔י עַל־שַׁדְרַ֥ךְ מֵישַׁ֖ךְ וַעֲבֵ֣ד נְג֑וֹ עָנֵ֤ה וְאָמַר֙ לְמֵזֵ֣א לְאַתּוּנָ֔א חַ֨ד־שִׁבְעָ֔ה עַ֛ל דִּ֥י חֲזֵ֖ה לְמֵזְיֵֽהּ
verses.translation: Nebuchadnezzar was so filled with rage at Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego that his visage was distorted, and he gave an order to heat up the furnace to seven times its usual heat.
verses.rashi: was filled with wrath [as translated,] was filled with wrath. and the features of his face changed [as translated,] and the features of his face changed. to heat the furnace to light the fiery furnace and to heat it. seven times as much as it should be heated instead of once, seven times as much as they calculated to heat it up; and it was as though they would kindle it seven times as much as it required. to be heated lit. to heat it.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:19] Then (בֵּאדַיִן - Be'adayin)... his face (אַנְפּוֹהִי - anpohi): [Means] His face/anger (appo). To heat up (לְמֵזֵא - l'meze): [Means] To add fuel/burning (l'hosif moked). Like [the word mezei found in the phrase]: “wasted by (mezei) hunger” [Deuteronomy 32:24].

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: וּלְגֻבְרִ֤ין גִּבָּֽרֵי־חַ֙יִל֙ דִּ֣י בְחַיְלֵ֔הּ אֲמַר֙ לְכַפָּתָ֔ה לְשַׁדְרַ֥ךְ מֵישַׁ֖ךְ וַעֲבֵ֣ד נְג֑וֹ לְמִרְמֵ֕א לְאַתּ֥וּן נוּרָ֖א יָקִֽדְתָּֽא
verses.translation: He commanded some of the strongest men of his army to bind Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego, and to throw them into the burning fiery furnace.
verses.rashi: certain mighty men in his army [as translated,] certain mighty men in his army. And he commanded...to bind He commanded to bind them because he saw that they were strengthening themselves to answer him harshly, and he said, “These are mighty”; so he commanded the mighty men in his army to bind them.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:20] And to men, mighty warriors (וּלְגֻבְרִין גִּבָּֽרֵי־חַיִל - ul'guvrin gibbarei-chayil): [Meaning] those who are in his armies (she-hem b'tziv'otav). To bind (לְכַפָּתָה - l'khappata): [Means] to tie (likshor).

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֜יִן גֻּבְרַיָּ֣א אִלֵּ֗ךְ כְּפִ֙תוּ֙ בְּסַרְבָּלֵיהוֹן֙ (פטישיהון) פַּטְּשֵׁיה֔וֹן וְכַרְבְּלָתְה֖וֹן וּלְבֻשֵׁיה֑וֹן וּרְמִ֕יו לְגֽוֹא־אַתּ֥וּן נוּרָ֖א יָקִֽדְתָּֽא
verses.translation: So these men, in their shirts, trousers, hats, and other garments, were bound and thrown into the burning fiery furnace.
verses.rashi: these men [as translated,] these men. were bound in their cloaks They were bound in the garments in which they enwrapped themselves. their trousers, their robes types of royal raiment that they had; and although they were going forth to be judged before Nebuchadnezzar regarding their death sentence, they did not change their clothes to humble their greatness. From here the Sages learned that even in times of danger, a person should not change from the ruling position to which he has become accustomed. their robes Aram. וְכַרְבְּלָתְהוֹן. There is a word similar to this (in I Chron. 15: 27): “And David was clothed (מְכֻרְבָּל). and they were cast into the burning, fiery furnce [as translated,] and they were cast into the burning, fiery furnace.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:21] Then (בֵּאדַיִן - Be'adayin)... in their shirts/mantles (בְּסַרְבָּלֵיהוֹן - b'sarbaleyhon): These are the outer garment/mantle (ha-simlah ha-elyonah). And it is the custom of the nations to burn people [while still] in their garments, which are [considered] worth nothing [in this context]. And their hats/turbans (וְכַרְבְּלָתְהוֹן - v'kharbelatehon): Like [the related verb k-r-b-l used in the verse] “and David was clothed/wrapped (mekhurbal) with a robe of fine linen” [I Chronicles 15:27]. And their trousers/tunics (וּפַטְּשֵׁיהוֹן - u'patt'sheihon): [Means] shirts/tunics (challukin) or trousers (mikhnasayim). And their garments (וּלְבוּשֵׁיהוֹן - ul'vusheihon): [Means] other/remaining garments (sh'ar begadim).

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־קֳבֵ֣ל דְּנָ֗ה מִן־דִּ֞י מִלַּ֤ת מַלְכָּא֙ מַחְצְפָ֔ה וְאַתּוּנָ֖א אֵזֵ֣ה יַתִּ֑ירָה גֻּבְרַיָּ֣א אִלֵּ֗ךְ דִּ֤י הַסִּ֙קוּ֙ לְשַׁדְרַ֤ךְ מֵישַׁךְ֙ וַעֲבֵ֣ד נְג֔וֹ קַטִּ֣ל הִמּ֔וֹן שְׁבִיבָ֖א דִּ֥י נוּרָֽא
verses.translation: Because the king’s order was urgent, and the furnace was heated to excess, a tongue of flame killed the men who carried up Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego.
verses.rashi: In view of this in view of this, that the king’s command was strong. and the furnace was heated excessively and the furnace was heated to a great degree. these men these mighty men who had bound Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah and had cast them into the furnace. were killed by a spark of fire The power of a spark of fire burned them.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:22] Therefore [lit. Corresponding to all this] (כָּל־קֳבֵל דְּנָה - Kol kovel denah)... urgent (מַחְצְפָה - mach'tz'fa): [Means] with speed/quickly (bimehirut). Who carried up (דִּי הַסִּקוּ - di hassiqu): [This means "they caused to ascend" or "they carried up"]. [Ibn Ezra compares it to the Hebrew verb hishku]: Like “And they watered seven years” (וְהִשְׁקוּ שֶׁבַע שָׁנִים - v'hishku sheva shanim). Flame (שְׁבִיבָא - sheviva): [This Aramaic word for spark/flame is] like [its Hebrew cognate sheviv in the verse]: “the spark (sheviv) of his fire will not shine” [Job 18:5].

verses.hebrew_full: וְגֻבְרַיָּ֤א אִלֵּךְ֙ תְּלָ֣תֵּה֔וֹן שַׁדְרַ֥ךְ מֵישַׁ֖ךְ וַעֲבֵ֣ד נְג֑וֹ נְפַ֛לוּ לְגֽוֹא־אַתּוּן־נוּרָ֥א יָֽקִדְתָּ֖א מְכַפְּתִֽין
verses.translation: But those three men, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego, dropped, bound, into the burning fiery furnace.
verses.rashi: bound [as translated,] bound.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱדַ֙יִן֙ נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֣ר מַלְכָּ֔א תְּוַ֖הּ וְקָ֣ם בְּהִתְבְּהָלָ֑ה עָנֵ֨ה וְאָמַ֜ר לְהַדָּֽבְר֗וֹהִי הֲלָא֩ גֻבְרִ֨ין תְּלָתָ֜ה רְמֵ֤ינָא לְגוֹא־נוּרָא֙ מְכַפְּתִ֔ין עָנַ֤יִן וְאָמְרִין֙ לְמַלְכָּ֔א יַצִּיבָ֖א מַלְכָּֽא
verses.translation: Then King Nebuchadnezzar was astonished and, rising in haste, addressed his companions, saying, “Did we not throw three men, bound, into the fire?” They spoke in reply, “Surely, O king.”
verses.rashi: was bewildered Aram. תְּוַהּ, wondered. and stood up in haste Aram. וְקָם בְּהִתְבְּהָלָה. answered and said Aram. עָנַיִן וְאָמְרִין They answered and said to the king. The king is true Aram. יַצִיבָא מַלְכָּא The king has spoken the truth.
verses.ibn_ezra: To his companions (לְהַדָּֽבְרוֹהִי - l'haddab'rohi): [This refers to the king's assembled advisors]. [Ibn Ezra compares the concept of them being gathered, perhaps analogically, to the phrase] like: “inside its pen/fold” (בְּתוֹךְ הַדָּֽבְרוֹ - b'tokh haddovro) [Micah 2:12].

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: עָנֵ֣ה וְאָמַ֗ר הָֽא־אֲנָ֨ה חָזֵ֜ה גֻּבְרִ֣ין אַרְבְּעָ֗ה שְׁרַ֙יִן֙ מַהְלְכִ֣ין בְּגֽוֹא־נוּרָ֔א וַחֲבָ֖ל לָא־אִיתַ֣י בְּה֑וֹן וְרֵוֵהּ֙ דִּ֣י (רביעיא) רְֽבִיעָאָ֔ה דָּמֵ֖ה לְבַר־אֱלָהִֽין
verses.translation: He answered, “But I see four figures walking about unbound and unharmed in the fire and the fourth looks like a divine being.”
verses.rashi: free men walking in the midst of the fire freed from their bonds and walking in the midst of the fire. and there is no wound upon them [as translated,] and there is no wound upon them. and the form of the fourth [as translated,] and the form of the fourth. is like [that of] an angel It is like the angel I saw when I was with Sannecherib, when his armies were burned, as it is said (II Kings 19: 35): “[And an angel of the Lord went forth] and slew...of the camp of Assyria.” And Nebuchadnezzar was there and fled among the ten who fled from them.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:25] Unbound (שְׁרַ֙יִן֙ - sherayin): [Means] unbound/untied (mutarin). And harm (וַחֲבָ֖ל - va-chaval): [Means] destruction/damage (hashchatah). Like [the related Hebrew verb ch-b-l in the verse]: “and destroy (vəchibbel) the work of your hands” [Ecclesiastes 5:5]. And the appearance of the fourth (וְרֵוֵהּ֙ דִּ֣י רְֽבִיעָאָ֔ה - v'reveh di revi'a'ah): [Means] And the form/appearance (u'to'ar) of the fourth. Like a divine being [lit. like a son of gods/God] (לְבַר־אֱלָהִֽין - l'var elahin): [Means] like a son of God/gods (k'ven elohim). Like [the word bar meaning son in]: “What, my son (beri)? And what, O son (bar) of my womb?” [Proverbs 31:2].

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֜יִן קְרֵ֣ב נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֗ר לִתְרַע֮ אַתּ֣וּן נוּרָ֣א יָקִֽדְתָּא֒ עָנֵ֣ה וְאָמַ֗ר שַׁדְרַ֨ךְ מֵישַׁ֧ךְ וַעֲבֵד־נְג֛וֹ עַבְד֛וֹהִי דִּֽי־אֱלָהָ֥א (עליא) עִלָּאָ֖ה פֻּ֣קוּ וֶאֱת֑וֹ בֵּאדַ֣יִן נָֽפְקִ֗ין שַׁדְרַ֥ךְ מֵישַׁ֛ךְ וַעֲבֵ֥ד נְג֖וֹ מִן־גּ֥וֹא נוּרָֽא
verses.translation: Nebuchadnezzar then approached the hatch of the burning fiery furnace and called, “Shadrach, Meshach, Abed-nego, servants of the Most High God, come out!” So Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego came out of the fire.
verses.rashi: approached the gate of the... fiery furnace He approached the gate of the court, in which the furnace was situated, to speak with them, and at first, he saw them from afar. Step out and come “step out and come,” but he did not say to them, “step out and ascend.” From here we learn that the floor of the furnace floated up and became even with the ground, so that they should not be troubled to go up. Then...came out Although they were confident of the miracle that had been performed for them, they did not dare to treat the king lightly, to go out without permission.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:26] Then (בֵּאדַיִן - Be'adayin)... to the opening/gate (לִתְרַע - l'taraʿ): [Means] To the gate (l'shaʿar). And [the root ת-ר-ע also appears in Hebrew, as in the name] “Tirathites (Tirʿatim), Sucathites” [I Chronicles 2:55]. Come out (פֻּקוּ - puqu</i.):>Tze'u). And come (וֶאֱתוֹ - ve'eto): [Means] And come (u-vo'u). Like [the imperative form etayu in the phrase] “Return, come!” (שֻׁ֥בוּ אֵתָֽיוּ - shuvu etayu) [Isaiah 21:12].

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"
sign of the definite direct object

verses.hebrew_full: וּ֠מִֽתְכַּנְּשִׁ֠ין אֲחַשְׁדַּרְפְּנַיָּ֞א סִגְנַיָּ֣א וּפַחֲוָתָא֮ וְהַדָּבְרֵ֣י מַלְכָּא֒ חָזַ֣יִן לְגֻבְרַיָּ֣א אִלֵּ֡ךְ דִּי֩ לָֽא־שְׁלֵ֨ט נוּרָ֜א בְּגֶשְׁמְה֗וֹן וּשְׂעַ֤ר רֵֽאשְׁהוֹן֙ לָ֣א הִתְחָרַ֔ךְ וְסַרְבָּלֵיה֖וֹן לָ֣א שְׁנ֑וֹ וְרֵ֣יחַ נ֔וּר לָ֥א עֲדָ֖ת בְּהֽוֹן
verses.translation: The satraps, the prefects, the governors, and the royal companions gathered around to look at those men, on whose bodies the fire had had no effect, the hair of whose heads had not been singed, whose shirts looked no different, to whom not even the odor of fire clung.
verses.rashi: And the satraps...gathered Four nations were counted above in the first gathering who were not counted here: Adargazeria, Dethaveria, Gedaveria, Tiftaei. They are the mighty men whom the spark of fire had killed when they cast Hananiah and his colleagues into the fire. saw these men saw these men: Hananiah and his colleagues. that the fire had taken no effect on their bodies [as translated,] that the fire had taken no effect on their bodies. on their bodies בְּגֶשְּׁמְהוֹן, their bodies, in Aramaic, and there are many [instances of it] in this Book. the hair of their heads was not singed [as translated,] the hair of their heads was not singed. their robes were not changed They did not change from their appearance. and the smell of fire had not been absorbed by them Aram. לָא עֲדַת בְּהוֹן, did not pass into their midst.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:27] And gathered (וּמִֽתְכַּנְּשִׁין - umitkanneshin)... and the royal companions (וְהַדָּבְרֵי מַלְכָּא - v'haddavrei malka): [These are] his counselors (yoʿatzav) who are accustomed to being with him (she-hen nehogin ʿimo). On whose bodies (בְּגֶשְׁמְהוֹן - b'geshmehon): [Means] their bodies (gufotam). Like [the Hebrew word geshem (meaning rain, homonymous with the Aramaic for body) used in]: “and the rain (geshem) makes them grow” [Isaiah 44:14]. And the odor of fire... had not clung [lit. passed on] (וְרֵיחַ נוּר... לָא עֲדָת בְּהוֹן - v'reiach nur... la ʿadat b'hon): The odor of fire (reiach esh) did not pass (ʿavar) onto them. Like [the Hebrew verb ʿadah in]: “like one who takes off (maʿadeh) a garment on a cold day” [Proverbs 25:20]. Had not been singed (לָא הִתְחָרַךְ - la hitḥarakh): [This Aramaic verb is] like [its Hebrew cognate ch-r-k in]: “The lazy man will not roast (lo yacharokh) his prey” [Proverbs 12:27]. And [the verse] mentioned the outer garment (סַרְבָּלֵיהוֹן - sarbaleyhon), and there is no need to mention the others [as it implies they were also unharmed].

verses.hebrew_full: עָנֵ֨ה נְבֽוּכַדְנֶצַּ֜ר וְאָמַ֗ר בְּרִ֤יךְ אֱלָהֲהוֹן֙ דִּֽי־שַׁדְרַ֤ךְ מֵישַׁךְ֙ וַעֲבֵ֣ד נְג֔וֹ דִּֽי־שְׁלַ֤ח מַלְאֲכֵהּ֙ וְשֵׁיזִ֣ב לְעַבְד֔וֹהִי דִּ֥י הִתְרְחִ֖צוּ עֲל֑וֹהִי וּמִלַּ֤ת מַלְכָּא֙ שַׁנִּ֔יו וִיהַ֣בוּ (גשמיהון) גֶשְׁמְה֗וֹן דִּ֠י לָֽא־יִפְלְח֤וּן וְלָֽא־יִסְגְּדוּן֙ לְכׇל־אֱלָ֔הּ לָהֵ֖ן לֵאלָֽהֲהֽוֹן
verses.translation: Nebuchadnezzar spoke up and said, “Blessed be the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego—in whom they placed their trust—and who sent an angel to save these servants, who flouted the king’s decree at the risk of their lives rather than serve or worship any god but their own God.
verses.rashi: Nebuchadnezzar cried out [as translated,] Nebuchadnezzar cried out. who trusted him [as translated,] who trusted him. deviated from the command of the king They deviated from the king’s command by not fulfilling it. and risked their lives lit. delivered their bodies. except to their God [as translated,] except to their God.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:28] Nebuchadnezzar spoke up (עָנֵה - ʿAne)... who sent His angel (דִּֽי־שְׁלַח מַלְאֲכֵהּ - di shelach malakheh): This [angel] is the fourth [figure seen in the furnace, Daniel 3:25]. Like [these examples of God sending angels to protect]: “The angel of the LORD encamps around those who fear Him” [Psalms 34:8]; And likewise: “and He sent an angel and brought us out of Egypt” [Numbers 20:16]; “And the angel of His presence saved them” [Isaiah 63:9]; “The angel who has redeemed me” [Genesis 48:16]; “Then the angel of God... moved” [Exodus 14:19]. And I will yet explain this to you very well / explain this thoroughly (be'er heitev). [See Ibn Ezra on Daniel 10:21.]

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִנִּי֮ שִׂ֣ים טְעֵם֒ דִּי֩ כׇל־עַ֨ם אֻמָּ֜ה וְלִשָּׁ֗ן דִּֽי־יֵאמַ֤ר (שלה) שָׁלוּ֙ עַ֣ל אֱלָהֲה֗וֹן דִּֽי־שַׁדְרַ֤ךְ מֵישַׁךְ֙ וַעֲבֵ֣ד נְג֔וֹא הַדָּמִ֣ין יִתְעֲבֵ֔ד וּבַיְתֵ֖הּ נְוָלִ֣י יִשְׁתַּוֵּ֑ה כׇּל־קֳבֵ֗ל דִּ֣י לָ֤א אִיתַי֙ אֱלָ֣הּ אׇחֳרָ֔ן דִּֽי־יִכֻּ֥ל לְהַצָּלָ֖ה כִּדְנָֽה
verses.translation: I hereby give an order that [anyone of] any people or nation of whatever language who blasphemes the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego shall be torn limb from limb, and their house confiscated, for there is no other God who is able to save in this way.”
verses.rashi: Now an order is issued by me [as translated,] Now an order is issued by me. that will speak amiss that will speak in error, i.e., any disrespectful word. limb from limb Aram הַּדָּמִין limbs; i.e., they will cut him into limbs. shall be made a dungheap [as translated,] shall be made a dungheap. that can save in this manner [as translated,] that can save in this manner.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:29] And from me (וּמִנִּי - Uminni)... blasphemes [lit. says something amiss/error] (שָׁלוּ - shalu): [This means] like [the Hebrew word for] error (shegagah). And [it is] like [the related Hebrew word shal in the phrase]: “for his error” (עַל הַשַּׁל - ʿal ha-shal) [II Samuel 6:7]. For [lit. Corresponding to all this] (כָּל־קֳבֵל - Kol kovel): [Regarding the phrase] “to save in this way” (לְהַצָּלָה כִּדְנָה - l'hatzalah kidnah): [It means] like this God (k'elohim ha-zeh) [can save].

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֣יִן מַלְכָּ֗א הַצְלַ֛ח לְשַׁדְרַ֥ךְ מֵישַׁ֛ךְ וַעֲבֵ֥ד נְג֖וֹ בִּמְדִינַ֥ת בָּבֶֽל
verses.translation: Thereupon the king promoted Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed-nego in the province of Babylon.
verses.rashi: made...prosper made them prosper and aggrandized them.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:30] Thereupon the king promoted (הַצְלַח - Hatzlach): [This is] from the root/noun-form success/prosperity (מִגִּזְרַת הַצְלָחָה - mi-gizrat hatzlachah). And [it is] like [the same verb used concerning Daniel]: “And Daniel prospered” (וְדָנִיֵּאל הַצְלַח - v'Daniel hatzlach) [Daniel 6:29].

verses.hebrew_full: נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֣ר מַלְכָּ֗א לְֽכׇל־עַֽמְמַיָּ֞א אֻמַּיָּ֧א וְלִשָּׁנַיָּ֛א דִּֽי־דָיְרִ֥ין (דארין) בְּכׇל־אַרְעָ֖א שְׁלָמְכ֥וֹן יִשְׂגֵּֽא
verses.translation: “King Nebuchadnezzar to all people and nations of every language that inhabit the whole earth: May your well-being abound!
verses.rashi: King Nebuchadnezzar [says] to all peoples This is the text of the missive: King Nebuchadnezzar greets all peoples.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:31] King Nebuchadnezzar: Now begins the account of the letter (iggeret) that Nebuchadnezzar wrote to all the peoples dwelling (ha-darin) in all the land of his kingdom. And at the beginning of the writing [he says]: May your well-being abound (שְׁלָמְכוֹן יִשְׂגֵּא - sh'lamkhon yisge) – [The word yisge (abound) means] may it increase (yirbeh). Like [the related word saggi (great/mighty) in the verse]: “Behold, God is great (saggi)” [Job 36:26].

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: אָֽתַיָּא֙ וְתִמְהַיָּ֔א דִּ֚י עֲבַ֣ד עִמִּ֔י אֱלָהָ֖א (עליא) עִלָּאָ֑ה שְׁפַ֥ר קׇֽדָמַ֖י לְהַחֲוָיָֽה
verses.translation: The signs and wonders that the Most High God has worked for me I am pleased to relate.
verses.rashi: it behooves me to recite It is good for me to tell.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:32] Signs (אָתַיָּא - Ataya): [Means] Signs (Otot). And wonders (וְתִמְהַיָּא - v'Timhaya): [Means] Wonders/Marvels (Moftim) at which a person wonders (yitmah) because they have not seen their like. The Most High God (אֱלָהָא עִלָּאָה - Elaha Illa'ah): [Means] Most High (Elyon) above all gods. I am pleased [lit. it seemed good before me] to relate (שְׁפַר קֳדָמַי לְהַחֲוָיָה - Shefar kodamai l'hachavaya): [The word Shefar (pleased/seemed good)] is like [its Hebrew cognate shafer in the verse]: “who delivers words of beauty (imrei shafer)” [Genesis 49:21].

verses.hebrew_full: אָת֙וֹהִי֙ כְּמָ֣ה רַבְרְבִ֔ין וְתִמְה֖וֹהִי כְּמָ֣ה תַקִּיפִ֑ין מַלְכוּתֵהּ֙ מַלְכ֣וּת עָלַ֔ם וְשׇׁלְטָנֵ֖הּ עִם־דָּ֥ר וְדָֽר
verses.translation: How great are God’s signs; how mighty God’s wonders! God’s kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and God’s dominion endures throughout the generations.”
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 3:33] His signs (אָתוֹהִי - Atohi)... great (רַבְרְבִין - ravrevin): [Means] great/large (gedolim). And this doubling [of the root letters ר-ב in ravrevin for emphasis] is like [the formation of the Hebrew word] galgal (גַּלְגַּל - wheel/whirlwind) [which is also formed by reduplication of the root ג-ל].

verses.hebrew_full: אֲנָ֣ה נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֗ר שְׁלֵ֤ה הֲוֵית֙ בְּבֵיתִ֔י וְרַעְנַ֖ן בְּהֵיכְלִֽי
verses.translation: I, Nebuchadnezzar, was living serenely in my house, flourishing in my palace.
verses.rashi: was tranquil in my house [as translated,] was tranquil in my house.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:1] I (אֲנָה)... serene (שְׁלֵה): [The Aramaic word sh'leh (serene/at ease)] is like [the Hebrew]: "I was serene" (שָׁלֵו הָיִיתִי - shalev hayiti) [Job 16:12].

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: חֵ֥לֶם חֲזֵ֖ית וִֽידַחֲלִנַּ֑נִי וְהַרְהֹרִין֙ עַֽל־מִשְׁכְּבִ֔י וְחֶזְוֵ֥י רֵאשִׁ֖י יְבַהֲלֻנַּֽנִי
verses.translation: I had a dream that frightened me, and my thoughts in bed and the vision of my mind alarmed me.
verses.rashi: and it frightened me It brought upon me fear and fright.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:2] Dream (חֵ֥לֶם), and it frightened me (וִֽידַחֲלִנַּ֑נִי): [Meaning] it scared me (הִפְחִידַנִי - hiphchidani). And my thoughts (וְהַרְהֹרִין֙): The contemplation/musing of the heart. And it [this specific Aramaic word harhorin] has no [exact] parallel [in Scripture]. And the visions of my head (וְחֶזְוֵ֥י רֵאשִׁ֖י): [This phrase is used] because the eyes are in the head; or, because the vision is [formed] in the brain.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִנִּי֙ שִׂ֣ים טְעֵ֔ם לְהַנְעָלָ֣ה קׇֽדָמַ֔י לְכֹ֖ל חַכִּימֵ֣י בָבֶ֑ל דִּֽי־פְשַׁ֥ר חֶלְמָ֖א יְהֽוֹדְעֻנַּֽנִי
verses.translation: I gave an order to bring all the sages of Babylon before me to let me know the meaning of the dream.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:3] And from me [an order was issued] (וּמִנִּי֙): He [Nebuchadnezzar, writing in the first person] recounts the entire event that transpired. And it was written this way for the honor of Daniel, in that the king wrote this [account] in this manner [as a royal proclamation] to all the provinces of his kingdom. And [this constituted] a great glory/splendor (תִּפְאֶרֶת - tif'eret) for Israel.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֣יִן (עללין) עׇלִּ֗ין חַרְטֻמַּיָּא֙ אָֽשְׁפַיָּ֔א (כשדיא) כַּשְׂדָּאֵ֖י וְגָזְרַיָּ֑א וְחֶלְמָ֗א אָמַ֤ר אֲנָה֙ קֳדָ֣מֵיה֔וֹן וּפִשְׁרֵ֖הּ לָא־מְהוֹדְעִ֥ין לִֽי
verses.translation: The magicians, exorcists, Chaldeans, and diviners came, and I related the dream to them, but they could not make its meaning known to me.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:4] Then came in (בֵּאדַ֣יִן עׇלִּ֗ין): [Meaning,] they were coming in before him. I said (אָמַ֤ר אֲנָה֙): [Nebuchadnezzar uses the phrase] “I said” (אָמַ֤ר אֲנָה֙ - amar ana) and not [the standard conjugated past tense form] “I said” (אָמְרִית - amrit). For this [construction, using the Aramaic participle amar with the explicit pronoun ana] is also correct/valid in the language. And similar [examples in Hebrew showing valid structures where the pronoun is used explicitly alongside the verb or descriptor for emphasis or clarity] are: “I am the man who has seen affliction” (אֲנִ֤י הַגֶּ֙בֶר֙ רָאָ֣ה עֳנִ֔י - Ani ha-gever ra’ah oni) [from Lamentations 3:1, where the explicit pronoun “I” (Ani) begins the clause, linking the subject directly to the following descriptive phrase]; and [phrases such as] “and I remained/was left” (וְנֵֽאשְׁאַ֖ר אָ֑נִי - v'ne'eshar ani) [quoting Ezekiel 9:8, where the explicit pronoun “I” (ani) follows the standard inflected verb ne'eshar (“I remained”), reinforcing the subject], “for I am faint” (כִּֽי־אֻמְלַ֣ל אָ֑נִי - ki umlal ani) [quoting Psalms 6:3, where the descriptive adjective/participle “faint” (umlal) is followed by the explicit pronoun “I” (ani), directly linking the state to the subject]. Because it [the form amar ana, using the participle with the pronoun] serves to introduce [the speech/narrative] (כִּי הוּא פֶּתַח - ki hu petach).

verses.hebrew_full: וְעַ֣ד אׇחֳרֵ֡ין עַל֩ קׇֽדָמַ֨י דָּנִיֵּ֜אל דִּֽי־שְׁמֵ֤הּ בֵּלְטְשַׁאצַּר֙ כְּשֻׁ֣ם אֱלָהִ֔י וְדִ֛י רֽוּחַ־אֱלָהִ֥ין קַדִּישִׁ֖ין בֵּ֑הּ וְחֶלְמָ֖א קׇֽדָמ֥וֹהִי אַמְרֵֽת
verses.translation: Finally, Daniel, called Belteshazzar after the name of my god, in whom the spirit of the holy gods was, came to me, and I related the dream to him, [saying],
verses.rashi: Until, at last, until, at last, Daniel was brought in before me. like the name of my god The deity of Babylon was named Bel. Teshazzar is the name of an expression of wisdom in Aramaic.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:5] And finally (וְעַ֣ד אׇחֳרֵ֡ין): [The Aramaic word acharein means] last/finally (אַחֲרוֹן - acharon [in Hebrew]). And in whom [was] the spirit of the holy gods (וְדִ֛י רֽוּחַ־אֱלָהִ֥ין קַדִּישִׁ֖ין בֵּ֑הּ): [Ibn Ezra draws a conclusion from Nebuchadnezzar's statement:] If the spirit of holy gods is in him, he [Daniel] is holy.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּלְטְשַׁאצַּר֮ רַ֣ב חַרְטֻמַּיָּא֒ דִּ֣י אֲנָ֣ה יִדְעֵ֗ת דִּ֠י ר֣וּחַ אֱלָהִ֤ין קַדִּישִׁין֙ בָּ֔ךְ וְכׇל־רָ֖ז לָא־אָנֵ֣ס לָ֑ךְ חֶזְוֵ֨י חֶלְמִ֧י דִֽי־חֲזֵ֛ית וּפִשְׁרֵ֖הּ אֱמַֽר
verses.translation: “Belteshazzar, chief magician, in whom I know the spirit of the holy gods to be, and whom no mystery baffles, tell me the meaning of my dream vision that I have seen.
verses.rashi: and no secret is hidden from you No mystery is robbed [from you] or denied you.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:6] Belteshazzar, [...whom no mystery] baffles (לָא־אָנֵ֣ס): [The Aramaic phrase la anes lakh means] it will not overcome you (לֹא יְנַצֵּחֲךָ - lo y'natzechecha). And similar to it [using the related root א.נ.ס to mean force/compulsion] is [the phrase from Esther 1:8]: “no compulsion/forcing” (אֵ֣ין אֹנֵ֑ס - ein ones). [The root implies being] like: conquering (מְנַצֵּחַ - m'natzeach), compelling/forcing (מַכְרִיחַ - machriach).

verses.hebrew_full: וְחֶזְוֵ֥י רֵאשִׁ֖י עַֽל־מִשְׁכְּבִ֑י חָזֵ֣ה הֲוֵ֔ית וַאֲל֥וּ אִילָ֛ן בְּג֥וֹא אַרְעָ֖א וְרוּמֵ֥הּ שַׂגִּֽיא
verses.translation: In the visions of my mind in bedI saw a tree of great height in the midst of the earth;
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:7] And the visions (וְחֶזְוֵ֥י)... a tree (אִילָ֛ן): [The Aramaic word ilan means] a tree (עֵץ - etz) from within the earth.

verses.hebrew_full: רְבָ֥ה אִֽילָנָ֖א וּתְקִ֑ף וְרוּמֵהּ֙ יִמְטֵ֣א לִשְׁמַיָּ֔א וַחֲזוֹתֵ֖הּ לְס֥וֹף כׇּל־אַרְעָֽא
verses.translation: The tree grew and became mighty;Its top reached heaven,And it was visible to the ends of the earth.
verses.rashi: The tree grew and became strong The tree grew and became stronger and stronger. reached the sky [as translated,] reached the sky.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:8] Grew (רְבָ֥ה): [Meaning, it] grew great (גָּדַל - gadal). Reached (יִמְטֵ֣א): [Meaning, it] reached (יַגִּיעַ - yagia). And its visibility (וַחֲזוֹתֵ֖הּ): [Meaning] its branches/foliage (שֶׂעֲפָיו - se'apayiv). [Ibn Ezra interprets the tree's visibility as stemming from its extensive branches/foliage]. And similar to it [using the root ח.ז.ה related to vision/appearance] is: “a conspicuous horn” (קֶרֶן חָזוּת - keren chazut) [found in Daniel 8:5], because it [the horn] has the likeness of branches/foliage (שֶׁיֵּשׁ לוֹ כִּדְמוּת שְׂעִיפִים - sheyesh lo kidmut s'ifim). [Ibn Ezra interprets keren chazut not just as 'visible' but as prominent or conspicuous like spreading branches, linking the concept of visibility/appearance in both verses to the idea of branching structure].

verses.hebrew_full: עׇפְיֵ֤הּ שַׁפִּיר֙ וְאִנְבֵּ֣הּ שַׂגִּ֔יא וּמָז֨וֹן לְכֹ֖לָּא־בֵ֑הּ תְּחֹת֜וֹהִי תַּטְלֵ֣ל חֵיוַ֣ת בָּרָ֗א וּבְעַנְפ֙וֹהִי֙ (ידרון) יְדוּרָן֙ צִפְּרֵ֣י שְׁמַיָּ֔א וּמִנֵּ֖הּ יִתְּזִ֥ין כׇּל־בִּשְׂרָֽא
verses.translation: Its foliage was beautifulAnd its fruit abundant;There was food for all in it.Beneath it the beasts of the field found shade,And the birds of the sky dwelt on its branches;All creatures fed on it.
verses.rashi: its branches were beautiful [as translated,] its branches were beautiful. and its fruit was plentiful [as translated,] and its fruit was plentiful. and on it was sustenance for all On it was enough sustenance for all took shade Aram. תַּטְלֵל, an expression of shade. dwelt Aram. יְדֻרָן, an expression of dwelling and lodging. was nourished Aram. יִתְּזִין, an expression of food.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:9] Its foliage (עׇפְיֵ֤הּ): Yefet [ben Ali] says [this means] its branches (עֲנָפָיו - anafayv). And similar to it [using a related word for foliage/branches] is: “from among the foliage (עֳפָאיִם) they give voice” [Psalms 104:12]. He [Yefet] also interpreted “and its visibility” (vachazoteh, from the previous verse [Daniel 4:8]) as its thickness/density (עֳבִיּוֹ - ovyo), and similarly [interpreted] “a conspicuous horn” (keren chazut, from Daniel 8:5) [in terms of its thickness or prominence rather than branching structure as Ibn Ezra preferred]. And some [other commentators] say that “its foliage” (ofyeh) means its leaves (עָלָיו - alav). And its fruit (וְאִנְבֵּ֣הּ): [Means] and its fruit (וּפִרְיוֹ - u’piryo). Found shade (תַּטְלֵ֣ל): [Meaning] like shade/shadow (כִּדְמוּת צֵל - kidmut tzel). [From the root צ.ל.ל - shade]. And on its branches (וּבְעַנְפ֙וֹהִי֙): [Means] its branches (עֲנָפָיו - anafayv). Birds (צִפְּרֵ֣י): [Means] fowl/birds (עוֹף - oph). Fed (יִתְּזִ֥ין): [This word] is derived from [the word/root for] food (מִגִּזְרַת מָזוֹן - mi-gizrat mazon).

verses.hebrew_full: חָזֵ֥ה הֲוֵ֛ית בְּחֶזְוֵ֥י רֵאשִׁ֖י עַֽל־מִשְׁכְּבִ֑י וַאֲלוּ֙ עִ֣יר וְקַדִּ֔ישׁ מִן־שְׁמַיָּ֖א נָחִֽת
verses.translation: In the vision of my mind in bed, I looked and saw a holy Watcher coming down from heaven—
verses.rashi: I saw lit. I was seeing. and behold a wakeful holy one i.e., an angel who is forever awake and holy.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:10] Watcher (עִיר): [Means] an angel (מַלְאָךְ - mal'ach). And Rabbi Moshe [ibn Gikatilla?] said: that it is not a body, and it is always awake (עֵר - er) and does not sleep [connecting the Aramaic word ir (watcher) etymologically to the Hebrew er (awake)]. Coming down (נָחִֽת): [Means] descended (יָרַד - yarad). And similar to it [using the related Hebrew root נ.ח.ת which implies downward action] is [the phrase from Psalms 65:11]: “leveling its ridges” (נַחֵ֣ת גְּדוּדֶ֑הָ - nachet gedudeha). [Although meaning 'leveling' here, it demonstrates the root's connection to downward movement, akin to the descent implied by nachit].

verses.hebrew_full: קָרֵ֨א בְחַ֜יִל וְכֵ֣ן אָמַ֗ר גֹּ֤דּוּ אִֽילָנָא֙ וְקַצִּ֣צוּ עַנְפ֔וֹהִי אַתַּ֥רוּ עׇפְיֵ֖הּ וּבַדַּ֣רוּ אִנְבֵּ֑הּ תְּנֻ֤ד חֵֽיוְתָא֙ מִן־תַּחְתּ֔וֹהִי וְצִפְּרַיָּ֖א מִן־עַנְפֽוֹהִי
verses.translation: who called loudly and said:‘Hew down the tree, lop off its branches,Strip off its foliage, scatter its fruit.Let the beasts of the field flee from beneath itAnd the birds from its branches,
verses.rashi: Crying out loudly crying with might. Cut down the tree...shake off its branches shake off and cause its branches to fall. shake off its branches shake off and cause its branches to fall. and scatter its fruit [as translated,] and scatter its fruit. the beasts shall wander away from beneath it [as translated,] the beasts shall wander away from beneath it.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:11] Called loudly (קָרֵ֨א בְחַ֜יִל): [Means called] with strength/power (בְּכֹחַ - be-khoach). And lop off (וְקַצִּ֣צוּ): [Means] cut/hew it down (כְּרוֹתוּ - k'rotu). Strip off (אַתַּ֥רוּ): [Means] cause to fall/cast down (הַפִּילוּ - happilu). Scatter (וּבַדַּ֣רוּ): [Means] scatter/disperse (הָפִיצוּ - hafitzu) or destroy (הַשְׁחִיתוּ - hashchitu). Flee (תְּנֻ֤ד): [From the root meaning to wander/move restlessly, as in the phrase used regarding Cain in Genesis 4:12, Genesis 4:14]: “moving and wandering” (נָע וָנָד - na va-nad).

verses.hebrew_full: בְּרַ֨ם עִקַּ֤ר שׇׁרְשׁ֙וֹהִי֙ בְּאַרְעָ֣א שְׁבֻ֔קוּ וּבֶֽאֱסוּר֙ דִּֽי־פַרְזֶ֣ל וּנְחָ֔שׁ בְּדִתְאָ֖א דִּ֣י בָרָ֑א וּבְטַ֤ל שְׁמַיָּא֙ יִצְטַבַּ֔ע וְעִם־חֵיוְתָ֥א חֲלָקֵ֖הּ בַּעֲשַׂ֥ב אַרְעָֽא
verses.translation: But leave the stump with its roots in the ground.In fetters of iron and bronzeIn the grass of the field,Let him be drenched with the dew of heaven,And share earth’s verdure with the beasts.
verses.rashi: But leave its main roots in the ground but its main roots leave over. and in fetters of iron and copper i.e., place his feet in iron fetters as is done to a horse that is left in the meadow; so shall you chain him with fetters. in the grass Aram. בְדִתְאָא in the grass of the field, i.e., in the meadow, so that he should not move from there. it shall be drenched Aram. יִצְטַבַּע, it shall always be drenched, an expression of immersion. its lot [as translated,] its lot.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:12 (4:9 Heb)] But (בְּרַם): [Means] however/but (אַךְ - akh). Leave the stump of its roots in the ground (עִקַּ֤ר שׇׁרְש֙וֹהִי֙ בְּאַרְעָ֣א שְׁבֻ֔קוּ): [Meaning] Leave the stump of its roots in the earth, and let it stand among the grasses and among the vegetation (בֵּין הָעֲשָׂבִים וּבֵין הַדְּשָׁאִים - bein ha-asavim u-vein ha-desha'im). [This state of being left in the field, bound,] is like [the description of a captive king being] “bound... in fetters of bronze” (אָסוּר בַּנְחֻשְׁתַּיִם - asur ba-n'chushtayim) [cf. II Kings 25:7, Jeremiah 39:7, Jeremiah 52:11 regarding Zedekiah; II Chronicles 36:6 regarding Jehoiakim]. And let him be drenched with the dew (וּבְטַ֤ל ... יִצְטַבַּ֔ע): [The word yitztaba means] he will be saturated/drenched (יִרְוֶה - yirveh).

verses.hebrew_full: לִבְבֵהּ֙ מִן־אֲנָשָׁ֣א (אנושא) יְשַׁנּ֔וֹן וּלְבַ֥ב חֵיוָ֖ה יִתְיְהִ֣ב לֵ֑הּ וְשִׁבְעָ֥ה עִדָּנִ֖ין יַחְלְפ֥וּן עֲלֽוֹהִי
verses.translation: Let his mind be altered from that of a human,And let him be given the mind of a beast,And let seven seasons pass over him.
verses.rashi: and seven periods seven years. will pass over him will pass over him with this decree.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:13 (4:10 Heb)] His mind (לִבְבֵהּ֙): [Means] his heart/mind (לִבּוֹ - libbo) will be changed from [that of] a human (מִלֵּב אֱנוֹשׁ יְשַׁנּוּ - mi-lev enosh yeshannu). Pass over (יַחְלְפ֥וּן): [Meaning] they will pass (יַעַבְרוּ - ya'avru). Like [the usage in Song of Songs 2:11]: “the rain is over and gone” (הַגֶּשֶׁם חָלַף הָלַךְ לוֹ - ha-geshem chalaf halach lo).

verses.hebrew_full: בִּגְזֵרַ֤ת עִירִין֙ פִּתְגָמָ֔א וּמֵאמַ֥ר קַדִּישִׁ֖ין שְׁאֵֽלְתָ֑א עַד־דִּבְרַ֡ת דִּ֣י יִנְדְּע֣וּן חַ֠יַּיָּ֠א דִּֽי־שַׁלִּ֨יט (עליא) עִלָּאָ֜ה בְּמַלְכ֣וּת (אנושא) אֲנָשָׁ֗א וּלְמַן־דִּ֤י יִצְבֵּא֙ יִתְּנִנַּ֔הּ וּשְׁפַ֥ל אֲנָשִׁ֖ים יְקִ֥ים (עליה) עֲלַֽהּ
verses.translation: This sentence is decreed by the Watchers;This verdict is commanded by the Holy OnesSo that all creatures may knowThat the Most High is sovereign over human dominion,And gives it out at will,Perhaps setting over it even the lowest of human beings.’
verses.rashi: the edict a decree, but since they take counsel from the Holy One, blessed be He, before they issue the decree, he calls it a question. in order that Aram. עַד דִּבְרַת, like לְמַעַן. that the living should know in order that all the living should know. and to whom He wishes [as translated,] and to whom He wishes.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:14] By the decree (בִּגְזֵרַ֤ת): Thus they [the Watchers] decreed, for they are the ministers of God (מְשָׁרְתֵי הַשֵּׁם - m'shar'tei Hashem), [acting] as He commanded them. And “this is the command of the holy ones” (וּמֵאמַ֥ר קַדִּישִׁ֖ין שְׁאֵֽלְתָ֑א). And the question is, why this [decree]? [The verse answers:] “So that the living may know” (עַד־דִּבְרַ֡ת דִּ֣י יִנְדְּע֣וּן חַ֠יַּיָּ֠א) that the Most High (הָעֶלְיוֹן - Ha-Elyon), who is God (הַשֵּׁם - Hashem), “is sovereign over the lowly kingdom of men” (שַׁלִּ֨יט ... בְּמַלְכ֣וּת אֲנָשָׁ֗א הַשְּׁפֵלָה - shallit... b'malchut anasha ha-sh'felah), “and He gives it to whom He wishes, and sets up over it the lowest of men” (וּלְמַן־דִּ֤י יִצְבֵּא֙ יִתְּנִנַּ֔הּ וּשְׁפַ֥ל אֲנָשִׁ֖ים יְקִ֥ים עֲלַֽהּ).

verses.hebrew_full: דְּנָה֙ חֶלְמָ֣א חֲזֵ֔ית אֲנָ֖ה מַלְכָּ֣א נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֑ר (ואנתה) וְאַ֨נְתְּ בֵּלְטְשַׁאצַּ֜ר פִּשְׁרֵ֣א אֱמַ֗ר כׇּל־קֳבֵל֙ דִּ֣י כׇּל־חַכִּימֵ֣י מַלְכוּתִ֗י לָֽא־יָכְלִ֤ין פִּשְׁרָא֙ לְהוֹדָ֣עוּתַ֔נִי (ואנתה) וְאַ֣נְתְּ כָּהֵ֔ל דִּ֛י רֽוּחַ־אֱלָהִ֥ין קַדִּישִׁ֖ין בָּֽךְ
verses.translation: “I, King Nebuchadnezzar, had this dream; now you, Belteshazzar, tell me its meaning, since all the sages of my kingdom are not able to make its meaning known to me, but you are able, for the spirit of the holy gods is in you.”
verses.rashi: its interpretation [as translated,] its interpretation. but you can [as translated,] but you can.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:15] This (דְּנָה֙): [Meaning, Nebuchadnezzar is saying:] Thus I spoke to Daniel (כָּךְ אָמַרְתִּי לְדָנִיֵּאל - kach amarti l'Daniel).

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: אֱדַ֨יִן דָּֽנִיֵּ֜אל דִּֽי־שְׁמֵ֣הּ בֵּלְטְשַׁאצַּ֗ר אֶשְׁתּוֹמַם֙ כְּשָׁעָ֣ה חֲדָ֔ה וְרַעְיֹנֹ֖הִי יְבַהֲלֻנֵּ֑הּ עָנֵ֨ה מַלְכָּ֜א וְאָמַ֗ר בֵּלְטְשַׁאצַּר֙ חֶלְמָ֤א וּפִשְׁרֵא֙ אַֽל־יְבַהֲלָ֔ךְ עָנֵ֤ה בֵלְטְשַׁאצַּר֙ וְאָמַ֔ר מָרִ֕אי חֶלְמָ֥א (לשנאיך) לְשָֽׂנְאָ֖ךְ וּפִשְׁרֵ֥הּ (לעריך) לְעָרָֽךְ
