verses.translation: Then Daniel, called Belteshazzar, was perplexed for a while, and alarmed by his thoughts. The king addressed him, “Let the dream and its meaning not alarm you.” Belteshazzar replied, “My lord, would that the dream were for your enemy and its meaning for your foe!
verses.rashi: Then Daniel, whose name is Belteshazzar, was bewildered He was silent. and his thoughts [as translated,] and his thoughts. alarmed him for he was afraid to interpret the dream for him. My lord Aram. מָרִאי. Our Rabbis said (Shevu. 35b): “My Lord, may the dream be for Your enemies,” refers to God. He raised his eyes to the Holy One, blessed be He, and said, “May this dream be fulfilled upon this enemy of Yours.” But if you say that he said it to Nebuchadnezzar, was not Israel his enemy? Is it possible that he would curse them? for your foes Aram. לְעָרָךְ, like (Sam. 28:16): “and has become [the supporter of] your foes (עָרֶךָ).”
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:16] Then... was perplexed (אֱדַ֨יִן... אֶשְׁתּוֹמַם֙): [Meaning] He was stunned/perplexed (הִשְׁתּוֹמֵם - hishtomem) for one moment/hour (שָׁעָה אַחַת - sha'ah achat). My lord (מָרִ֕אי): [Means] my lord (אֲדוֹנִי - adoni). For your foe (לְעָרָֽךְ): [The word arakh means "your foe," like the Hebrew word עָר (ar - foe/enemy)]. And this [phrase, "would that the dream were for your enemy... and its meaning for your foe"] is the proper etiquette (דֶּרֶךְ מוּסָר - derech musar) [when speaking] with the king. And some say that his [Daniel's] heart [in saying this] was [directed] towards God [i.e., wishing the evil away entirely], but there is no need [for such an interpretation], for it is correct according to its plain meaning.

verses.hebrew_full: אִֽילָנָא֙ דִּ֣י חֲזַ֔יְתָ דִּ֥י רְבָ֖ה וּתְקִ֑ף וְרוּמֵהּ֙ יִמְטֵ֣א לִשְׁמַיָּ֔א וַחֲזוֹתֵ֖הּ לְכׇל־אַרְעָֽא
verses.translation: The tree that you saw grow and become mighty, whose top reached heaven, which was visible throughout the earth,
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:17] The tree (אִֽילָנָא֙): Rabbi Yeshua [ben Yehuda] said: that “and its visibility” (vachazoteh, from Daniel 4:8) is from the root/language (מִלָּשׁוֹן - mi-lashon) of “vision/see” (chazah - חָזָה), meaning it is visible/seen (שֶׁהוּא נִרְאֶה - shehu nir'eh). And similarly [he explains] “a conspicuous horn” (keren chazut) [from Daniel 8:5] derives its meaning [from being easily seen]. [However, Ibn Ezra challenges this interpretation:] And if so, how will he [Rabbi Yeshua] explain [the phrase in Daniel 8:8]: “and four chazut came up in its place” (וַתַּעֲלֶ֛ינָה חָז֥וּת אַרְבַּ֖ע תַּחְתֶּֽיהָ - vata'alena chazut arba tachteha)? [Ibn Ezra implies that if chazut merely means 'visible,' then 'four visibles' coming up in place of the horn doesn't make clear sense, suggesting chazut must carry a more substantial meaning related to the horn/branch structure itself, supporting Ibn Ezra's own earlier interpretation in his commentary on Daniel 4:8].

verses.hebrew_full: וְעׇפְיֵ֤הּ שַׁפִּיר֙ וְאִנְבֵּ֣הּ שַׂגִּ֔יא וּמָז֨וֹן לְכֹ֖לָּא־בֵ֑הּ תְּחֹת֗וֹהִי תְּדוּר֙ חֵיוַ֣ת בָּרָ֔א וּבְעַנְפ֕וֹהִי יִשְׁכְּנָ֖ן צִפְּרֵ֥י שְׁמַיָּֽא
verses.translation: whose foliage was beautiful, whose fruit was so abundant that there was food for all in it, beneath which the beasts of the field dwelt, and in whose branches the birds of the sky lodged—
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:18] And its foliage (וְעׇפְיֵ֤הּ): [Regarding the beginning of the verse, "And its foliage..."] And the verse following it [i.e., this entire verse, which repeats the description from Daniel 4:9] does not need explanation (אֵין צָרִיךְ פֵּרוּשׁ - ein tzarich perush).

verses.hebrew_full: (אנתה) אַנְתְּ־ה֣וּא מַלְכָּ֔א דִּ֥י (רבית) רְבַ֖ת וּתְקֵ֑פְתְּ וּרְבוּתָ֤ךְ רְבָת֙ וּמְטָ֣ת לִשְׁמַיָּ֔א וְשׇׁלְטָנָ֖ךְ לְס֥וֹף אַרְעָֽא
verses.translation: it is you, O king, you who have grown and become mighty, whose greatness has grown to reach heaven, and whose dominion is to the end of the earth.

verses.hebrew_full: וְדִ֣י חֲזָ֣ה מַלְכָּ֡א עִ֣יר וְקַדִּ֣ישׁ נָחִ֣ת מִן־שְׁמַיָּ֡א וְאָמַר֩ גֹּ֨דּוּ אִֽילָנָ֜א וְחַבְּל֗וּהִי בְּרַ֨ם עִקַּ֤ר שׇׁרְשׁ֙וֹהִי֙ בְּאַרְעָ֣א שְׁבֻ֔קוּ וּבֶאֱסוּר֙ דִּֽי־פַרְזֶ֣ל וּנְחָ֔שׁ בְּדִתְאָ֖א דִּ֣י בָרָ֑א וּבְטַ֧ל שְׁמַיָּ֣א יִצְטַבַּ֗ע וְעִם־חֵיוַ֤ת בָּרָא֙ חֲלָקֵ֔הּ עַ֛ד דִּֽי־שִׁבְעָ֥ה עִדָּנִ֖ין יַחְלְפ֥וּן עֲלֽוֹהִי
verses.translation: The holy Watcher whom the king saw descend from heaven and say,Hew down the tree and destroy it,But leave the stump with its roots in the ground.In fetters of iron and bronzeIn the grass of the field,Let him be drenched with the dew of heaven,And share the lot of the beasts of the fieldUntil seven seasons pass over him—

verses.hebrew_full: דְּנָ֥ה פִשְׁרָ֖א מַלְכָּ֑א וּגְזֵרַ֤ת (עליא) עִלָּאָה֙ הִ֔יא דִּ֥י מְטָ֖ת עַל־מָרִ֥אי מַלְכָּֽא
verses.translation: this is its meaning, O king; it is the decree of the Most High that has overtaken my lord the king.
verses.rashi: which befalls my lord, the king [as translated,] which befalls my lord, the king.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:21 (4:18 Heb)] This (דְּנָ֥ה): [Meaning:] This (hu) is the interpretation (ha-pitaron) as it will now be told to you (ka'asher ne'emar lach). And the explanation/meaning of [the concept of offering counsel, anticipating Daniel's statement “let my counsel...” (milki) in Daniel 4:24] is similar to [the usage describing internal deliberation in Nehemiah 5:7]: “and my heart took counsel within me” (וַיִּמָּלֵךְ לִבִּי עָלַי - vayimmalech libbi alai).

verses.hebrew_full: וְלָ֣ךְ טָֽרְדִ֣ין מִן־אֲנָשָׁ֡א וְעִם־חֵיוַ֣ת בָּרָא֩ לֶהֱוֵ֨ה מְדֹרָ֜ךְ וְעִשְׂבָּ֥א כְתוֹרִ֣ין לָ֣ךְ יְטַֽעֲמ֗וּן וּמִטַּ֤ל שְׁמַיָּא֙ לָ֣ךְ מְצַבְּעִ֔ין וְשִׁבְעָ֥ה עִדָּנִ֖ין יַחְלְפ֣וּן (עליך) עֲלָ֑ךְ עַ֣ד דִּֽי־תִנְדַּ֗ע דִּֽי־שַׁלִּ֤יט (עליא) עִלָּאָה֙ בְּמַלְכ֣וּת אֲנָשָׁ֔א וּלְמַן־דִּ֥י יִצְבֵּ֖א יִתְּנִנַּֽהּ
verses.translation: You will be driven away from society and have your habitation with the beasts of the field. You will be fed grass like cattle, and be drenched with the dew of heaven; seven seasons will pass over you until you come to know that the Most High is sovereign over human dominion, and gives it out at will.
verses.rashi: And they will banish you from mankind [as translated,] and they will banish you from mankind. and with the dew of the heavens you shall be drenched They will immerse you. and seven periods shall pass over you We may say that this is the recompense for the Temple, which he destroyed, which was built in seven years.

verses.hebrew_full: וְדִ֣י אֲמַ֗רוּ לְמִשְׁבַּ֞ק עִקַּ֤ר שׇׁרְשׁ֙וֹהִי֙ דִּ֣י אִֽילָנָ֔א מַלְכוּתָ֖ךְ לָ֣ךְ קַיָּמָ֑א מִן־דִּ֣י תִנְדַּ֔ע דִּ֥י שַׁלִּטִ֖ן שְׁמַיָּֽא
verses.translation: And the meaning of the command to leave the stump of the tree with its roots is that the kingdom will remain yours from the time you come to know that Heaven is sovereign.
verses.rashi: And what they said, etc. What they said—to leave the roots of the tree—this is the interpretation: מַלְכוּתָךְ, your kingdom—will remain yours. Your kingdom will ultimately be restored to you. as soon as you know that Heaven rules as soon as you know that the heavenly beings rule over the world.

verses.hebrew_full: לָהֵ֣ן מַלְכָּ֗א מִלְכִּי֙ יִשְׁפַּ֣ר (עליך) עֲלָ֔ךְ (וחטיך) וַחֲטָאָךְ֙ בְּצִדְקָ֣ה פְרֻ֔ק וַעֲוָיָתָ֖ךְ בְּמִחַ֣ן עֲנָ֑יִן הֵ֛ן תֶּהֱוֵ֥ה אַרְכָ֖ה לִשְׁלֵוְתָֽךְ
verses.translation: Therefore, O king, may my advice be acceptable to you: Redeem your sins by beneficence and your iniquities by generosity to the poor; then your serenity may be extended.”
verses.rashi: Indeed, O king Indeed, O king, hearken to my counsel. my counsel Aram. מִלְכִּי, my counsel, like (Num. 24:14): “I shall advise you (אִיעָצְךָ),” which is translated: אֲמַלְּכִינָךְ. please you May my counsel please you that you hearken to my words. and with charity you will remove your sin With charity, break your sin off your neck. and your iniquity by showing mercy to poor and remove your iniquities by showing mercy to the poor. perhaps your tranquility will last Perhaps there will be time for your tranquility, that the evil should not come swiftly. Now what did Daniel see to give Nebuchadnezzar good advice? He saw the Jews poor and humbled by the exile, going from door to door begging for alms, and he gave him advice to deal mercifully with them. He said to him, “These poor people whom you exiled are hungry; nourish them.” And so he did. He opened his storehouses and fed them for a full twelve months.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:24] Redeem (פְרֻק): [Means] redeem (גְּאַל - g'al). Like [the phrase from Psalms 136:24 (and commonly found in the Passover Haggadah)]: “And He redeemed us from our enemies” (וַיִּפְרְקֵנוּ מִצָּרֵינוּ - vayifrǝqenu mitzareinu). By generosity to the poor (בְּמִחַן עֲנָיִן): [Meaning] by showing favor/grace to the poor (לָחוֹן עֲנִיִּים - lachon aniyim) [connecting the Aramaic michan to the Hebrew root ח.נ.נ - favor/grace]. Then your serenity may be extended (הֵן תֶּהֱוֵה אַרְכָה לִשְׁלֵוְתָךְ): [Meaning] Perhaps there will be a lengthening (אֲרִיכוּת - arichut) for your serenity. Or [it could be understood] like [the concept of] healing/restoration (אֲרוּכָה - arukah) for your error (לִשְׁגָגָתְךָ - lishgagatecha). [Ibn Ezra draws a parallel between arkah (lengthening/extension) and arukah (healing, cf. Jeremiah 8:22, Nehemiah 4:1), suggesting the extension of peace is akin to a healing or remedy for the error that caused the decree].

verses.hebrew_full: כֹּ֣לָּא מְּטָ֔א עַל־נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֖ר מַלְכָּֽא
verses.translation: All this befell King Nebuchadnezzar.
verses.rashi: All this befell The entire interpretation befell Nebuchadnezzar.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:25] All (כֹּלָּא): [Meaning] Everything (ha-kol) came and befell [him]. Thus it is written in the book [of Daniel]. And do not be astonished that it did not write "[All this befell] me" (עָלַי - alai), but rather "upon Nebuchadnezzar." [This shift from first to third person is acceptable], like [in I Samuel 12:11]: “And the Lord sent Jerubbaal, and Bedan, and Jephthah, and Samuel,” and he [Samuel] is the one speaking/narrating (וְהוּא הַמְסַפֵּר - v'hu ha-m'sapper). [Samuel refers to himself in the third person within his own speech]. And similarly [consider the phrase] “he was walking” (מְהַלֵּךְ הֲוָה - m'halekh hava) [found later in Daniel 4:26 describing Nebuchadnezzar's actions; although the narrative has shifted to third person, this participial form is used]. Also [consider] “the king answered” (עָנֵה מַלְכָּא - aneh malka) [from Daniel 4:16], which is as if he had said “I answered” (עָנִיתִי - aniti). [Even within Nebuchadnezzar's first-person account, third-person narrative descriptions like "the king answered" appear]. And similarly [the phrase] “in the mouth of the king” (בְּפוּם מַלְכָּא - b'fum malka) [in Daniel 4:28]. [These examples show that transitions between first and third-person perspectives, or using third-person descriptions within a first-person frame, are normal narrative techniques].

words.eng: subst·Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: לִקְצָ֥ת יַרְחִ֖ין תְּרֵֽי־עֲשַׂ֑ר עַל־הֵיכַ֧ל מַלְכוּתָ֛א דִּ֥י בָבֶ֖ל מְהַלֵּ֥ךְ הֲוָֽה
verses.translation: Twelve months later, as he was walking on the roof of the royal palace at Babylon,
verses.rashi: he was walking [as translated,] he was walking.

words.eng: the Twelve — name of twelve prophetical books (Hosea

verses.hebrew_full: עָנֵ֤ה מַלְכָּא֙ וְאָמַ֔ר הֲלָ֥א דָא־הִ֖יא בָּבֶ֣ל רַבְּתָ֑א דִּֽי־אֲנָ֤ה בֱנַיְתַהּ֙ לְבֵ֣ית מַלְכ֔וּ בִּתְקָ֥ף חִסְנִ֖י וְלִיקָ֥ר הַדְרִֽי
verses.translation: the king exclaimed, “There is great Babylon, which I have built by my vast power to be a royal residence for the glory of my majesty!”
verses.rashi: Is this not He heard poor people coming to the door and crying out. He said, “What is the sound of this multitude in my ears?” His servants said to him, “These are the poor for whom you arranged a time to feed.” He said, “Is this not the great Babylon, etc. If I had squandered my storehouses, from where would I have built all these palaces? From now on, I shall not feed them.” Rabbi Tanhuma (Mishpatim 4) explained it in this manner. and for the honor of my glory [as translated,] and for the honor of my glory.

words.eng: to be or become king or queen

verses.hebrew_full: ע֗וֹד מִלְּתָא֙ בְּפֻ֣ם מַלְכָּ֔א קָ֖ל מִן־שְׁמַיָּ֣א נְפַ֑ל לָ֤ךְ אָֽמְרִין֙ נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֣ר מַלְכָּ֔א מַלְכוּתָ֖א עֲדָ֥ת מִנָּֽךְ
verses.translation: The words were still on the king’s lips, when a voice fell from heaven, “It has been decreed for you, O King Nebuchadnezzar: The kingdom has passed out of your hands.
verses.rashi: has turned away from you [as translated,] has turned away from you.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:28] Passed (עֲדָת): [Meaning] It has turned aside (סָרָה - sarah) and passed away (וְעָבְרָה - v'avrah) from you. I have no knowledge/understanding to know what these “seven seasons” (shiv'ah idanin) are – whether days, or weeks, or months, or years? But for them to be days or weeks is not plausible, because it is written [that this state lasted long enough that] “until his hair grew like eagles’ [feathers]” [Daniel 4:30]. And the Gaon [Saadia Gaon] said that they are months. However, according to my opinion, they are years, like [the usage in] “a time, times, and half a time” (idan v'idanin u-flag idan) [found in Daniel 7:25; Daniel 12:7]. And let it not enter your mind that Nebuchadnezzar [physically] became a male or female animal, for it is only written that he would “dwell with the beasts” and “eat grass like them.” The meaning of [being given] “the mind of a beast” [Daniel 4:13] is that his intellect/reason (da'ato) – which is the intellect of the [human] soul (da'at ha-neshamah) – would depart. And behold, when the wisdom of the soul departs, his mind reverts and remains like the mind of a beast. The proof for all this is what he [Nebuchadnezzar] says at the end: “I lifted my eyes to heaven, and my understanding returned to me” (mandəʿî yəṯûḇ ʿălay) [Daniel 4:31]. And I saw [i.e., knew] one of our colleagues, trustworthy in spirit, who told me that he was on an island, which is Sardinia. And behold, an uncircumcised man [i.e., a non-Jew] went out and fled from his parents because his reason had departed and he went mad. He was with the deer for many years, and he walked on his feet and his hands like them. Now, the king of the island went to hunt game and captured many deer, and the mad, uncircumcised man, thought to be a deer, was [also] captured. His parents came and recognized him. They spoke to him, but he did not answer. They placed bread before him to eat and wine to drink, but he refused. They gave him grasses with the deer, and he ate. And in the middle of the night, he escaped back to the deer of the field. [This anecdote illustrates that a human can lose reason and adopt animalistic behaviors without undergoing physical transformation].

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִן־אֲנָשָׁא֩ לָ֨ךְ טָֽרְדִ֜ין וְֽעִם־חֵיוַ֧ת בָּרָ֣א מְדֹרָ֗ךְ עִשְׂבָּ֤א כְתוֹרִין֙ לָ֣ךְ יְטַעֲמ֔וּן וְשִׁבְעָ֥ה עִדָּנִ֖ין יַחְלְפ֣וּן (עליך) עֲלָ֑ךְ עַ֣ד דִּֽי־תִנְדַּ֗ע דִּֽי־שַׁלִּ֤יט (עליא) עִלָּאָה֙ בְּמַלְכ֣וּת אֲנָשָׁ֔א וּלְמַן־דִּ֥י יִצְבֵּ֖א יִתְּנִנַּֽהּ
verses.translation: You are being driven away from society, and your habitation is to be with the beasts of the field. You are to be fed grass like cattle, and seven seasons will pass over you until you come to know that the Most High is sovereign over human dominion and gives it out at will.”
verses.rashi: they will banish Aram. טָרְדִין. an expression of driving away.

verses.hebrew_full: בַּהּ־שַׁעֲתָ֗א מִלְּתָא֮ סָ֣פַת עַל־נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּר֒ וּמִן־אֲנָשָׁ֣א טְרִ֔יד וְעִשְׂבָּ֤א כְתוֹרִין֙ יֵאכֻ֔ל וּמִטַּ֥ל שְׁמַיָּ֖א גִּשְׁמֵ֣הּ יִצְטַבַּ֑ע עַ֣ד דִּ֥י שַׂעְרֵ֛הּ כְּנִשְׁרִ֥ין רְבָ֖ה וְטִפְר֥וֹהִי כְצִפְּרִֽין
verses.translation: There and then the sentence was carried out upon Nebuchadnezzar. He was driven away from society, he ate grass like cattle, and his body was drenched with the dew of heaven until his hair grew like eagle’s [feathers] and his nails like [the talons of] birds.
verses.rashi: was fulfilled The decree was executed upon him. his hair grew like [the feathers of] eagles His hair grew over his entire body like eagles’ feathers. and his nails like birds and his nails were like a bird’s [talons].

words.eng: meaning ‘in her’·to look at or to
subst·Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: וְלִקְצָ֣ת יֽוֹמַיָּא֩ אֲנָ֨ה נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֜ר עַיְנַ֣י לִשְׁמַיָּ֣א נִטְלֵ֗ת וּמַנְדְּעִי֙ עֲלַ֣י יְת֔וּב (ולעליא) וּלְעִלָּאָה֙ בָּרְכֵ֔ת וּלְחַ֥י עָלְמָ֖א שַׁבְּחֵ֣ת וְהַדְּרֵ֑ת דִּ֤י שׇׁלְטָנֵהּ֙ שׇׁלְטָ֣ן עָלַ֔ם וּמַלְכוּתֵ֖הּ עִם־דָּ֥ר וְדָֽר
verses.translation: “When the time had passed, I, Nebuchadnezzar, lifted my eyes to heaven, and my reason was restored to me. I blessed the Most High, and praised and glorified the Ever-Living One,Whose dominion is an everlasting dominionAnd whose kingdom endures throughout the generations.
verses.rashi: And at the end of the days at the end of the days that were set for him; at the end of the seven years. raised [as translated,] raised. and my understanding was restored to me [as translated,] and my understanding was restored to me. and I blessed the Most High [as translated,] and I blessed the Most High.

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: וְכׇל־דָּיְרֵ֤י (דארי) אַרְעָא֙ כְּלָ֣ה חֲשִׁיבִ֔ין וּֽכְמִצְבְּיֵ֗הּ עָבֵד֙ בְּחֵ֣יל שְׁמַיָּ֔א (ודארי) וְדָיְרֵ֖י אַרְעָ֑א וְלָ֤א אִיתַי֙ דִּֽי־יְמַחֵ֣א בִידֵ֔הּ וְיֵ֥אמַר לֵ֖הּ מָ֥ה עֲבַֽדְתְּ
verses.translation: All the inhabitants of the earth are of no account.[God] deals at will with the host of heaven,And with the inhabitants of the earth.There is none to stay God’s handOr to ask, ‘What have You done?’
verses.rashi: are reckoned as nothing Aram. כְּלָה. Our Rabbis said (Yoma 20b): The dust of the sun is called לָא i.e., the dust that appears in the sun. and according to His will, He does with the host of heaven [as translated,] and according to His will, He does with the host of heaven. and no one can stop His hand Aram. וְלָא אִיתַי דִּי יְמַחֵא בִידֵהּ. And there is none who will stop His hand.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּהּ־זִמְנָ֞א מַנְדְּעִ֣י יְת֣וּב עֲלַ֗י וְלִיקַ֨ר מַלְכוּתִ֜י הַדְרִ֤י וְזִיוִי֙ יְת֣וּב עֲלַ֔י וְלִ֕י הַדָּֽבְרַ֥י וְרַבְרְבָנַ֖י יְבַע֑וֹן וְעַל־מַלְכוּתִ֣י הׇתְקְנַ֔ת וּרְב֥וּ יַתִּירָ֖ה ה֥וּסְפַת לִֽי
verses.translation: There and then my reason was restored to me, and my majesty and splendor were restored to me for the glory of my kingdom. My companions and nobles sought me out, and I was reestablished over my kingdom, and added greatness was given me.
verses.rashi: At that time [as translated,] at that time. and I returned to the glory of my kingdom [as translated,] and I returned to the glory of my kingdom. and my splendor the features of my face. my leaders and my dignitaries were seeking me and My advisers and my princes were seeking me and hoping for my return. and I was established [as translated,] and I was established. and excessive greatness was added to me and more greatness than the earlier [kings] was added to me. Our Rabbis said (Shab. 150a) that he rode astride a lion and tied a serpent to its head.

verses.hebrew_full: כְּעַ֞ן אֲנָ֣ה נְבֻכַדְנֶצַּ֗ר מְשַׁבַּ֨ח וּמְרוֹמֵ֤ם וּמְהַדַּר֙ לְמֶ֣לֶךְ שְׁמַיָּ֔א דִּ֤י כׇל־מַעֲבָד֙וֹהִי֙ קְשֹׁ֔ט וְאֹרְחָתֵ֖הּ דִּ֑ין וְדִי֙ מַהְלְכִ֣ין בְּגֵוָ֔ה יָכִ֖ל לְהַשְׁפָּלָֽה
verses.translation: So now I, Nebuchadnezzar, praise, exalt, and glorify the Ruler of Heaven, all of whose works are just and whose ways are right, and who is able to humble those who behave arrogantly.”
verses.rashi: and Whose ways are just His ways are just. who walk with arrogance Those who walk with arrogance He can humble.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 4:34] And those who behave arrogantly (וְדִי֙ מַהְלְכִ֣ין בְּגֵוָ֔ה): [The word b'gevah means] pride/arrogance (גַּאֲוָה - ga'avah). Like [the usage in Job 22:29]: “When they have humbled [you], you will say, ‘[It is] pride!’” (כִּֽי־הִ֭שְׁפִּילוּ וַתֹּ֣אמֶר גֵּוָ֑ה - ki hishpilu vatomer gevah).

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּלְשַׁאצַּ֣ר מַלְכָּ֗א עֲבַד֙ לְחֶ֣ם רַ֔ב לְרַבְרְבָנ֖וֹהִי אֲלַ֑ף וְלׇקֳבֵ֥ל אַלְפָּ֖א חַמְרָ֥א שָׁתֵֽה
verses.translation: King Belshazzar gave a great banquet for his thousand nobles, and in the presence of the thousand he drank wine.
verses.rashi: King Belshazzar He was his son, and he reigned after Evil-merodach, who reigned instead of Nebuchadnezzar, and (he) too was the son of Nebuchadnezzar. made a great feast We find in Josephon (Book 1, ch. 3) that he waged war by day with Darius the Mede and Cyrus and was victorious in the battle. So in the evening he made a feast, as Isaiah had prophesied about him: (21: 5) “Setting the table, setting up the lamp, eating, drinking. ‘Arise, princes, etc.’” For in the midst of the feast the enemies returned, waged war on the city and captured it. as much wine as the thousand The equivalent of one thousand men [was the amount] he was drinking [in] wine.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 5:1] Belshazzar... made a great banquet (עֲבַד֙ לְחֶ֣ם רַ֔ב - avad lechem rav): [Means] he made a feast (עָשָׂה מַאֲכָל - asah ma'achal). And in the presence of the thousand (וְלׇקֳבֵ֥ל אַלְפָּ֖א - v'lokovel alpá): [Meaning] before/opposite a thousand nobles. [This was] contrary to the protocol (or: custom, rule - מִשׁפָּט - mishpat) of kings.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּלְשַׁאצַּ֞ר אֲמַ֣ר בִּטְעֵ֣ם חַמְרָ֗א לְהַיְתָיָה֙ לְמָאנֵי֙ דַּהֲבָ֣א וְכַסְפָּ֔א דִּ֤י הַנְפֵּק֙ נְבוּכַדְנֶצַּ֣ר אֲב֔וּהִי מִן־הֵיכְלָ֖א דִּ֣י בִירוּשְׁלֶ֑ם וְיִשְׁתּ֣וֹן בְּה֗וֹן מַלְכָּא֙ וְרַבְרְבָנ֔וֹהִי שֵׁגְלָתֵ֖הּ וּלְחֵנָתֵֽהּ
verses.translation: Under the influence of the wine, Belshazzar ordered the gold and silver vessels that his father Nebuchadnezzar had taken out of the temple at Jerusalem to be brought so that the king and his nobles, his consorts, and his concubines could drink from them.
verses.rashi: said, with the counsel of the wine He said with the counsel of the wine that he had imbibed. to bring [as translated,] to bring. that...had taken out [as translated,] had taken out. his queen Aram. שֵּׁגְלָתֵה, an expression of a queen in Aramaic, like: (Neh. 2:6): “and the queen (וְהַשֵׁגָל) was sitting beside him.”
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 5:2] Belshazzar... under the influence of the wine (בִּטְעֵם חַמְרָא - bit'em chamra): This [command] was the effect (lit. counsel - עֵצָה - etzah) of the wine that he drank, having become drunk. His consorts (שֵׁגְלָתֵהּ - sheglateih): [This word shegal means queen or primary consort], like [in the verse]: “and the consort (shegal) sitting beside him” [Nehemiah 2:6]. And she is the mistress/lady designated for the king's bed. And similarly [the related verb form appears in]: “...you shall become engaged to a woman, but another man shall violate her (יִשְׁגָּלֶנָּה - yishgalennah)” [Deuteronomy 28:30], and this refers to the act [of intercourse]. And his concubines (וּלְחֵנָתֵהּ - ul'chenateih): [Meaning] his secondary wives/concubines (פִּילַגְשָׁיו - pilagshav). And behold, he committed two evils: [first,] that everyone drank from the holy vessels, and [second,] that they praised gods of gold, wood, and stone [cf. Daniel 5:4, Daniel 5:23].

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֗יִן הַיְתִיו֙ מָאנֵ֣י דַהֲבָ֔א דִּ֣י הַנְפִּ֗קוּ מִן־הֵ֥יכְלָ֛א דִּֽי־בֵ֥ית אֱלָהָ֖א דִּ֣י בִירֽוּשְׁלֶ֑ם וְאִשְׁתִּ֣יו בְּה֗וֹן מַלְכָּא֙ וְרַבְרְבָנ֔וֹהִי שֵׁגְלָתֵ֖הּ וּלְחֵנָתֵֽהּ
verses.translation: The golden vessels that had been taken out of the sanctuary of the House of God in Jerusalem were then brought, and the king, his nobles, his consorts, and his concubines drank from them.
verses.rashi: were brought [as translated,] were brought. that they had taken out [as translated,] that they had taken out.

verses.hebrew_full: אִשְׁתִּ֖יו חַמְרָ֑א וְ֠שַׁבַּ֠חוּ לֵֽאלָהֵ֞י דַּהֲבָ֧א וְכַסְפָּ֛א נְחָשָׁ֥א פַרְזְלָ֖א אָעָ֥א וְאַבְנָֽא
verses.translation: They drank wine and praised the gods of gold and silver, bronze, iron, wood, and stone.

verses.hebrew_full: בַּהּ־שַׁעֲתָ֗ה (נפקו) נְפַ֙קָה֙ אֶצְבְּעָן֙ דִּ֣י יַד־אֱנָ֔שׁ וְכָֽתְבָן֙ לׇקֳבֵ֣ל נֶבְרַשְׁתָּ֔א עַל־גִּירָ֕א דִּֽי־כְתַ֥ל הֵיכְלָ֖א דִּ֣י מַלְכָּ֑א וּמַלְכָּ֣א חָזֵ֔ה פַּ֥ס יְדָ֖א דִּ֥י כָתְבָֽה
verses.translation: Just then, the fingers of a human hand appeared and wrote on the plaster of the wall of the king’s palace opposite the lampstand, so that the king could see the hand as it wrote.
verses.rashi: the fingers...emerged The fingers of a human hand emerged from heaven. opposite the candelabrum opposite the menorah that was before the table, and a similar word appears (in Tractate Yoma 37a): “made a candelabrum (נִבְרֶשֶׁת) of gold.” on the plaster on the lime that is plastered on the wall, like (Isa. 27:9): “like crushed chalkstones (אַבְנֵי גֵר),” which resemble lime.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 5:5] At that moment (בַּהּ־שַׁעֲתָ֗ה), opposite the lampstand (נֶבְרַשְׁתָּ֔א): [The word nebrashta means] a lamp/light (נוּרָה - nurah). On the plaster (עַל־גִּירָ֕א): [This is] like "limestones" (אַבְנֵי-גִר - avnei-gir) [Isaiah 27:9], and it refers to lime/plaster (הַסִּיד - ha-sid). Of the wall of the palace (דִּֽי־כְתַ֥ל הֵיכְלָ֖א): [The word k'tal (wall) is] like [in the phrase] "...behind our wall" (אַחַר כָּתְלֵנוּ - achar kotleinu) [Song of Songs 2:9]. The part of the hand (פַּ֥ס יְדָ֖א): [Meaning] a piece/part of a hand (חֲתִיכַת יָד - chatichat yad). And similarly [the word pas appears in]: “May there be abundance (פִּסַּת - pissat) of grain” [Psalms 72:16]; also “a coat of many pieces/colors (פַּסִּים - passim)” [Genesis 37:3]. And all [these usages] are derived from the root meaning [seen in] “for the faithful have vanished (פַּסּוּ - passu)” [Psalms 12:2]. And behold, they saw hands without a body.

words.eng: meaning ‘in her’·to look at or to

verses.hebrew_full: אֱדַ֤יִן מַלְכָּא֙ זִיוֺ֣הִי שְׁנ֔וֹהִי וְרַעְיֹנֹ֖הִי יְבַהֲלוּנֵּ֑הּ וְקִטְרֵ֤י חַרְצֵהּ֙ מִשְׁתָּרַ֔יִן וְאַ֨רְכֻבָּתֵ֔הּ דָּ֥א לְדָ֖א נָֽקְשָֽׁן
verses.translation: The king’s face darkened, and his thoughts alarmed him; the joints of his loins were loosened and his knees knocked together.
verses.rashi: his color changed His features changed upon him. and his thoughts [as translated,] and his thoughts. and the belts of his loins became untied The belts of his loins opened because his loins were emptying out of fear, and his belt opened. and the belts of his loins became untied were untied. and his knees [as translated,] and his knees. knocked one against the other bumped and knocked.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 5:6] And the joints of (וְקִטְרֵ֤י - v'kitrei): [This refers to] the ligaments/joints (lit. bindings - קִשׁוּרֵי - kishurei) of his loins; they were almost loosened/untied (מֻתָּרִים - mutarim). Knocked (נָֽקְשָֽׁן - nokshan): In the manner of one touching the other, [like the common biblical idiom] "one to another" (אִשָּׁה אֶל-אֲחוֹתָהּ - ishah el achotah - e.g., Exodus 26:3, describing curtains joined together).

verses.hebrew_full: קָרֵ֤א מַלְכָּא֙ בְּחַ֔יִל לְהֶֽעָלָה֙ לְאָ֣שְׁפַיָּ֔א (כשדיא) כַּשְׂדָּאֵ֖י וְגָזְרַיָּ֑א עָנֵ֨ה מַלְכָּ֜א וְאָמַ֣ר לְחַכִּימֵ֣י בָבֶ֗ל דִּ֣י כׇל־אֱ֠נָ֠שׁ דִּֽי־יִקְרֵ֞ה כְּתָבָ֣ה דְנָ֗ה וּפִשְׁרֵהּ֙ יְחַוִּנַּ֔נִי אַרְגְּוָנָ֣א יִלְבַּ֗שׁ (והמנוכא) וְהַֽמְנִיכָ֤א דִֽי־דַהֲבָא֙ עַֽל־צַוְּארֵ֔הּ וְתַלְתִּ֥י בְמַלְכוּתָ֖א יִשְׁלַֽט
verses.translation: The king called loudly for the exorcists, Chaldeans, and diviners to be brought. The king addressed the sages of Babylon, “Whoever can read this writing and tell me its meaning shall be clothed in purple and wear a golden chain on his neck, and shall rule as one of three in the kingdom.”
verses.rashi: aloud Aram. בְּחַיִל, lit. with strength. to bring in [as translated,] to bring in. this writing [as translated,] this writing. shall wear purple following the custom of the nobles. chain Aram. וְהַמְנִיכָא, medallion, necklace. and rule over over a third of the kingdom [as translated,] and rule over a third of the kingdom.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 5:7] And the chain (וְהַֽמְנִיכָ֤א - v'hamnikha): [This means] the golden necklace/chain (רְבִיד הַזָּהָב - revid hazahav). And third (וְתַלְתִּ֥י - v'talti): [Meaning] and he shall rule as the third in the kingdom (וּבַשְּׁלִישִׁית בַּמַּלְכוּת יִשְׁלֹט - u'vashlishit ba'malchut yishlot).

verses.hebrew_full: אֱדַ֙יִן֙ (עללין) עָֽלִּ֔ין כֹּ֖ל חַכִּימֵ֣י מַלְכָּ֑א וְלָֽא־כָהֲלִ֤ין כְּתָבָא֙ לְמִקְרֵ֔א (ופשרא) וּפִשְׁרֵ֖הּ לְהוֹדָעָ֥ה לְמַלְכָּֽא
verses.translation: Then all the king’s sages came, but they could not read the writing or make known its meaning to the king.
verses.rashi: entered [as translated,] entered.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱ֠דַ֠יִן מַלְכָּ֤א בֵלְשַׁאצַּר֙ שַׂגִּ֣יא מִתְבָּהַ֔ל וְזִיוֺ֖הִי שָׁנַ֣יִן עֲל֑וֹהִי וְרַבְרְבָנ֖וֹהִי מִֽשְׁתַּבְּשִֽׁין
verses.translation: King Belshazzar grew exceedingly alarmed and his face darkened, and his nobles were dismayed.
verses.rashi: became very frightened Aram. שַּׂגִּיא מִתְבָּהַל, extremely frightened. were perplexed They were confused.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 5:9] Were dismayed (מִֽשְׁתַּבְּשִֽׁין - mishtabshin): [This is] according to its literal meaning (כְּמַשְׁמָעוֹ - k'mashma'o); like a person who does not know what to do.

verses.hebrew_full: מַלְכְּתָ֕א לׇקֳבֵ֨ל מִלֵּ֤י מַלְכָּא֙ וְרַבְרְבָנ֔וֹהִי לְבֵ֥ית מִשְׁתְּיָ֖א (עללת) עַלַּ֑ת עֲנָ֨ת מַלְכְּתָ֜א וַאֲמֶ֗רֶת מַלְכָּא֙ לְעָלְמִ֣ין חֱיִ֔י אַֽל־יְבַהֲלוּךְ֙ רַעְיוֹנָ֔ךְ (וזיויך) וְזִיוָ֖ךְ אַל־יִשְׁתַּנּֽוֹ
verses.translation: Because of the state of the king and his nobles, the queen came to the banquet hall. The queen spoke up and said, “O king, live forever! Let your thoughts not alarm you or your face darken.
verses.rashi: The queen, in response to the words of the king The queen, because she heard the words of the king and his princes, came to the banquet hall.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 5:10] The queen (מַלְכְּתָא): There are those who say [this was] the wife of Nebuchadnezzar.

verses.hebrew_full: אִיתַ֨י גְּבַ֜ר בְּמַלְכוּתָ֗ךְ דִּ֠י ר֣וּחַ אֱלָהִ֣ין קַדִּישִׁין֮ בֵּהּ֒ וּבְיוֹמֵ֣י אֲב֗וּךְ נַהִיר֧וּ וְשׇׂכְלְתָנ֛וּ וְחׇכְמָ֥ה כְּחׇכְמַת־אֱלָהִ֖ין הִשְׁתְּכַ֣חַת בֵּ֑הּ וּמַלְכָּ֤א נְבֻֽכַדְנֶצַּר֙ אֲב֔וּךְ רַ֧ב חַרְטֻמִּ֣ין אָֽשְׁפִ֗ין כַּשְׂדָּאִין֙ גָּזְרִ֔ין הֲקִימֵ֖הּ אֲב֥וּךְ מַלְכָּֽא
verses.translation: There is a man in your kingdom who has the spirit of the holy gods in him; in your father’s time, illumination, understanding, and wisdom like that of the gods were to be found in him, and your father, King Nebuchadnezzar, appointed him chief of the magicians, exorcists, Chaldeans, and diviners.
verses.rashi: There is a man There is a man in your kingdom in whom is the spirit of the holy ones. like the wisdom of the angels [as translated,] angels. appointed him He appointed him prince over them all.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 5:11] Illumination (נַהִיר֧וּ - nahiru): [This word is] like [the Aramaic word for light/illumination found, for example, in Daniel 2:22]: “and light (וּנְהוֹרָא - u’nehora)”.

words.eng: Nebuchadnezzar or Nebuchadrezzar = "may Nebo protect the crown"

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־קֳבֵ֡ל דִּ֣י ר֣וּחַ יַתִּירָ֡ה וּמַנְדַּ֡ע וְשׇׂכְלְתָנ֡וּ מְפַשַּׁ֣ר חֶלְמִין֩ וְֽאַֽחֲוָיַ֨ת אֲחִידָ֜ן וּמְשָׁרֵ֣א קִטְרִ֗ין הִשְׁתְּכַ֤חַת בֵּהּ֙ בְּדָ֣נִיֵּ֔אל דִּֽי־מַלְכָּ֥א שָׂם־שְׁמֵ֖הּ בֵּלְטְשַׁאצַּ֑ר כְּעַ֛ן דָּנִיֵּ֥אל יִתְקְרֵ֖י וּפִשְׁרָ֥ה יְהַֽחֲוֵֽה
verses.translation: Seeing that there is to be found in Daniel (whom the king called Belteshazzar) extraordinary spirit, knowledge, and understanding to interpret dreams, to explain riddles and solve problems, let Daniel now be called to tell the meaning [of the writing].”
verses.rashi: All because, etc. because a superior spirit, knowledge and understanding are within him, and the talent to tell riddles. and untie knots The revelation of all secrets is found in Daniel.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 5:12] To explain riddles (וְאַֽחֲוָיַ֨ת אֲחִידָ֜ן - v'achawayat achidan) [and solve problems (וּמְשָׁרֵ֣א קִטְרִ֗ין - u'm'share kitrin)]: [This means] telling [the meaning of] riddles (מַגִּיד חִידוֹת - maggid chidot) and untying knots (וּמַתִּיר קְשׁוּרִים - u'matir k'shurim).

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֙יִן֙ דָּֽנִיֵּ֔אל הֻעַ֖ל קֳדָ֣ם מַלְכָּ֑א עָנֵ֨ה מַלְכָּ֜א וְאָמַ֣ר לְדָנִיֵּ֗אל (אנתה) אַנְתְּ־ה֤וּא דָנִיֵּאל֙ דִּֽי־מִן־בְּנֵ֤י גָלוּתָא֙ דִּ֣י יְה֔וּד דִּ֥י הַיְתִ֛י מַלְכָּ֥א אַ֖בִי מִן־יְהֽוּד
verses.translation: Daniel was then brought before the king. The king addressed Daniel, “You are Daniel, one of the exiles of Judah whom my father, the king, brought from Judah.

words.eng: Judah-the territory of the tribe of Judah
prep·Judah-the territory of the tribe of Judah

verses.hebrew_full: וְשִׁמְעֵ֣ת (עליך) עֲלָ֔ךְ דִּ֛י ר֥וּחַ אֱלָהִ֖ין בָּ֑ךְ וְנַהִיר֧וּ וְשׇׂכְלְתָנ֛וּ וְחׇכְמָ֥ה יַתִּירָ֖ה הִשְׁתְּכַ֥חַת בָּֽךְ
verses.translation: I have heard about you that you have the spirit of the gods in you, and that illumination, knowledge, and extraordinary wisdom are to be found in you.

verses.hebrew_full: וּכְעַ֞ן הֻעַ֣לּוּ קׇֽדָמַ֗י חַכִּֽימַיָּא֙ אָֽשְׁפַיָּ֔א דִּֽי־כְתָבָ֤ה דְנָה֙ יִקְר֔וֹן וּפִשְׁרֵ֖הּ לְהוֹדָעֻתַ֑נִי וְלָֽא־כָהֲלִ֥ין פְּשַֽׁר־מִלְּתָ֖א לְהַחֲוָיָֽה
verses.translation: Now the sages and exorcists have been brought before me to read this writing and to make known its meaning to me. But they could not tell what it meant.

verses.hebrew_full: וַאֲנָה֙ שִׁמְעֵ֣ת (עליך) עֲלָ֔ךְ דִּֽי־תִכּ֥וּל (תוכל) פִּשְׁרִ֛ין לְמִפְשַׁ֖ר וְקִטְרִ֣ין לְמִשְׁרֵ֑א כְּעַ֡ן הֵן֩ (תוכל) תִּכּ֨וּל כְּתָבָ֜א לְמִקְרֵ֗א וּפִשְׁרֵהּ֙ לְהוֹדָ֣עוּתַ֔נִי אַרְגְּוָנָ֣א תִלְבַּ֗שׁ (והמונכא) וְהַֽמְנִיכָ֤א דִֽי־דַהֲבָא֙ עַֽל־צַוְּארָ֔ךְ וְתַלְתָּ֥א בְמַלְכוּתָ֖א תִּשְׁלַֽט
verses.translation: I have heard about you, that you can give interpretations and solve problems. Now if you can read the writing and make known its meaning to me, you shall be clothed in purple and wear a golden chain on your neck and rule as one of three in the kingdom.”
verses.rashi: Now if you can [as translated,] Now if you can.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֜יִן עָנֵ֣ה דָנִיֵּ֗אל וְאָמַר֙ קֳדָ֣ם מַלְכָּ֔א מַתְּנָתָךְ֙ לָ֣ךְ לֶֽהֶוְיָ֔ן וּנְבָ֥זְבְּיָתָ֖ךְ לְאׇחֳרָ֣ן הַ֑ב בְּרַ֗ם כְּתָבָא֙ אֶקְרֵ֣א לְמַלְכָּ֔א וּפִשְׁרָ֖א אֲהוֹדְעִנֵּֽהּ
verses.translation: Then Daniel said in reply to the king, “You may keep your gifts for yourself, and give your presents to others. But I will read the writing for the king, and make its meaning known to him.
verses.rashi: and give your lavish gifts [as translated,] and give your lavish gifts. give to someone else give to another person.

verses.hebrew_full: (אנתה) אַ֖נְתְּ מַלְכָּ֑א אֱלָהָא֙ (עליא) עִלָּאָ֔ה מַלְכוּתָ֤א וּרְבוּתָא֙ וִיקָרָ֣א וְהַדְרָ֔א יְהַ֖ב לִנְבֻכַדְנֶצַּ֥ר אֲבֽוּךְ
verses.translation: O king, the Most High God bestowed kingship, grandeur, glory, and majesty upon your father Nebuchadnezzar.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 5:18] You (אַ֖נְתְּ - an’t) [O king]: The meaning [of starting with "You"] is: Listen, you, O king (הַטַּעַם שְׁמַע אַתָּה הַמֶּלֶךְ - ha'ta'am sh'ma atah ha'melech).

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִן־רְבוּתָא֙ דִּ֣י יְהַב־לֵ֔הּ כֹּ֣ל עַֽמְמַיָּ֗א אֻמַּיָּא֙ וְלִשָּׁ֣נַיָּ֔א הֲו֛וֹ (זאעין) זָיְעִ֥ין וְדָחֲלִ֖ין מִן־קֳדָמ֑וֹהִי דִּֽי־הֲוָ֨א צָבֵ֜א הֲוָ֣ה קָטֵ֗ל וְדִֽי־הֲוָ֤ה צָבֵא֙ הֲוָ֣ה מַחֵ֔א וְדִֽי־הֲוָ֤ה צָבֵא֙ הֲוָ֣ה מָרִ֔ים וְדִֽי־הֲוָ֥א צָבֵ֖א הֲוָ֥א מַשְׁפִּֽל
verses.translation: And because of the grandeur that [God] bestowed upon him, all the peoples and nations of every language trembled in fear of him. He put to death whom he wished, and whom he wished he let live; he raised high whom he wished and whom he wished he brought low.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 5:19] Trembled (זָיְעִ֥ין - zay'in): [This is] like [the phrase in Esther 5:9 describing Mordecai's defiance of Haman]: “and did not move/tremble before him” (וְלֹא-זָע מִמֶּנּוּ - v'lo za mimenu).

verses.hebrew_full: וּכְדִי֙ רִ֣ם לִבְבֵ֔הּ וְרוּחֵ֖הּ תִּֽקְפַ֣ת לַהֲזָדָ֑ה הׇנְחַת֙ מִן־כׇּרְסֵ֣א מַלְכוּתֵ֔הּ וִֽיקָרָ֖ה הֶעְדִּ֥יו מִנֵּֽהּ
verses.translation: But when he grew haughty and willfully presumptuous, he was deposed from his royal throne and his glory was removed from him.
verses.rashi: and when his heart became haughty [as translated,] and when his heart became haughty. so that he dealt wickedly [as translated,] so that he dealt wickedly. he was deposed Aram. הָנְחַת, lit. he took him down. was removed from him. Aram. הֶעְדִּיו, He removed from him.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 5:20] To act presumptuously (לַהֲזָדָה - lahazadah): [Meaning] in a way of insolence/presumption (דֶּרֶךְ זָדוֹן - derech zadon). And the meaning of [the phrase later in Daniel 5:23] “and whose are all your ways” (וְכֹל אֹרְחָתָךְ לֵהּ - v'chol orchatecha leh) is: that you are in His hand, and He leads you to the place He desires, like the bridle of the horse in the hand of the rider. But Yefet [ben Ali] said [interpreting Daniel 5:23]: "And in your end, you will go to Him," along the lines of [the verse]: “and the spirit returns to God who gave it” [Ecclesiastes 12:7].

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִן־בְּנֵי֩ אֲנָשָׁ֨א טְרִ֜יד וְלִבְבֵ֣הּ עִם־חֵיוְתָ֣א (שוי) שַׁוִּ֗יו וְעִם־עֲרָֽדַיָּא֙ מְדֹרֵ֔הּ עִשְׂבָּ֤א כְתוֹרִין֙ יְטַ֣עֲמוּנֵּ֔הּ וּמִטַּ֥ל שְׁמַיָּ֖א גִּשְׁמֵ֣הּ יִצְטַבַּ֑ע עַ֣ד דִּֽי־יְדַ֗ע דִּֽי־שַׁלִּ֞יט אֱלָהָ֤א (עליא) עִלָּאָה֙ בְּמַלְכ֣וּת אֲנָשָׁ֔א וּלְמַן־דִּ֥י יִצְבֵּ֖א יְהָקֵ֥ים (עליה) עֲלַֽהּ
verses.translation: He was driven away from society, and his mind made like that of a beast, and his habitation was with wild asses. He was fed grass like cattle, and his body was drenched with the dew of heaven until he came to know that the Most High God is sovereign over human dominion, and sets rulers over it at will.
verses.rashi: was banished Aram. טְרִיד was driven away.

verses.hebrew_full: (ואנתה) וְאַ֤נְתְּ בְּרֵהּ֙ בֵּלְשַׁאצַּ֔ר לָ֥א הַשְׁפֵּ֖לְתְּ לִבְבָ֑ךְ כׇּל־קֳבֵ֕ל דִּ֥י כׇל־דְּנָ֖ה יְדַֽעְתָּ
verses.translation: But you, Belshazzar his son, did not humble yourself although you knew all this.
verses.rashi: in view of the fact that you know all this in view of the fact that you know all this that befell your father, and you should have feared the Holy One, blessed be He.

verses.hebrew_full: וְעַ֣ל מָרֵֽא־שְׁמַיָּ֣א הִתְרוֹמַ֡מְתָּ וּלְמָֽאנַיָּ֨א דִֽי־בַיְתֵ֜הּ הַיְתִ֣יו (קדמיך) קׇֽדָמָ֗ךְ (ואנתה) וְאַ֨נְתְּ (ורברבניך) וְרַבְרְבָנָ֜ךְ שֵֽׁגְלָתָ֣ךְ וּלְחֵנָתָךְ֮ חַמְרָא֮ שָׁתַ֣יִן בְּהוֹן֒ וְלֵֽאלָהֵ֣י כַסְפָּֽא־וְ֠דַהֲבָ֠א נְחָשָׁ֨א פַרְזְלָ֜א אָעָ֣א וְאַבְנָ֗א דִּ֠י לָֽא־חָזַ֧יִן וְלָא־שָׁמְעִ֛ין וְלָ֥א יָדְעִ֖ין שַׁבַּ֑חְתָּ וְלֵֽאלָהָ֞א דִּֽי־נִשְׁמְתָ֥ךְ בִּידֵ֛הּ וְכׇל־אֹרְחָתָ֥ךְ לֵ֖הּ לָ֥א הַדַּֽרְתָּ
verses.translation: You exalted yourself against the Sovereign of Heaven, whose temple vessels you had brought to you. You and your nobles, your consorts, and your concubines drank wine from them and praised the gods of silver and gold, bronze and iron, wood and stone, which do not see, hear, or understand; but the God who controls your lifebreath and every move you make—this One you did not glorify!

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֙יִן֙ מִן־קֳדָמ֔וֹהִי שְׁלִ֖יחַ פַּסָּ֣א דִֽי־יְדָ֑א וּכְתָבָ֥א דְנָ֖ה רְשִֽׁים
verses.translation: He therefore made the hand appear, and caused the writing to be inscribed.
verses.rashi: the palm of a hand was sent forth [as translated,] the palm of a hand was sent forth. and it inscribed this writing and this is the writing that it inscribed.

verses.hebrew_full: וּדְנָ֥ה כְתָבָ֖א דִּ֣י רְשִׁ֑ים מְנֵ֥א מְנֵ֖א תְּקֵ֥ל וּפַרְסִֽין
verses.translation: This is the writing that is inscribed: MENE MENE TEKEL UPHARSIN.
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 5:25] Inscribed (רְשִׁים - r'shim): [Meaning] recorded/inscribed (רָשַׁם - rasham). And like it [is the phrase in Daniel 10:21]: “inscribed in the writing [of truth]” (רָשׁוּם בִּכְתָב - rashum biktav). MENE (מְנֵא): [Signifies that] everything that is counted (יְסֻפַּר - y'suppar) [or] numbered (יֵחָשֵׁב - yechashev). Furthermore, the word UPHARSIN (וּפַרְסִין) has two [meanings implied by the singular Peres]: The first PERES (פְּרֵס) relates to breaking/dividing (פְּרִיסַת - p'risat), [signifying] the breaking/division of his kingdom and its cutting off (כְּרִיתוּתָם - k'ritutam). And the second PERES (פֶּרֶס) relates to Persia (פָּרַס - Paras), from [the common phrase like in Daniel 6:9, Daniel 6:13, Daniel 6:16; Esther 1:3, etc.] “Media and Persia” (מָדַי וּפָרַס - Madai uFaras). Therefore, he [Daniel] said [in the interpretation, Daniel 5:28]: “and it is given to the Medes and Persians” (וִיהִיבַת לְמָדַי וּפָרָס - viyhivath l'Madai uFaras).

verses.hebrew_full: דְּנָ֖ה פְּשַֽׁר־מִלְּתָ֑א מְנֵ֕א מְנָֽה־אֱלָהָ֥א מַלְכוּתָ֖ךְ וְהַשְׁלְמַֽהּ
verses.translation: And this is its meaning: MENE—God has numbered. [the days of] your kingdom and brought it to an end;
verses.rashi: MENE God has counted i.e., MENE means that God has counted your kingdom. and has brought it to an end He calculated the end of the allotted time for the kingdom of your father and his seed, and found that it has already been completed.

verses.hebrew_full: תְּקֵ֑ל תְּקִ֥ילְתָּ בְמֹֽאזַנְיָ֖א וְהִשְׁתְּכַ֥חַתְּ חַסִּֽיר
verses.translation: TEKEL—you have been weighed. in the balance and found wanting;
verses.rashi: TEKEIL You were weighed on the scales TEKEIL means that you were weighed on the scales and found wanting of all righteousness.

verses.hebrew_full: פְּרֵ֑ס פְּרִיסַת֙ מַלְכוּתָ֔ךְ וִיהִיבַ֖ת לְמָדַ֥י וּפָרָֽס
verses.translation: PERES—your kingdom has been divided. and given to the Medes and the Persians.”
verses.rashi: Your kingdom has been broken up, etc. i.e., UFARSIN means two definitions of PEREIS; one is that your kingdom פְּרִיסַת, your kingdom has been broken up, and the second one is that it has been given to Media and Persia (וּפָּרַס).

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֣יִן אֲמַ֣ר בֵּלְשַׁאצַּ֗ר וְהַלְבִּ֤שׁוּ לְדָֽנִיֵּאל֙ אַרְגְּוָנָ֔א (והמנוכא) וְהַֽמְנִיכָ֥א דִֽי־דַהֲבָ֖א עַֽל־צַוְּארֵ֑הּ וְהַכְרִ֣זֽוּ עֲל֔וֹהִי דִּֽי־לֶהֱוֵ֥א שַׁלִּ֛יט תַּלְתָּ֖א בְּמַלְכוּתָֽא
verses.translation: Then, at Belshazzar’s command, they clothed Daniel in purple, placed a golden chain on his neck, and proclaimed that he should rule as one of three in the kingdom.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּ֚הּ בְּלֵ֣ילְיָ֔א קְטִ֕יל בֵּלְאשַׁצַּ֖ר מַלְכָּ֥א (כשדיא) כַשְׂדָּאָֽה
verses.translation: That very night, Belshazzar, the Chaldean king, was killed,
verses.ibn_ezra: [Daniel 5:30] Was killed (קְטִיל - k'til): [Means] was slain (נֶהֱרַג - nehĕrag). Like [the usage in Job 24:14]: “he murders (יִקְטֹל - yiktol) the poor and needy”.

verses.hebrew_full: וְדָרְיָ֙וֶשׁ֙ (מדיא) מָֽדָאָ֔ה קַבֵּ֖ל מַלְכוּתָ֑א כְּבַ֥ר שְׁנִ֖ין שִׁתִּ֥ין וְתַרְתֵּֽין
verses.translation: and Darius the Mede received the kingdom, being about sixty-two years old.
verses.rashi: And Darius the Mede received the kingdom at the age of sixty-two Why does he count his years? To tell you that on the day that Nebuchadnezzar entered the Heichal in the days of Jehoiachin, his adversary, Darius, was born (Seder Olam ch. 28). From the exile of Jeconiah until now were sixty-two years, and the master said: They were exiled in the days of Jehoiachin in the seventh year counting from the conquest of Jehoiakim, which is eight years after the beginning of Nebuchadnezzar’s reign. There remained to Nebuchadnezzar’s reign thirty-seven years, for he reigned forty-five years, and twenty-three of Evil-merodach’s, as our Sages stated in Tractate Megillah (11b), and the two years of Belshazzar that passed, totaling sixty-two.

verses.hebrew_full: שְׁפַר֙ קֳדָ֣ם דָּרְיָ֔וֶשׁ וַהֲקִים֙ עַל־מַלְכוּתָ֔א לַאֲחַשְׁדַּרְפְּנַיָּ֖א מְאָ֣ה וְעֶשְׂרִ֑ין דִּ֥י לֶהֱוֺ֖ן בְּכׇל־מַלְכוּתָֽא
verses.translation: It pleased Darius to appoint over the kingdom one hundred and twenty satraps to be in charge of the whole kingdom;

verses.hebrew_full: וְעֵ֤לָּא מִנְּהוֹן֙ סָרְכִ֣ין תְּלָתָ֔ה דִּ֥י דָנִיֵּ֖אל חַֽד־מִנְּה֑וֹן דִּֽי־לֶהֱוֺ֞ן אֲחַשְׁדַּרְפְּנַיָּ֣א אִלֵּ֗ין יָהֲבִ֤ין לְהוֹן֙ טַעְמָ֔א וּמַלְכָּ֖א לָֽא־לֶהֱוֵ֥א נָזִֽק
verses.translation: over them were three ministers, one of them Daniel, to whom these satraps reported, in order that the king not be troubled.
verses.rashi: And over them [as translated,] and over them. that these satraps should give them counsel that these satraps should give counsel to the three viziers. and the king should not suffer any injury And the king should not suffer any loss in any royal matter that is incumbent upon them to perform.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱדַ֙יִן֙ דָּנִיֵּ֣אל דְּנָ֔ה הֲוָ֣א מִתְנַצַּ֔ח עַל־סָרְכַיָּ֖א וַאֲחַשְׁדַּרְפְּנַיָּ֑א כׇּל־קֳבֵ֗ל דִּ֣י ר֤וּחַ יַתִּירָא֙ בֵּ֔הּ וּמַלְכָּ֣א עֲשִׁ֔ית לַהֲקָמוּתֵ֖הּ עַל־כׇּל־מַלְכוּתָֽא
verses.translation: This man Daniel surpassed the other ministers and satraps by virtue of his extraordinary spirit, and the king considered setting him over the whole kingdom.
verses.rashi: this Daniel [as translated,] this Daniel. surpassed [as translated,] surpassed. and the king contemplated The king was thinking in his heart to set him up over all of them.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱדַ֨יִן סָֽרְכַיָּ֜א וַאֲחַשְׁדַּרְפְּנַיָּ֗א הֲו֨וֹ בָעַ֧יִן עִלָּ֛ה לְהַשְׁכָּחָ֥ה לְדָנִיֵּ֖אל מִצַּ֣ד מַלְכוּתָ֑א וְכׇל־עִלָּ֨ה וּשְׁחִיתָ֜ה לָא־יָכְלִ֣ין לְהַשְׁכָּחָ֗ה כׇּל־קֳבֵל֙ דִּֽי־מְהֵימַ֣ן ה֔וּא וְכׇל־שָׁלוּ֙ וּשְׁחִיתָ֔ה לָ֥א הִשְׁתְּכַ֖חַת עֲלֽוֹהִי
verses.translation: The ministers and satraps looked for some fault in Daniel’s conduct in matters of state, but they could find neither fault nor corruption, inasmuch as he was trustworthy, and no negligence or corruption was to be found in him.
verses.rashi: sought to find a pretext They were seeking a pretext to find about Daniel in matters of the kingdom so that they could inform on him.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱ֠דַ֠יִן גֻּבְרַיָּ֤א אִלֵּךְ֙ אָֽמְרִ֔ין דִּ֣י לָ֧א נְהַשְׁכַּ֛ח לְדָנִיֵּ֥אל דְּנָ֖ה כׇּל־עִלָּ֑ה לָהֵ֕ן הַשְׁכַּ֥חְנָֽא עֲל֖וֹהִי בְּדָ֥ת אֱלָהֵֽהּ
verses.translation: Those men then said, “We are not going to find any fault with this Daniel, unless we find something against him in connection with the laws of his God.”
verses.rashi: No pretext can be found They said among themselves, “No pretext can be found about this Daniel.” but we shall find [a pretext] against him but we shall find a pretext against him through the law of his God if we ask the king to issue some decree to cause him to transgress His law, and he will not fulfill the king’s command; and this is the pretext.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱ֠דַ֠יִן סָרְכַיָּ֤א וַאֲחַשְׁדַּרְפְּנַיָּא֙ אִלֵּ֔ן הַרְגִּ֖שׁוּ עַל־מַלְכָּ֑א וְכֵן֙ אָמְרִ֣ין לֵ֔הּ דָּרְיָ֥וֶשׁ מַלְכָּ֖א לְעָלְמִ֥ין חֱיִֽי
verses.translation: Then these ministers and satraps came thronging in to the king and said to him, “O King Darius, live forever!
verses.rashi: assembled Aram. הַרְגִּישׁוּ, they approached the king.

verses.hebrew_full: אִתְיָעַ֜טוּ כֹּ֣ל סָרְכֵ֣י מַלְכוּתָ֗א סִגְנַיָּ֤א וַֽאֲחַשְׁדַּרְפְּנַיָּא֙ הַדָּֽבְרַיָּ֣א וּפַחֲוָתָ֔א לְקַיָּמָ֤ה קְיָם֙ מַלְכָּ֔א וּלְתַקָּפָ֖ה אֱסָ֑ר דִּ֣י כׇל־דִּֽי־יִבְעֵ֣א בָ֠ע֠וּ מִן־כׇּל־אֱלָ֨הּ וֶֽאֱנָ֜שׁ עַד־יוֹמִ֣ין תְּלָתִ֗ין לָהֵן֙ מִנָּ֣ךְ מַלְכָּ֔א יִתְרְמֵ֕א לְגֹ֖ב אַרְיָוָתָֽא
verses.translation: All the ministers of the kingdom, the prefects, satraps, companions, and governors are in agreement that a royal ban should be issued under sanction of an oath that whoever shall address a petition to anyone, divine or human, besides you, O king, during the next thirty days shall be thrown into a lions’ den.
verses.rashi: have taken counsel [as translated,] have taken counsel. to preserve the king’s law to preserve the king’s law with power because you have recently ascended the throne. [Moreover], you are from another country and are in need of support. make any request [as translated,] make any request. except from you, O King [as translated,] except from you, O King. into a pit of lions into the pit where the king’s lions were [kept].

verses.hebrew_full: כְּעַ֣ן מַלְכָּ֔א תְּקִ֥ים אֱסָרָ֖א וְתִרְשֻׁ֣ם כְּתָבָ֑א דִּ֣י לָ֧א לְהַשְׁנָיָ֛ה כְּדָת־מָדַ֥י וּפָרַ֖ס דִּי־לָ֥א תֶעְדֵּֽא
verses.translation: So issue the ban, O king, and put it in writing so that it be unalterable as a law of the Medes and Persians that may not be abrogated.”
verses.rashi: you shall issue a decree lit. you shall put up this decree. that will not be amended that will not be amended, like the law of Media and Persia. which will not be repealed which will not pass.

verses.hebrew_full: כׇּל־קֳבֵ֖ל דְּנָ֑ה מַלְכָּא֙ דָּֽרְיָ֔וֶשׁ רְשַׁ֥ם כְּתָבָ֖א וֶאֱסָרָֽא
verses.translation: Thereupon King Darius put the ban in writing.

verses.hebrew_full: וְ֠דָנִיֵּ֠אל כְּדִ֨י יְדַ֜ע דִּֽי־רְשִׁ֤ים כְּתָבָא֙ עַ֣ל לְבַיְתֵ֔הּ וְכַוִּ֨ין פְּתִיחָ֥ן לֵהּ֙ בְּעִלִּיתֵ֔הּ נֶ֖גֶד יְרוּשְׁלֶ֑ם וְזִמְנִין֩ תְּלָתָ֨ה בְיוֹמָ֜א ה֣וּא בָּרֵ֣ךְ עַל־בִּרְכ֗וֹהִי וּמְצַלֵּ֤א וּמוֹדֵא֙ קֳדָ֣ם אֱלָהֵ֔הּ כׇּל־קֳבֵל֙ דִּֽי־הֲוָ֣א עָבֵ֔ד מִן־קַדְמַ֖ת דְּנָֽה
verses.translation: When Daniel learned that it had been put in writing, he went to his house, in whose upper chamber he had had windows made facing Jerusalem, and three times a day he knelt down, prayed, and made confession to his God, as he had always done.
verses.rashi: when he knew and when he found out. where there were open windows in his upper chamber Aram. בְּעִלִּיתֵהּ, in his upper story. opposite Jerusalem toward the side of the Temple, and although it was in ruins, he did as Solomon said (I Kings 8:48): “and pray to You toward their land.” and three times the evening prayer, the morning prayer and the afternoon prayer. kneeled on his knees [as translated,] kneeled on his knees. as he had done prior to this just as all that he had done before this.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱ֠דַ֠יִן גֻּבְרַיָּ֤א אִלֵּךְ֙ הַרְגִּ֔שׁוּ וְהַשְׁכַּ֖חוּ לְדָנִיֵּ֑אל בָּעֵ֥ה וּמִתְחַנַּ֖ן קֳדָ֥ם אֱלָהֵֽהּ
verses.translation: Then those men came thronging in and found Daniel petitioning his God in supplication.
verses.rashi: searched Aram. הַרְגִישּׁוּ, tapped and searched.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּ֠אדַ֠יִן קְרִ֨בוּ וְאָמְרִ֥ין קֳדָם־מַלְכָּא֮ עַל־אֱסָ֣ר מַלְכָּא֒ הֲלָ֧א אֱסָ֣ר רְשַׁ֗מְתָּ דִּ֣י כׇל־אֱנָ֡שׁ דִּֽי־יִבְעֵא֩ מִן־כׇּל־אֱלָ֨הּ וֶֽאֱנָ֜שׁ עַד־יוֹמִ֣ין תְּלָתִ֗ין לָהֵן֙ מִנָּ֣ךְ מַלְכָּ֔א יִתְרְמֵ֕א לְג֖וֹב אַרְיָוָתָ֑א עָנֵ֨ה מַלְכָּ֜א וְאָמַ֗ר יַצִּיבָ֧א מִלְּתָ֛א כְּדָת־מָדַ֥י וּפָרַ֖ס דִּי־לָ֥א תֶעְדֵּֽא
verses.translation: They then approached the king and reminded him of the royal ban: “Did you not put in writing a ban that whoever addresses a petition to anyone, divine or human, besides you, O king, during the next thirty days, shall be thrown into a lions’ den?” The king said in reply, “The order stands firm, as a law of the Medes and Persians that may not be abrogated.”
verses.rashi: concerning the king’s decree concerning the infraction of the king’s decree, about which they had come to inform. the thing is true [as translated,] the thing is true.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּ֠אדַ֠יִן עֲנ֣וֹ וְאָמְרִין֮ קֳדָ֣ם מַלְכָּא֒ דִּ֣י דָנִיֵּ֡אל דִּי֩ מִן־בְּנֵ֨י גָלוּתָ֜א דִּ֣י יְה֗וּד לָא־שָׂ֨ם (עליך) עֲלָ֤ךְ מַלְכָּא֙ טְעֵ֔ם וְעַל־אֱסָרָ֖א דִּ֣י רְשַׁ֑מְתָּ וְזִמְנִ֤ין תְּלָתָה֙ בְּיוֹמָ֔א בָּעֵ֖א בָּעוּתֵֽהּ
verses.translation: Thereupon they said to the king, “Daniel, one of the exiles of Judah, pays no heed to you, O king, or to the ban that you put in writing; three times a day he offers his petitions [to his God].”
verses.rashi: did not pay heed to you, O king He did not pay heed or counsel to care about your decrees. he offers up his prayer Aram. בָּעֵא בָּעוּתֵה, he prays his prayer.

words.eng: Judah-the territory of the tribe of Judah

verses.hebrew_full: אֱדַ֨יִן מַלְכָּ֜א כְּדִ֧י מִלְּתָ֣א שְׁמַ֗ע שַׂגִּיא֙ בְּאֵ֣שׁ עֲל֔וֹהִי וְעַ֧ל דָּנִיֵּ֛אל שָׂ֥ם בָּ֖ל לְשֵׁיזָבוּתֵ֑הּ וְעַד֙ מֶֽעָלֵ֣י שִׁמְשָׁ֔א הֲוָ֥א מִשְׁתַּדַּ֖ר לְהַצָּלוּתֵֽהּ
verses.translation: Upon hearing that, the king was very disturbed, and he set his heart upon saving Daniel, and until the sun set made every effort to rescue him.
verses.rashi: when...heard the thing [as translated,] when he heard the thing. he became very displeased It was very bad in his eyes because he loved Daniel, and his death would be painful to him. and he gave Daniel a reprieve to save him He gave Daniel a reprieve to push off the matter to save him, and he said to them, “I don’t believe you.” a reprieve Aram. בָּל. I do not know of any word like it, but its context shows its meaning. and until sunset and until sunset he tried to save him, but when the time of his prayer arrived, he prayed his prayer, and the king had no longer had a pretext to save him.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֙יִן֙ גֻּבְרַיָּ֣א אִלֵּ֔ךְ הַרְגִּ֖שׁוּ עַל־מַלְכָּ֑א וְאָמְרִ֣ין לְמַלְכָּ֗א דַּ֤ע מַלְכָּא֙ דִּֽי־דָת֙ לְמָדַ֣י וּפָרַ֔ס דִּֽי־כׇל־אֱסָ֥ר וּקְיָ֛ם דִּֽי־מַלְכָּ֥א יְהָקֵ֖ים לָ֥א לְהַשְׁנָיָֽה
verses.translation: Then those men came thronging in to the king and said to the king, “Know, O king, that it is a law of the Medes and Persians that any ban that the king issues under sanction of oath is unalterable.”
verses.rashi: that the king establishes What the king decrees and ratifies, no man can alter.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֜יִן מַלְכָּ֣א אֲמַ֗ר וְהַיְתִיו֙ לְדָ֣נִיֵּ֔אל וּרְמ֕וֹ לְגֻבָּ֖א דִּ֣י אַרְיָוָתָ֑א עָנֵ֤ה מַלְכָּא֙ וְאָמַ֣ר לְדָנִיֵּ֔אל אֱלָהָ֗ךְ דִּ֣י (אנתה) אַ֤נְתְּ פָּֽלַֽח־לֵהּ֙ בִּתְדִירָ֔א ה֖וּא יְשֵׁיזְבִנָּֽךְ
verses.translation: By the king’s order, Daniel was then brought and thrown into the lions’ den. The king spoke to Daniel and said, “Your God, whom you serve so regularly, will deliver you.”

verses.hebrew_full: וְהֵיתָ֙יִת֙ אֶ֣בֶן חֲדָ֔ה וְשֻׂמַ֖ת עַל־פֻּ֣ם גֻּבָּ֑א וְחַתְמַ֨הּ מַלְכָּ֜א בְּעִזְקְתֵ֗הּ וּבְעִזְקָת֙ רַבְרְבָנ֔וֹהִי דִּ֛י לָא־תִשְׁנֵ֥א צְב֖וּ בְּדָנִיֵּֽאל
verses.translation: A rock was brought and placed over the mouth of the den; the king sealed it with his signet and with the signet of his nobles, so that nothing might be altered concerning Daniel.
verses.rashi: And a stone was brought lit. and one stone was brought. Throughout the entire land of Babylon there are no stones but bricks, as it is written: (Gen 11:3): “Let us make bricks.” We learn that there are no stones in Babylon, but for the occasion, angels brought it [a stone] from the land of Israel. If God stipulated with the Creation on the sea that it should split for the Children of Israel, and on the fire that it cool off for Hananiah and his colleagues, and on the lions that they should not hurt Daniel, He did not stipulate about casting stones or weapons that they should not hurt any man. So the king said, “From the lions I cannot take him away. I shall be careful with him that no man hurt him, and if the miracle comes, let it come.” This does not appear in some editions. and placed on the mouth of the pit [as translated,] and placed on the mouth of the pit. with his signet ring with his signet ring so that no one could move it from its place and cast stones upon him to kill him. that his will about Daniel not be altered so that his will should not be changed; i.e., so that they should not harm him against his [the king’s] will.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱ֠דַ֠יִן אֲזַ֨ל מַלְכָּ֤א לְהֵֽיכְלֵהּ֙ וּבָ֣ת טְוָ֔ת וְדַחֲוָ֖ן לָא־הַנְעֵ֣ל קׇֽדָמ֑וֹהִי וְשִׁנְתֵּ֖הּ נַדַּ֥ת עֲלֽוֹהִי
verses.translation: The king then went to his palace and spent the night fasting; no diversions were brought to him, and his sleep fled from him.
verses.rashi: and retired while fasting [as translated,] and retired while fasting. table Aram. וְּדַּחֲוָן, and a table, in the Mishnah (Tosefta Kelim, B.M. 5:1). So did Dunash explain (p. 58). They did not bring before him a table upon which to eat. and his sleep wandered from him [as translated,] his sleep wandered.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֣יִן מַלְכָּ֔א בִּשְׁפַּרְפָּרָ֖א יְק֣וּם בְּנׇגְהָ֑א וּ֨בְהִתְבְּהָלָ֔ה לְגֻבָּ֥א דִֽי־אַרְיָוָתָ֖א אֲזַֽל
verses.translation: Then, at the first light of dawn, the king arose and rushed to the lions’ den.
verses.rashi: at dawn Aram. בִּשְׁפַרְפָרָה, at the rise of dawn, and there are many [examples of this] in Targum Jonathan. arose to the light of morning He arose at the light of morning, not after three hours, like other kings. and in haste went to the pit of the lions [as translated,] and in haste went to the pit of the lions.

verses.hebrew_full: וּכְמִקְרְבֵ֣הּ לְגֻבָּ֔א לְדָ֣נִיֵּ֔אל בְּקָ֥ל עֲצִ֖יב זְעִ֑ק עָנֵ֨ה מַלְכָּ֜א וְאָמַ֣ר לְדָנִיֵּ֗אל דָּֽנִיֵּאל֙ עֲבֵד֙ אֱלָהָ֣א חַיָּ֔א אֱלָהָ֗ךְ דִּ֣י (אנתה) אַ֤נְתְּ פָּֽלַֽח־לֵהּ֙ בִּתְדִירָ֔א הַיְכִ֥ל לְשֵׁיזָבוּתָ֖ךְ מִן־אַרְיָוָתָֽא
verses.translation: As he approached the den, he cried to Daniel in a mournful voice; the king said to Daniel, “Daniel, servant of the living God, was the God whom you served so regularly able to deliver you from the lions?”

verses.hebrew_full: אֱדַ֙יִן֙ דָּנִיֵּ֔אל עִם־מַלְכָּ֖א מַלִּ֑ל מַלְכָּ֖א לְעָלְמִ֥ין חֱיִֽי
verses.translation: Daniel then talked with the king, “O king, live forever!

verses.hebrew_full: אֱלָהִ֞י שְׁלַ֣ח מַלְאֲכֵ֗הּ וּֽסְגַ֛ר פֻּ֥ם אַרְיָוָתָ֖א וְלָ֣א חַבְּל֑וּנִי כׇּל־קֳבֵ֗ל דִּ֤י קׇֽדָמ֙וֹהִי֙ זָכוּ֙ הִשְׁתְּכַ֣חַת לִ֔י וְאַ֤ף (קדמיך) קׇֽדָמָךְ֙ מַלְכָּ֔א חֲבוּלָ֖ה לָ֥א עַבְדֵֽת
verses.translation: My God sent an angel, who shut the mouths of the lions so that they did not injure me, inasmuch as I was deemed to be innocent, nor have I, O king, done you any injury.”
verses.rashi: I have done no harm No unseemly or false act have I committed.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֣יִן מַלְכָּ֗א שַׂגִּיא֙ טְאֵ֣ב עֲל֔וֹהִי וּלְדָ֣נִיֵּ֔אל אֲמַ֖ר לְהַנְסָקָ֣ה מִן־גֻּבָּ֑א וְהֻסַּ֨ק דָּנִיֵּ֜אל מִן־גֻּבָּ֗א וְכׇל־חֲבָל֙ לָא־הִשְׁתְּכַ֣ח בֵּ֔הּ דִּ֖י הֵימִ֥ן בֵּאלָהֵֽהּ
verses.translation: The king was very glad, and ordered Daniel to be brought up out of the den. Daniel was brought up out of the den, and no injury was found on him, for he had trusted in his God.
verses.rashi: it pleased...exceedingly It was very good in his eyes. and...Daniel and he ordered that Daniel be lifted up out of the pit. because he believed in his God [as translated,] because he believed in his God.

verses.hebrew_full: וַאֲמַ֣ר מַלְכָּ֗א וְהַיְתִ֞יו גֻּבְרַיָּ֤א אִלֵּךְ֙ דִּֽי־אֲכַ֤לוּ קַרְצ֙וֹהִי֙ דִּ֣י דָֽנִיֵּ֔אל וּלְג֤וֹב אַרְיָוָתָא֙ רְמ֔וֹ אִנּ֖וּן בְּנֵיה֣וֹן וּנְשֵׁיה֑וֹן וְלָֽא־מְט֞וֹ לְאַרְעִ֣ית גֻּבָּ֗א עַ֠ד דִּֽי־שְׁלִ֤טֽוּ בְהוֹן֙ אַרְיָ֣וָתָ֔א וְכׇל־גַּרְמֵיה֖וֹן הַדִּֽקוּ
verses.translation: Then, by order of the king, those men who had slandered Daniel were brought and, together with their children and wives, were thrown into the lions’ den. They had hardly reached the bottom of the den when the lions overpowered them and crushed all their bones.
verses.rashi: who had informed on Daniel [as translated,] who had informed on Daniel. and they did not reach the bottom of the pit They did not reach the bottom of the pit before the lions overwhelmed them and crushed their bones to powder.

verses.hebrew_full: בֵּאדַ֜יִן דָּרְיָ֣וֶשׁ מַלְכָּ֗א כְּ֠תַ֠ב לְֽכׇל־עַֽמְמַיָּ֞א אֻמַּיָּ֧א וְלִשָּׁנַיָּ֛א דִּֽי־דָיְרִ֥ין (דארין) בְּכׇל־אַרְעָ֖א שְׁלָמְכ֥וֹן יִשְׂגֵּֽא
verses.translation: Then King Darius wrote to all peoples and nations of every language that inhabit the earth, “May your well-being abound!
verses.rashi: Then King Darius...that lived in all the land, May your peace increase! [as translated,] that lived in all the land, May your peace increase!

verses.hebrew_full: מִן־קֳדָמַי֮ שִׂ֣ים טְעֵם֒ דִּ֣י בְּכׇל־שׇׁלְטָ֣ן מַלְכוּתִ֗י לֶהֱוֺ֤ן (זאעין) זָיְעִין֙ וְדָ֣חֲלִ֔ין מִן־קֳדָ֖ם אֱלָהֵ֣הּ דִּי־דָֽנִיֵּ֑אל דִּי־ה֣וּא אֱלָהָ֣א חַיָּ֗א וְקַיָּם֙ לְעָ֣לְמִ֔ין וּמַלְכוּתֵהּ֙ דִּֽי־לָ֣א תִתְחַבַּ֔ל וְשׇׁלְטָנֵ֖הּ עַד־סוֹפָֽא
verses.translation: I have hereby given an order that throughout my royal domain everyone must tremble in fear before the God of Daniel, for this is the living God who endures forever; whose kingdom is indestructible, and whose dominion is to the end of time;

verses.hebrew_full: מְשֵׁיזִ֣ב וּמַצִּ֔ל וְעָבֵד֙ אָתִ֣ין וְתִמְהִ֔ין בִּשְׁמַיָּ֖א וּבְאַרְעָ֑א דִּ֚י שֵׁיזִ֣ב לְדָֽנִיֵּ֔אל מִן־יַ֖ד אַרְיָוָתָֽא
verses.translation: who delivers and saves, and performs signs and wonders in heaven and on earth, by having delivered Daniel from the power of the lions.”

verses.hebrew_full: וְדָנִיֵּ֣אל דְּנָ֔ה הַצְלַ֖ח בְּמַלְכ֣וּת דָּרְיָ֑וֶשׁ וּבְמַלְכ֖וּת כּ֥וֹרֶשׁ (פרסיא) פָּרְסָאָֽה
verses.translation: Thus Daniel prospered during the reign of Darius and during the reign of Cyrus the Persian.
verses.rashi: and in the kingdom of Cyrus who reigned after Darius, for Darius reigned only one year, and he was slain in battle, as it is written in the book of Joseph ben Gurion, and they crowned Cyrus his son- in-law in the midst of the battle.

verses.hebrew_full: בִּשְׁנַ֣ת חֲדָ֗ה לְבֵלְאשַׁצַּר֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ בָּבֶ֔ל דָּנִיֵּאל֙ חֵ֣לֶם חֲזָ֔ה וְחֶזְוֵ֥י רֵאשֵׁ֖הּ עַֽל־מִשְׁכְּבֵ֑הּ בֵּאדַ֙יִן֙ חֶלְמָ֣א כְתַ֔ב רֵ֥אשׁ מִלִּ֖ין אֲמַֽר
verses.translation: In the first year of King Belshazzar of Babylon, Daniel saw a dream and a vision of his mind in bed; afterward he wrote down the dream. Beginning the account,
verses.rashi: then he wrote the dream Then [he wrote the dream] and told the beginnings of the matters.

verses.hebrew_full: עָנֵ֤ה דָנִיֵּאל֙ וְאָמַ֔ר חָזֵ֥ה הֲוֵ֛ית בְּחֶזְוִ֖י עִם־לֵֽילְיָ֑א וַאֲר֗וּ אַרְבַּע֙ רוּחֵ֣י שְׁמַיָּ֔א מְגִיחָ֖ן לְיַמָּ֥א רַבָּֽא
verses.translation: Daniel related the following:“In my vision at night, I saw the four winds of heaven stirring up the great sea.
verses.rashi: and behold [as translated,] and behold. the four winds of the heavens were stirring up, etc. four blowing winds coming from the four directions of the world and waging war with the Great Sea and stirring it up.

verses.hebrew_full: וְאַרְבַּ֤ע חֵיוָן֙ רַבְרְבָ֔ן סָלְקָ֖ן מִן־יַמָּ֑א שָׁנְיָ֖ן דָּ֥א מִן־דָּֽא
verses.translation: Four mighty beasts different from each other emerged from the sea.
verses.rashi: And four beasts [as translated,] and four beasts. each one different from the other [as translated,] each one different from the other.

verses.hebrew_full: קַדְמָיְתָ֣א כְאַרְיֵ֔ה וְגַפִּ֥ין דִּֽי־נְשַׁ֖ר לַ֑הּ חָזֵ֣ה הֲוֵ֡ית עַד֩ דִּי־מְּרִ֨יטוּ (גפיה) גַפַּ֜הּ וּנְטִ֣ילַת מִן־אַרְעָ֗א וְעַל־רַגְלַ֙יִן֙ כֶּאֱנָ֣שׁ הֳקִימַ֔ת וּלְבַ֥ב אֱנָ֖שׁ יְהִ֥יב לַֽהּ
verses.translation: The first was like a lion but had eagles’ wings. As I looked on, its wings were plucked off, and it was lifted off the ground and set on its feet like a human and given a human mind.
verses.rashi: The first one was like a lion, and it had the wings of an eagle It was like a lion, and it had the wings of an eagle; that is the kingdom of Babylon, which was ruling at that time, and so did Jeremiah see it (4:7): “A lion has come up from its thicket,” and he says also (48:40): “like an eagle he shall soar.” until its wings were plucked Its wings were plucked, which is an allusion to its downfall and it was taken from the earth an expression of being removed from the earth, an allusion to the curtailment of the kingdom from the world. and the heart of a mortal Aram. וּלְבַב אֶנָשׁ, an expression of weakness, like (Psalms 9:21): “Let the nations know that they are forever mortal men (אֶנוֹשׁ)”.

verses.hebrew_full: וַאֲר֣וּ חֵיוָה֩ אׇחֳרִ֨י תִנְיָנָ֜ה דָּמְיָ֣ה לְדֹ֗ב וְלִשְׂטַר־חַד֙ הֳקִמַ֔ת וּתְלָ֥ת עִלְעִ֛ין בְּפֻמַּ֖הּ בֵּ֣ין (שניה) שִׁנַּ֑הּ וְכֵן֙ אָמְרִ֣ין לַ֔הּ ק֥וּמִֽי אֲכֻ֖לִי בְּשַׂ֥ר שַׂגִּֽיא
verses.translation: Then I saw a second, different beast, which was like a bear but raised on one side, and with three fangs in its mouth among its teeth; it was told, ‘Arise, eat much meat!’
verses.rashi: another [as translated,] another. second that emerged second from the sea. resembling a bear This represents the kingdom of Persia, which will reign after Babylon, who eats and drinks like a bear and is enwrapped in flesh like a bear. resembling a bear It is spelled לְדֹב [without a “vav,”] like דִיבָא, the Aramaic for זְאֵב, a wolf, for the kingdom of Persia was also called a wolf, as it is said: (Jer. 5: 6): “Therefore a lion smote them, a wolf of the deserts spoils them.” and it stood to one side and it stood to one side, indicating that when the kingdom of Babylon terminates, Persia will wait one year, when Media will reign. and there were three ribs in its mouth Aram. וּתְלָת עִלָעִין בְּפֻמַּהּ, three ribs. Our Sages explained that three provinces were constantly rebelling against it [i.e., Persia] and making peace with it; sometimes it would swallow them and sometimes spit them out. That is the meaning of “in its mouth between its teeth,” sometimes outside its teeth, sometimes inside (Kid. 72a), but I say that the three עִלָעִין are three kings who will rise from Persia: Cyrus, Ahasuerus, and Darius who built the Temple. much meat [as translated,] much meat.

verses.hebrew_full: בָּאתַ֨ר דְּנָ֜ה חָזֵ֣ה הֲוֵ֗ית וַאֲר֤וּ אׇֽחֳרִי֙ כִּנְמַ֔ר וְלַ֨הּ גַּפִּ֥ין אַרְבַּ֛ע דִּי־ע֖וֹף עַל־גַּבַּ֑הּ (גביה) וְאַרְבְּעָ֤ה רֵאשִׁין֙ לְחֵ֣יוְתָ֔א וְשׇׁלְטָ֖ן יְהִ֥יב לַֽהּ
verses.translation: After that, as I looked on, there was another one, like a leopard, and it had on its back four wings like those of a bird; the beast had four heads, and dominion was given to it.
verses.rashi: four wings...four heads They are the four rulers to whom Alexander of Macedon allotted his kingdom at his death, as is written in the book of Joseph ben Gurion (Book 3, ch. 14), for this third beast is the kingdom of Antiochus, and it is called נָמֵר because it issued decrees upon Israel [which were] spotted (מְנֻמָּרוֹת) and varied one from the other. and dominion was given it [as translated,] and dominion was given it.

verses.hebrew_full: בָּאתַ֣ר דְּנָה֩ חָזֵ֨ה הֲוֵ֜ית בְּחֶזְוֵ֣י לֵֽילְיָ֗א וַאֲר֣וּ חֵיוָ֣ה (רביעיה) רְֽבִיעָאָ֡ה דְּחִילָה֩ וְאֵֽימְתָנִ֨י וְתַקִּיפָ֜א יַתִּ֗ירָה וְשִׁנַּ֨יִן דִּֽי־פַרְזֶ֥ל לַהּ֙ רַבְרְבָ֔ן אָֽכְלָ֣ה וּמַדֱּקָ֔ה וּשְׁאָרָ֖א (ברגליה) בְּרַגְלַ֣הּ רָפְסָ֑ה וְהִ֣יא מְשַׁנְּיָ֗ה מִן־כׇּל־חֵֽיוָתָא֙ דִּ֣י (קדמיה) קׇֽדָמַ֔הּ וְקַרְנַ֥יִן עֲשַׂ֖ר לַֽהּ
verses.translation: After that, as I looked on in the night vision, there was a fourth beast—fearsome, dreadful, and very powerful, with great iron teeth—that devoured and crushed, and stamped the remains with its feet. It was different from all the other beasts that had gone before it; and it had ten horns.
verses.rashi: in the visions of the night on another night. The first three he saw on one night, and this one on another night, because it is equal to them all. In Leviticus Rabbah (13:5). and it had...iron teeth [as translated,] iron teeth. and crushed It crushed and ground finely. and...the rest what it left over from its eating. and...ten horns Aram. וְקַרְנַיִן עֲשַׂר. The angel explained to him that these are the ten kings who would ascend [the throne] of Rome before Vespasian, who would destroy the Temple.

verses.hebrew_full: מִשְׂתַּכַּ֨ל הֲוֵ֜ית בְּקַרְנַיָּ֗א וַ֠אֲל֠וּ קֶ֣רֶן אׇחֳרִ֤י זְעֵירָה֙ סִלְקָ֣ת (ביניהון) בֵּֽינֵיהֵ֔ן וּתְלָ֗ת מִן־קַרְנַיָּא֙ קַדְמָ֣יָתָ֔א (אתעקרו) אֶתְעֲקַ֖רָה מִן־קֳדָמַ֑הּ (קדמיה) וַאֲל֨וּ עַיְנִ֜ין כְּעַיְנֵ֤י אֲנָשָׁא֙ בְּקַרְנָא־דָ֔א וּפֻ֖ם מְמַלִּ֥ל רַבְרְבָֽן
verses.translation: While I was gazing upon these horns, a new little horn sprouted up among them; three of the older horns were uprooted to make room for it. There were eyes in this horn like those of a human, and a mouth that spoke arrogantly.
verses.rashi: and behold Aram. וַאֲלוּ, like וַאֲרוּ, an expression of “behold.” speaking arrogantly words of arrogance. That is Titus, about whom the Rabbis, of blessed memory, said (Gittin 56b) that he blasphemed and berated and entered the Heichal with brazenness.

verses.hebrew_full: חָזֵ֣ה הֲוֵ֗ית עַ֣ד דִּ֤י כׇרְסָוָן֙ רְמִ֔יו וְעַתִּ֥יק יוֹמִ֖ין יְתִ֑ב לְבוּשֵׁ֣הּ כִּתְלַ֣ג חִוָּ֗ר וּשְׂעַ֤ר רֵאשֵׁהּ֙ כַּעֲמַ֣ר נְקֵ֔א כׇּרְסְיֵהּ֙ שְׁבִבִ֣ין דִּי־נ֔וּר גַּלְגִּלּ֖וֹהִי נ֥וּר דָּלִֽק
verses.translation: As I looked on,Thrones were set in place,And the Ancient of Days took a seat—Wearing a garment like white snow,And with hair like lamb’s wool.This throne was tongues of flame;Its wheels were blazing fire;
verses.rashi: until thrones were set up Thrones were set up and established to sit in judgment, one for judgment for the nations of the world and one for charity for Israel. and the Ancient of Days sat The Holy One, blessed be He, was sitting in judgment on this kingdom and on those before it, who provoked Him and oppressed His children. as white as snow to whiten the iniquities of His people. and the hair of His head was like clean wool He cleanses Himself of the merits that the nations have before Him, and He pays them all their reward in this world. In Lev. Rabbah.

words.eng: N’har Dinur [Fire-River] a fictitious river (v. Dan. VII

verses.hebrew_full: נְהַ֣ר דִּי־נ֗וּר נָגֵ֤ד וְנָפֵק֙ מִן־קֳדָמ֔וֹהִי אֶ֤לֶף (אלפים) אַלְפִין֙ יְשַׁמְּשׁוּנֵּ֔הּ וְרִבּ֥וֹ (רבון) רִבְבָ֖ן קָֽדָמ֣וֹהִי יְקוּמ֑וּן דִּינָ֥א יְתִ֖ב וְסִפְרִ֥ין פְּתִֽיחוּ
verses.translation: A river of fire streamed forth from it.Thousands upon thousands rendered service;Myriads upon myriads stood in attendance;The court sat and the books were opened.
verses.rashi: of fire [as translated,] of fire. a burning fire [as translated,] a burning fire. was flowing and emerging [as translated,] was flowing and emerging. serve Him i.e., the Ancient of Days. justice was established The judgment is established before Him. and the books were opened The books were opened; the book of the transgressions and the evils that they committed.

words.eng: N’har Dinur [Fire-River] a fictitious river (v. Dan. VII

verses.hebrew_full: חָזֵ֣ה הֲוֵ֔ית בֵּאדַ֗יִן מִן־קָל֙ מִלַּיָּ֣א רַבְרְבָתָ֔א דִּ֥י קַרְנָ֖א מְמַלֱּלָ֑ה חָזֵ֣ה הֲוֵ֡ית עַד֩ דִּ֨י קְטִילַ֤ת חֵֽיוְתָא֙ וְהוּבַ֣ד גִּשְׁמַ֔הּ וִיהִיבַ֖ת לִיקֵדַ֥ת אֶשָּֽׁא
verses.translation: I looked on. Then, because of the arrogant words that the horn spoke, the beast was killed as I looked on; its body was destroyed and it was consigned to the flames.
verses.rashi: from the sound of the arrogant words From the sound of the haughty words that the horn was speaking, the wrath of the Ancient of Days was aroused. until the beast was slain and its body was destroyed and given [as translated,] and its body was destroyed. to a flame of fire to a flame of fire; this refers to Jacob’s fire etc.

verses.hebrew_full: וּשְׁאָר֙ חֵֽיוָתָ֔א הֶעְדִּ֖יו שׇׁלְטָנְה֑וֹן וְאַרְכָ֧ה בְחַיִּ֛ין יְהִ֥יבַת לְה֖וֹן עַד־זְמַ֥ן וְעִדָּֽן
verses.translation: The dominion of the other beasts was taken away, but an extension of life was given to them for a time and season.
verses.rashi: But as for the other beasts, their dominion was removed And from the other kingdoms, their dominion was removed by Heaven. and they were given an extension of life And He gave them time to live until a set day in the future, the wars of Gog and Magog. an extension Aram. אַרְכָה, waiting.

verses.hebrew_full: חָזֵ֤ה הֲוֵית֙ בְּחֶזְוֵ֣י לֵֽילְיָ֔א וַאֲרוּ֙ עִם־עֲנָנֵ֣י שְׁמַיָּ֔א כְּבַ֥ר אֱנָ֖שׁ אָתֵ֣ה הֲוָ֑א וְעַד־עַתִּ֤יק יֽוֹמַיָּא֙ מְטָ֔ה וּקְדָמ֖וֹהִי הַקְרְבֽוּהִי
verses.translation: As I looked on, in the night vision,One like a human beingCame with the clouds of heaven;He reached the Ancient of DaysAnd was given an audience.
verses.rashi: one like a man was coming That is the King Messiah. and...up to the Ancient of Days Who was sitting in judgment and judging the nations. came arrived, reached.

verses.hebrew_full: וְלֵ֨הּ יְהִ֤ב שׇׁלְטָן֙ וִיקָ֣ר וּמַלְכ֔וּ וְכֹ֣ל עַֽמְמַיָּ֗א אֻמַּיָּ֛א וְלִשָּׁנַיָּ֖א לֵ֣הּ יִפְלְח֑וּן שׇׁלְטָנֵ֞הּ שׇׁלְטָ֤ן עָלַם֙ דִּֽי־לָ֣א יֶעְדֵּ֔ה וּמַלְכוּתֵ֖הּ דִּי־לָ֥א תִתְחַבַּֽל
verses.translation: Dominion, glory, and kingship were given to him;All peoples and nations of every language must serve him.His dominion is an everlasting dominion that shall not pass away,And his kingship, one that shall not be destroyed.
verses.rashi: And He gave him dominion And to that man He gave dominion over the nations, for the heathens he likens to beasts, and Israel he likens to a man because they are humble and innocent. which will not be removed [as translated,] will not be removed.

verses.hebrew_full: אֶתְכְּרִיַּ֥ת רוּחִ֛י אֲנָ֥ה דָנִיֵּ֖אל בְּג֣וֹא נִדְנֶ֑ה וְחֶזְוֵ֥י רֵאשִׁ֖י יְבַהֲלֻנַּֽנִי
verses.translation: As for me, Daniel, my spirit was disturbed within me and the vision of my mind alarmed me.
verses.rashi: My spirit... became troubled Aram. אֶתְכְּרִיַת an expression of (Exod. 21:33): “or if...digs (יִכְרֶה),” and Hebrew and Aramaic are alike in this case; i.e., it was humbled because of the depth of its digging. within its sheath Aram. בְּגוֹ נִדְנֶה ; i.e., my spirit became terrified in my body from this dream.

verses.hebrew_full: קִרְבֵ֗ת עַל־חַד֙ מִן־קָ֣אֲמַיָּ֔א וְיַצִּיבָ֥א אֶבְעֵֽא־מִנֵּ֖הּ עַֽל־כׇּל־דְּנָ֑ה וַאֲמַר־לִ֕י וּפְשַׁ֥ר מִלַּיָּ֖א יְהוֹדְעִנַּֽנִי
verses.translation: I approached one of the attendants and asked him the true meaning of all this. He gave me this interpretation of the matter:
verses.rashi: I drew near to one of those standing I drew near to one of those standing there beside the Ancient of Days. The angels are called standing ones, as it is said: (Zech. 3:7): “among these who stand by (הָעמְדִים),” because they have no joints. and I asked him the truth [as translated,] and I asked him the truth.

words.eng: to say·inflected pers. pron. meaning ‘to me’.

verses.hebrew_full: אִלֵּין֙ חֵיוָתָ֣א רַבְרְבָתָ֔א דִּ֥י אִנִּ֖ין אַרְבַּ֑ע אַרְבְּעָ֥ה מַלְכִ֖ין יְקוּמ֥וּן מִן־אַרְעָֽא
verses.translation: ‘These great beasts, four in number [mean] four kingdoms will arise out of the earth;
verses.rashi: four kingdoms Aram. אַרְבְּעָא מַלְכִין, lit. four kings.

verses.hebrew_full: וִֽיקַבְּלוּן֙ מַלְכוּתָ֔א קַדִּישֵׁ֖י עֶלְיוֹנִ֑ין וְיַחְסְנ֤וּן מַלְכוּתָא֙ עַד־עָ֣לְמָ֔א וְעַ֖ד עָלַ֥ם עָלְמַיָּֽא
verses.translation: then holy ones of the Most High will receive the kingdom, and will possess the kingdom forever—forever and ever.’
verses.rashi: And the high holy ones will receive the kingdom These are Israel, who will take the kingdom from the fourth one.

verses.hebrew_full: אֱדַ֗יִן צְבִית֙ לְיַצָּבָ֗א עַל־חֵֽיוְתָא֙ רְבִיעָ֣יְתָ֔א דִּֽי־הֲוָ֥ת שָֽׁנְיָ֖ה מִן־כׇּלְּהֵ֑ין (כלהון) דְּחִילָ֣ה יַתִּ֗ירָה (שניה) שִׁנַּ֤הּ דִּֽי־פַרְזֶל֙ (וטפריה) וְטִפְרַ֣הּ דִּֽי־נְחָ֔שׁ אָֽכְלָ֣ה מַדְּקָ֔ה וּשְׁאָרָ֖א (ברגליה) בְּרַגְלַ֥הּ רָֽפְסָֽה
verses.translation: Then I wanted to ascertain the true meaning of the fourth beast, which was different from them all, very fearsome, with teeth of iron, claws of bronze, that devoured and crushed, and stamped the remains;
verses.rashi: Then I wished to determine the truth Then I wished to determine the truth concerning the fourth beast. which was different from all of them—excessively dreadful which was different from all of them with excessive dreadfulness.

verses.hebrew_full: וְעַל־קַרְנַיָּ֤א עֲשַׂר֙ דִּ֣י בְרֵאשַׁ֔הּ וְאׇחֳרִי֙ דִּ֣י סִלְקַ֔ת (ונפלו) וּנְפַ֥לָה מִן־קֳדָמַ֖הּ (קדמיה) תְּלָ֑ת וְקַרְנָ֨א דִכֵּ֜ן וְעַיְנִ֣ין לַ֗הּ וּפֻם֙ מְמַלִּ֣ל רַבְרְבָ֔ן וְחֶזְוַ֖הּ רַ֥ב מִן־חַבְרָתַֽהּ
verses.translation: and of the ten horns on its head; and of the new one that sprouted, to make room for which three fell—the horn that had eyes, and a mouth that spoke arrogantly, and which was more conspicuous than its fellows.
verses.rashi: and [the] three [that] fell before it and three of the first ones fell before it. and the horn that was like this and that had eyes and the horn that was like this, and had eyes, as is written above.

verses.hebrew_full: חָזֵ֣ה הֲוֵ֔ית וְקַרְנָ֣א דִכֵּ֔ן עָבְדָ֥ה קְרָ֖ב עִם־קַדִּישִׁ֑ין וְיָכְלָ֖ה לְהֹֽן
verses.translation: (I looked on as that horn made war with the holy ones and overcame them,
verses.rashi: I looked in my first dream, which I told above. with the holy ones with Israel.

verses.hebrew_full: עַ֣ד דִּֽי־אֲתָ֗ה עַתִּיק֙ יֽוֹמַיָּ֔א וְדִינָ֣א יְהִ֔ב לְקַדִּישֵׁ֖י עֶלְיוֹנִ֑ין וְזִמְנָ֣א מְטָ֔ה וּמַלְכוּתָ֖א הֶחֱסִ֥נוּ קַדִּישִֽׁין
verses.translation: until the Ancient of Days came and judgment was rendered in favor of the holy ones of the Most High, for the time had come, and the holy ones took possession of the kingdom.)
verses.rashi: and gave revenge to the high holy ones and He gave them revenge from it.

verses.hebrew_full: כֵּן֮ אֲמַר֒ חֵֽיוְתָא֙ רְבִיעָ֣יְתָ֔א מַלְכ֤וּ (רביעיה) רְבִיעָאָה֙ תֶּהֱוֵ֣א בְאַרְעָ֔א דִּ֥י תִשְׁנֵ֖א מִן־כׇּל־מַלְכְוָתָ֑א וְתֵאכֻל֙ כׇּל־אַרְעָ֔א וּתְדוּשִׁנַּ֖הּ וְתַדְּקִנַּֽהּ
verses.translation: This is what he said: ‘The fourth beast [means]—there will be a fourth kingdom upon the earth that will be different from all the kingdoms; it will devour the whole earth, tread it down, and crush it.
verses.rashi: So he said So did the angel who interpreted the dream for me reply to me.

words.eng: to be or become king or queen

verses.hebrew_full: וְקַרְנַיָּ֣א עֲשַׂ֔ר מִנַּהּ֙ מַלְכוּתָ֔א עַשְׂרָ֥ה מַלְכִ֖ין יְקֻמ֑וּן וְאׇחֳרָ֞ן יְק֣וּם אַחֲרֵיהֹ֗ן וְה֤וּא יִשְׁנֵא֙ מִן־קַדְמָיֵ֔א וּתְלָתָ֥ה מַלְכִ֖ין יְהַשְׁפִּֽל
verses.translation: And the ten horns [mean]—from that kingdom, ten kings will arise, and after them another will arise. He will be different from the former ones, and will bring low three kings.
verses.rashi: And the ten horns And its ten horns that you saw—this is its interpretation. from that kingdom [as translated,] from that kingdom. and the last one will rise after them and the last one will rise after them—that is Titus.

verses.hebrew_full: וּמִלִּ֗ין לְצַ֤ד (עליא) עִלָּאָה֙ יְמַלִּ֔ל וּלְקַדִּישֵׁ֥י עֶלְיוֹנִ֖ין יְבַלֵּ֑א וְיִסְבַּ֗ר לְהַשְׁנָיָה֙ זִמְנִ֣ין וְדָ֔ת וְיִתְיַהֲב֣וּן בִּידֵ֔הּ עַד־עִדָּ֥ן וְעִדָּנִ֖ין וּפְלַ֥ג עִדָּֽן
verses.translation: He will speak words against the Most High, and will harass the holy ones of the Most High. He will think of changing times and laws, and they will be delivered into his power for a time, times, and half a time.
verses.rashi: against the Most High [as translated,] against the Most High. and he will oppress the high holy ones He will burden and oppress Israel. and he will think to change the times and the law He will plan in his heart to cause them to transgress all their appointed times and their laws. until a time, two times, and half a time This is an obscure end, as was said to Daniel (12:4): “And you, Daniel, close up the words and seal,” and the early commentators expounded on it, each one according to his view, and the ends have passed. We can still interpret it as I saw written in the name of Rav Saadia Gaon, that they are the 1,335 years stated at the end of the Book (12:12): “Fortunate is he who waits [and reaches the days one thousand three hundred and thirty-five],” and he explains the appointed time as until the time of two times and a half time, and he [Rav Saadia Gaon] said that the times are 480 [years], which is the time from the Exodus from Egypt until the Temple was built, and 410 [years], [which are] the days of the First Temple, totaling 890, and another half of this time, 445, totaling 1,335. Figure these from the time the daily sacrifice was discontinued until the daily sacrifice will be restored to its place; it was discontinued six years prior to the destruction, and there is somewhat of a proof in this Book. [See Rashi to 8:14.] Others bring further proof to this computation, namely that (Deut. 31:18): “And I, will hide My face” [the words] הַסְתֵּר אַסְתִּיר add up in gematria to 1,335.

verses.hebrew_full: וְדִינָ֖א יִתִּ֑ב וְשׇׁלְטָנֵ֣הּ יְהַעְדּ֔וֹן לְהַשְׁמָדָ֥ה וּלְהוֹבָדָ֖ה עַד־סוֹפָֽא
verses.translation: Then the court will sit and his dominion will be taken away, to be destroyed and abolished for all time.
verses.rashi: And the judgment was established And the judgment was established before Him. and they will remove his dominion from heaven. They will remove the dominion and the rule of that kingdom.
